BODY: For a number of years while living in Florida, we used TurboTax to prepare our Federal Income Tax returns. Florida does not have a personal income tax requirement so we purchased the Federal only Deluxe version from Amazon each year, and E-Filed the return. In 2023 we moved to Colorado which does have a personal income tax in place. And as a result, I purchased the TurboTax 2023 Deluxe Federal & State version for $55.99, excluding Colorado sales tax and fees. With the exception of an issue with the Fed E-File PIN that was resolved with just some relatively minor aggravation, completing the 2023 Fed return and the Colorado returns was easy enough. Then came the gotcha. ONLY in extremely small print in the product listing on the Amazon website is there an indication that there would be an additional fee to E-File a state return. Furthermore, on the back of the Turbo Tax DVD sleeve, again in very small print, there is mention of an extra fee to E-File a state return, but there is no indication of the actual amount of the fee by either Amazon or Intuit. I admit that I missed both of these “notices”, but it does seem that that was the outcome expected by Intuit/TurboTax. In any event the cost for state E-File was never revealed upfront. It turns out that the actual fee to E-File a state return is equal to almost a 72% premium over and above the amazon purchase price for the Fed & State Deluxe version, i.e., $40.00!!!!!!!!! That amount would also be subject to Colorado state sales tax and fees, amounting to $3.48. So, had I fallen for what seems to me to be a real bait and switch scam, I would have ended up paying a total of $104.23 to E-File our 2023 Federal and state income tax returns. That is just simply outrageous. There is no legitimate reason why Intuit/TurboTax needs to hide the truth about the actual cost to send out a state return via E-File. You are left in the dark about this significant extra expense until ONLY after completing both returns, and by that time you are locked in. Our 2023 Colorado return was filed using the USPS and $1.95 worth of stamps. Intuit/TurboTax is the 800-pound gorilla is this presumptuous and predatory “service” industry. We are getting serviced alright, just without the smile that usually follows. I’ll be using H&R Block software next year, for sure.
TITLE: Filing a state tax return - beware of the Gotcha !!!!
VARIATION: Platform For Display: PC/Mac Disc Edition: Deluxe + State

Also, where the battery sits in the car is behind a latch and a screw, which has to be unscrewed and rescrewed every time the battery is involved. These issues make this car less enjoyable after playtime. 2: setup. The manual that comes with this rc isn’t that helpful. I had to figure out setup on my own (with the help of a tutorial on YouTube). The only help the manual gave was with the controls. Overall, I’d give this rc car 3.5 stars, decent but nothing amazing.

BODY: ... So my timbs here have been through a lot. Ended up using a black stain. Long story short I was against painting them but screw it. The color is nice. Here is the reason for the 4 stars. #1 this paint lacks a good primer. As someone that's paints regularly I should either do a primer or the paint should be decent with bonding. This was tested on a surface that wasn't shoes. So it does contain some kind of primer. But I do feel like it's going to be the downfall of this product hence why I'm going to wait to give it a full either five stars or I'm going to give it less. It's also very low in quantity because if you care about streaks while painting you will have to thin this product and apply. The way I did it was I applied good liberal amount to coats sanded it with a 220 and then I hit it with a watered down version. But being brushed on and being a thick paint it's going to leave streaks. So that should be noted on there when painting yes if you're spraying you won't get streaks. I would prefer spray paint but because these timberlands have the plaid on top I was against it. I'm hoping the flexibility of this paint is good. Most of any acrylic paint is but this is a boot. Lastly I did add one more thing to this paint. I did spray a clear satin sheen on top of it. And this is because after sanding it I still couldn't get the streaks the way I wanted it so I had to match the sheen of the paint which is more of a eggshell. Nevertheless they came out really nice. I'm going to wait until tomorrow and then I'm going to shoot a leather protector on top of this so we'll see what happens wish me luck
TITLE: I will update this

BODY: I have been searching for years for an electric teakettle with no plastic inside, and finally I have found one! When I first got it, it had a plasticky, chemical smell. It had some sort of coating. After about 10 attempts at merely boiling water, then dumping it out didn’t remove the smell, I researched how to get rid of it. It turns out the best method is to cut up some lemons and/or limes and boil them in clean water in the teakettle, then let that sit (I let it sit over night), dump it out, then boil fresh water again. Finally, that worked. The smell was still barely perceptible to me, but I am a super smeller. No one in my family noticed it, and I couldn’t smell it once it was in the cup, not in the kettle anymore. After about a day of two of use, I could no longer smell it at all. I have had the kettle for about 2 months, and it still works well. It holds enough water for 4 mugs of tea, coffee, etc. The exterior of the kettle stays cool, such that my kids can use it without me having to worry. The way the auto-shutoff works is by a magnet that stops being magnetized when the boiling temperature has been reached. Apparently, that is how most analog kettles work. My autodidactic homeschooled son explained that to me. While I find that pretty cool, the real appeal is the lack of computers that will stop working before the actual mechanics of the appliance do. Reliably analog! I did drop it last week, when I was trying to take the lid off while I was holding the kettle. It still works, but the seam is slightly wonky now. It is not really noticeable. Top marks for taking a lickin’ and keeping on tickin’! The only cons are the initial smell, the fact that you can’t set the temperature or even know it, and the appearance is slightly tacky. I say slightly, because the handle is silver colored plastic, and that plastic jewel on the top is a little princessy for me. I hate the logo in silver. That is so tacky. However, I can abide by the pan-European-as-interpreted-by-the-Chinese aesthetic in exchange for WATER NEVER TOUCHING PLASTIC!!! You did it, guys!!! Wooo hooooo!!!! This company makes a more English-looking kettle with a thermometer, but apparently there is a little bit of plastic in that one, where the thermometer penetrates the kettle. Now, all my actual meat thermometers are metal probes, so I can’t quite figure that one out. The cream color is fine — pretty enough. Overall, it is attractive enough — except for that stupid dorky logo , but it isn’t a deal breaker. In conclusion, I recommend this tea kettle, but be prepared to do a little work to get the plasticky smell out, at first.
TITLE: NO PLASTIC TOUCHING WATER!!!

BODY: I purchased the Hiearcool Waterproof Phone Pouch with high hopes for keeping my phone safe during beach trips. The pouch fits my iPhone 15 well, and I like the idea of having it handy for water activities. However, I ended up returning it due to one main issue: the pouch doesn’t work well at depths beyond shallow water. While it’s advertised as IPX8 waterproof, meaning it should be safe up to 30 meters, I found that it didn’t perform as expected when submerged at deeper depths. If you're planning to use it just for shallow splashes or surface-level activities, it could be fine, but for any significant underwater use, I wouldn’t recommend it. Overall, I think it’s a decent option for light, surface-level use, but if you need something for deeper water activities, you may want to look into a more robust solution.
TITLE: Decent for Shallow Water, But Doesn’t Hold Up Under Deeper Submersion

BODY: I enjoy all kinds of protein bars and while this one was on the lower end of the protein content spectrum (10 grams per bar), I thought the flavors sounded interesting and unique compared to other protein bars. You get three flavors in this variety pack - Peanut Butter and Jelly, Churro, and Mexican Hot Chocolate. The product packaging is perfect - eye catching, vibrant, and premium looking. The texture is VERY sticky, chewy and taffy like. It is overly firm to bite into and then just sticks to everything in your mouth. If you have eaten a somewhat old piece of Laffy Taffy, that is very similar to the texture of these bars. The Peanut Butter and Jelly had a good flavor. I could taste both the peanut butter (prominent flavor) and the strawberry "jelly". I would have preferred a grape "jelly" over a strawberry but this was still pretty good. The Churro was my favorite. It contains the perfect amount of cinnamon and brown sugar, making it sweet but not sickening sweet. The Mexican Hot Chocolate was good but definitely NOT chocolatey. I was expecting a lot more chocolate flavor but I tasted more cinnamon and spice. The bars are even tricky to remove from the wrappers, I had to open them very carefully to avoid the wrapper sticking to the bar itself and ripping. If you are on a gluten free or dairy free diet, you may really enjoy these. We like them but I'm not sure if I would choose these over other options.
TITLE: Super sticky and chewy, pretty good for a filling snack

I was looking for an ice machine because I had switched over to a soft water system in my house and I came across this one. It didn’t seem to matter which ice machine I reviewed they all had mixed reviews and this one had reviews claiming it was too loud, which had me concerned, but I gave it a shot anyway. I will say this ice machine is great. It makes ice well. The ice stays nice and cold and my drink. The machine is not loud the only time it is loud as if it needs water. You will have to use distilled water with this unit or you risk the internal pieces getting clogged up, which is another reason it causes loud noises. The only reason I gave this a four instead of a five is because of the side container if you overfill it, there is no fail safe it will leak and I had a leak that carry through to my basement because it wasn’t smart enough to stop putting water in the well, even though the bin was full. Now I just fill the bottom with water and I don’t use the side container. Overall, very happy with this ice machine and don’t seem to be having the problems that others had. I was able to set it up with no issues no loud noises the loudest it is is may be a small hum and I’ve had it now for a few months!
TITLE: Great ice machine!

Beautiful Color, Great Value I recently tried the Wet n Wild Wild Shine Nail Polish in Red Burgundy Frost, and I’m impressed! The color is a stunning deep red with a frosty finish, perfect for fall and winter. It gives off a sophisticated, almost vampy vibe without being too dark, making it wearable for all occasions. The formula goes on smoothly and doesn’t streak, which is a huge plus. I did need two coats for full coverage, but it dried fairly quickly between layers. For the price, this polish is a great deal. It’s affordable but doesn’t compromise on quality. My only small gripe is that it doesn't seem as long-lasting as some other more expensive brands. After about 4-5 days, I noticed a bit of chipping, but that’s not a dealbreaker for me, considering the price. He's learning out to walk and uses our coffee to cruise, but sometimes his feet don't do what he wants and he takes a tumble. Last week, the table gave him a good one-two hit, where he hit the top tier of the table, rolled, and hit the bottom tier. These bumpers definitely saved us from some likely stitches as that table has some very sharp edges. Installation was kind of a pain in the butt, had to roll the sticky tape onto the bumpers, then peel, then stick to the table. It wasn't hard, but it was tedious. They did include a note that stated this is deliberate, because pre-installed glue on the bumpers have come unstuck. The note also says they are working on a fix to this, so hopefully next time it'll be pre installed. The instructions say to unroll the bumper and leave it unrolled for 24 hours...which I completely ignored. Why? Because I'm stubborn. And a mom to a one year old. And don't have the space to unroll it and keep it straight. In the end, it turned out okay. I did notice I did have to cut more after the initial cut, as the pieces ended up being too long, but that's definitely preferred over being to short and having a gap on the border. I had plenty of sticky tape left over, which was very much appreciated as I used it on another project. I did have to gently lift up and reapply one area that kept raising up, and since then it's hung on perfectly! I like that this creates a raised edge on the coffee table so my son can roll his toys around and they don't fall off the other side immediately. I've also noticed that he will occasionally use his teeth on the bumper, and while I am worried about him taking a chunk out, it hasn't happened yet! So far very happy with this purchase.
TITLE: Perfect for baby learning to walk
VARIATION: Size: Small 18 feet Color: Onyx Black

BODY: These fly strips do a great job of catching the phorid flies that have invaded my house. I have seen a significant reduction in the number of flies since I put these up. I placed one in the kitchen, another one in the main bath, two in the bedroom, one in my office, and one in the entryway. Each one has caught many of the flies, with the ones in the kitchen and bathroom full of them. Once they fly into the strip, they cannot get loose, no matter how they try. A minor nuisance is that the fly strips stick to anything they touch so be careful not to let them touch furniture, if you can. If some of the sticky adhesive comes off on your hands or the wall or a piece of furniture, it is fairly easy to get it off with soap and water on a damp washcloth. Their website suggests using an oil-rubbed cloth (baby oil, vegetable oil, etc.). More of a nuisance and waste is getting the strips to come out of the container without the container itself coming off. More often than not, it's not a problem. You need to be aware to pull out only as much as necessary (about 20 to 22 inches unwound). If you keep pulling, the container *will* come off and you've lost the weight to hold the strip open. I bought the 24-pack and lost five or six to these types of malfunctions. Twice, I hadn't even pulled the strip out seven or eight inches before the container came off. Once, the ribbon that's used for pulling came off of the strip before I had even pulled two inches of the strip out. I've heard that by squeezing the container as you pull and twist the strip out will help. I'll have to try that on my next batch. With all of that said, I will likely try these again and try to find a way to lessen the waste because these do a great job of catching the phorid flies and reducing their numbers.
TITLE: It Works For Me, But...

This shows that this mix has more dynamics than the CD & your not beaten over the head by having all the sounds being loud. The records themselves were pretty quiet & the mastering is done well. I'd give this 5 stars if someone had the sense to include a CD or lossless download of THIS mix along with the records so I wouldn't have to needledrop it myself, but I'll end up with a better sounding CD than the official one. BODY: We have had issues feeding our baby from the beginning when she was born nearly 11 lbs and momma was not making enough breastmilk for such a hungry chonker baby. We ended up needing to mix breastfeeding with formula supplementation in order to keep up with baby’s growth and we quickly realized the couple basic bottles we had weren’t going to be enough. Cue frantic product research on what kinds of bottles were best for using alongside breastfeeding and we ended up going on a months long journey trying out several different brands. After our journey I would really encourage people to be very skeptical of bottle company claims of "breast like", "perfect latch", "natural feel" nipple shapes as they don't really need to back that up with any real science. All these poorly shaped bottles plus other factors means our baby has nipple confusion and now hates to be breastfed even though momma is now making plenty of milk to feed baby and it has been a struggle every feeding. After eventually seeing a lactation consultant and having enough time to read some of the lactation books out there we decided to try these bottles since they had a nipple that looked ideal. The wide taper and really soft silicone requires the baby to manipulate the nipple in almost the same way as a breast in order to suck. Baby initially got frustrated with these nipples in exactly the same way as with the breast, which is how we knew we had found the right bottle to help her to breastfeed again. In addition to the nipple shape: the anti colic vent is simple and effective, the bottle is ergonomically curved and easy to hold, paced feeding is easy thanks to good visibility of the milk and taller bottle shape, the cap is removable one handed and doesn't roll away, the bottle fits the lansinoh breast pump which is the best one we tried, and the measuring lines are readable and accurate. The bottle is a pretty standard size and fits nicely in diaper bags, cup holders, sterilizers, and bottle warmers. There are a few shortcomings though that hold this bottle back over its near twin, the Pigeon Nursing bottles. Firstly, the 2S "slow flow" nipple flow rate is way too high for infants and early babies. Our baby is 4 months old now and still chokes on the fast flow of these nipples. It might be fine for an older baby, but its a mess to feed baby with so fast a flow. I think Lansinoh assumes you have waited the unrealistic recommendation of 6 months before introducing a bottle and sets the nipple flow accordingly. They also annoyingly don't sell a slower flow. The other more minor gripe I have is that the bottle plastic is pretty thin and lightweight which doesn't seem like it will hold up over a long time. Ultimately these bottles are great, but I still wouldn't choose them because the pigeon bottles exist. They have the same shaped nipple available at slower flow rates, and sturdier bottle materials like borosilicate glass and PPSU. The caps are interchangeable between the bottle brands so you could also just buy a few Pigeon nipples for while the baby is too little to use the faster flow on these bottles.
TITLE: Great nipple shape, but flows too fast

BODY: I rented it a few years ago and decided I should have it, just because. Violent - lots of car crashes. Unseemly racism appears in the portrayal of Italians as, shall we say - slow learners and undesireable types. Looking to put some teeth into the Immigration Act - sound familiar? Howard Hughes produces his 1st movie here -Howard Hawks directs.but it's terribly important to Hughes to make this his entrance to Hollywood filmmaking. At the same time, within months of each other, or during an overlapping time frame the hugely popular Warner Bros' Public Enemy is made and released.first. Scarface following because of 'censureship' re-shoots, and stalls, they say. There are a number of 1/2 lines and a few scenes that echoe in the Scarface movie story and script of Public Enemy. Call it a 'Tip of the Hat ' to the director Michael Mann? I don't think so. This production is not as classy either; poor print and/or little lighting and not as clever, scene for scene, in the look as Public Enemy.This is in a rack all it's own. Paul Muni, as Tony, had to really put on the act for this and he excels at it. George Raft is also good to look at - he's never displaying all of his charms tho at once, ever. My favorite character and actor here is Vince Barnett as 'Angelo' Tony's secretary. He's adorable. His film and tv bio is longer than my arm; I must mention that the 1983 version with Al Pacino which we all love and nearly has it's own channel, taps most of this very story pretty much. Surprising how even the casting in some roles matched the original actors. I got this within a very short time frame from order date and I used standard shipping. It came in easy to open DVD box. Reliable dealer shipped it right away.

BODY: I purchased this to put in my DIY solar generator/power station/battery box I built. It was an excellent value on sale and even if it wasn't on sale it was an excellent value. Like others have mentioned the included hardware is a bit odd. The wrench fit and worked, but there were a few 40A fuses and no indication as to where those would go or why they'd be included. At about 900w AC output the inverter draws just over 100A DC, so 40A fuses aren't going to be useful. The manufacturer included a nice length of wire (about 3ft red and 3ft black) for connecting to the DC power source. I'd say the wire is probably close to or between 4AWG and 6AWG. It looks smaller than 4AWG, but bigger than 6AWG. It seems to be sufficient for my use. If you're going to run 1000W through this AC inverter regularly, I would probably upgrade the wires to 4AWG. I like that they included plastic covers for the DC terminals and little plastic acorn nuts to cover the bolts that stick through the plastic covers. Those cover those bolts, but also keep the plastic housings in place over the DC terminals so nothing can short out. I tested this with a 900W space heater without any issues. I didn't test how long it would run it for since that depends mostly on the battery, however it powers it up no problems. Some reviewers stated this isn't a pure sine wave inverter, but I disagree. I had a friend bring over an oscilloscope and test the AC output and we saw a pure sine wave output at 119 volts and 59.6 Hertz. That is within range of the specifications (59.5Hz to 60.5Hz) that power companies have to deliver to your home. I feel completely comfortable using and recommending this inverter. My only real complaint is that the company makes a remote power switch and display that can be mounted away from the inverter, however it is not included with the inverter. It appears to be an optional $20 part. It's not a deal breaker for the price, but if you need it, be sure to purchase it separately. Overall I would recommend this inverter if you're on a budget or just want to save a bit of extra cash. I can't speak to longevity, but it felt durable and heavy duty, so I expect it should last awhile, especially since I won't be running 1000W all the time and I won't be using it daily either. If I have any issues, I'll update this review.
TITLE: Decent Pure Sine Wave Inverter

BODY: I've been wanting an upstairs ice maker for a long while, and I like this one. It's compact, but does the job in the ice department. In one full batch, it will serve a few glasses perfectly fine. I do wish it held more though. But it fits in the corner next to my water cooler and blends right in. It's fast. In less than 10 minutes, you'll have a good amount of ice for a typical drink. This will not out perform my refrigerator, of course; not in amount or ice quality. The ice from my refrigerator is much more dense and lasts a lot longer in drinks. The mini ice maker's ice well dissolve much more quickly, but it will make your drinks cold. The biggest drawback is that it can be loud. Not so loud that it gives you regrets, but loud enough to be very noticeable and wish it wasn't so. In the middle of the night, it will do this too, but I guess you could turn it off at those hours to escape that (if it's in your bedroom). If you're serious about having lots and lots of ice, maybe invest in something more heavy duty; but if having a decent amount of ice at the ready for a good price is what you're going for, chances are you'll enjoy this.
TITLE: 3.75: Fast ice, water goes far, but can be loud

Soap dispenser isn’t working. Not good. Update: I tested the ‘soap dispener’ with plain water and it empties the tank as you would expect. Then I mixed the same soap 10/1 and it sent soap through, I could finally very by seeing the foam and the dispenser showed a reductino from the original amount (which I measured before and after the trials). So: 1) Water only worked great but useless 2) Soap at soap bottle 20/1 recommendation and it did nothing. 3) Soap at 10/1 and it seemed to work. BODY: This stand is well built and a good value for the money. The design of the tray leaves a little to be desired though, especially if using with a laptop, which is why I purchased this stand. If you have a larger laptop, like the 14" HP laptop I have, the laptop just fits inside the tray. I measured before I bought and thought I'd be fine. The laptop fits; that is not the problem. When I went to connect the power cable and USB cable is when I found the problem. The sides of the tray are about 3/8" high and are just high enough to completely block access to the ports. Sure, you could leave the laptop cattywampus and hanging off the side of the stand so there's room to plug the cables in, but how safe is that? Not to mention it looks amateurish. I received this stand the day of a big presentation I had to make where the laptop had to be plugged in for power, and a USB cable for audio out. Luckily, I'm handy and had the time to cut one side off. I used my angle grinder to cut off the lip on the right side and hit the bare metal with a quick shot of flat black spray paint. It looked factory and no one was the wiser. I don't know if I'd put the stated 22 pounds capacity on it, but something in the 10-15 pound range, sure. For my purposes, holding up a laptop while I hit a few buttons to get through the presentation and resting my hand on it a bit, it was great. It feels like a decently made product in this price range, if that makes sense. It's not a 200-pound, solid wood lectern by any stretch, but for it's intended purpose it's great. Just understand possible limitations, based on your use case. Would buy again.
TITLE: Good value for the money, but if using with a laptop you may need to modify it

BODY: Maybe I'm stupid, in which case hopefully others are, too, so this review actually helps someone (sorry for hoping that, stupid people; no disrespect intended) but when buying this, as someone who has never before knitted more than two rows and certainly never purchased, or sought to purchase, yarn, I thought this was the heavier weight yarn (bulky, specifically). I saw no difference between the terms "super bulky" and "chunky", figured they were slang terms, and figured with "Super" and "Jumbo" in the same product title, one of the two had to mean it was thick yarn. I hope this is less of a dumb mistake than I think it is. "Worsted-weight" definitely did NOT seem to mean it was "Medium" or "4" in any of the research I did (an unreasonable amount, maybe enough that I confused myself). Since it took me one very inexpensive purchase of this yarn to realize my mistake (the manufacturer's inferior product naming skills, or lacking placement of details on the product packaging, or bad choice of terminology by our ancestors) and the yarn is, nevertheless, really nice and strong and a consistent color and soft and looks good after being tied into slip-knots with sticks (knitted, I mean), I honestly feel bad knocking it a star, so if I am, after all, just a moron, and if enough people tell me this, I'll probably change it to 5 stars. It was a difficult decision, let me tell you.
TITLE: 4.5 stars? 4 stars with a fifth star that's just an outline that can be filled in? Great quality though!!!

BODY: The water at my place used to taste great 5-7 years ago. Since then it has gotten and continues to worsen in both taste and smell. I bought a Brita pitcher and once the original filters were used up, I bought these. They do a great job taking out the stink and awful taste. At the same time, the water has no flavor good or bad. It makes me wonder if there are any good minerals left in the water at all. Without minerals in the water, the water dilutes and flushes away the minerals in the body that we need to be healthy. While these minerals, vitamins and other nutrients need and can be replaced, that is an expensive and difficult process. The best sources are through diet, but that is already difficult and we can only eat so much in a sitting and or day. There are water dispensers out there with filters filter systems out there that leave the good stuff behind leaving the water tasting fabulous, clean, refreshing, and satisfying. The satisfying because the good nutrients are still in the water. These filters Do last a long time. I go over 6 months regularly. But, I am only 1 person and I don’t drink nearly enough water. The filter can float up out of place when filling the water reservoir if not securely inserted into place. The filter will sometimes fall or pop out of place when pouring a glass of water. When this happens with the lid is on, the lid pops off with a lot of force causing big splashing and dumping of water. This happens even when it’s positioned correctly and checked to make sure it’s and secure. Sometimes this happened even when I’ve poured some other glasses from the same batch of added water before the mishap. The water mess can cause time consuming and frustrating cleanup. It can cause permanent and/or costly damage to items such as furniture, carpeting, clothing, and paper products including personal and professional photos, documents and books, etc. The spills can also cause cooking/baking processes to be interrupted and/or damaging to whatever you are making. The filters aren’t cheap, but given how much longer they last than the standard filter, they have less environmental impact. When I first bought the pitcher, there were boxes for sale that had 4 or more filters in it. I haven’t seen those available in a while and the price per unit has gone up quite a bit on buying 1 or 2 packs. I wish they could make a filter out of stainless steel with a natural fibers and filtering substances, like charcoal, but not necessarily charcoal. Recap: The product is a mixed bag. Pros: Price: less than monthly filter replacement and other water filtering products. Convenience: easier than monthly replacement. Less time spent buying. Filter effectiveness: Can get out the things in water that make it taste and smell bad. (Results will vary depending on where you live, water pipe ages and materials the pipes are made of, (in your home, neighborhood, town, county), public water treatment plants, well water and water sources, polluting sources nearby, what is the water, etc.) Cons: Filtering: so effective the water ends up flushing out and/or diluting the minerals, vitamins, and other nutrients one needs for a healthy body. Filter can float out of place when filling the reservoir. Filter can and does sometimes fall or pop out of place which causes the pitcher lid to forcefully pop off, time consuming cleanup, damage to food products (and inadvertently cause damage the products used to cook and bake), at any time during and after the process, and can cause costly repairable damage and/or costly permanent damage to personal and professional, items from inexpensive to very expensive. 3) Causes permanent environmental damage. 4) Expensive to buy.
TITLE: It filters.

BODY: After trying Liquid I.V.'s Hydration Multiplier in Lemon Lime flavor for a month, I can say it's become a regular part of my hydration routine. Pros: - Noticeably quick hydration, especially post-workout - Pleasant lemon-lime taste that's not too artificial - Convenient individual packets for on-the-go use - Dissolves easily in cold water - Contains essential electrolytes and vitamins - One pack makes a full 16 oz serving Cons: - Slightly higher sugar content than I'd prefer (11g per serving) - Can taste a bit too sweet if not diluted properly - Price point is higher than basic electrolyte drinks - Some packets had clumped powder due to moisture I initially bought this to combat dehydration during intense summer workouts, and it definitely delivers on its promise. The science behind their "Cellular Transport Technology" seems to work - I feel hydrated faster than with plain water alone. The lemon-lime flavor is refreshing and reminds me of a less intense Gatorade. Pro tip: I find it tastes best when mixed with 20 oz of water instead of the recommended 16 oz, which helps balance the sweetness. As someone who struggles to stay properly hydrated, I appreciate how easy these packets make it to maintain electrolyte levels. They're perfect for travel, hiking, or recovering from a night out. I've even started keeping a few packets in my desk drawer for afternoon energy slumps. Value-wise, while it's pricier than some alternatives, the effectiveness makes it worth it for me. However, I do wish they'd offer larger bulk packages for better pricing. Would I buy again? Yes, but I'll probably alternate with other hydration solutions due to the sugar content. It's an excellent product for specific situations like intense exercise or travel, rather than daily use. Worth noting: If you're sensitive to stevia, you might detect a slight aftertaste, though it's less noticeable than in many similar products. Overall, a solid hydration solution that delivers on its promises, even if it comes at a premium price point.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
TITLE: A Reliable Hydration Solution with a Zesty Kick

BODY: I had my Yeti for work - presenting at the occasional conference or event (often to be prerecorded), where my laptop mic or airpods just weren't cutting it. It was easy to set up and just worked - once I'd figured out the right audio pattern to use. After a year it stopped working, stopped powering on completely. I tried replacing the cable, different ports, via the hub and direct and nothing worked. I contacted Logitech support and after a bit of back and forth on chat was told it was a different department and they'd email me. Here we go, I thought, prepared for the runaround. To my surprise I got an email a few hours later asking for specifics (where I bought it, photo of the serial number, etc). The next day they told me they'd replace it and asked for the shipping address. And three days later (there was a weekend in there) a new one arrived. No need to send the old one back. In today's online experiences, customer support is something I've come to dread. But this was great - no long wait on hold with really bad music and intermittent ads bursting your eardrums, no frustrating chat bot sending you in circles only to end the session and ask you to rate how they did. I'd experienced bad Logitech support in the past, which is why I tend to avoid them now (I hadn't realized that the Blue Yeti was one of there's) but this is a pleasant surprise.
TITLE: Good customer support

BODY: PROS: Creates glossy, silky. smooth hair styles best for long, thick hair. Removes unwanted waves and curls. Dries and styles very quickly. CONS: Really too big for shoulder length or shorter, and layered styles. Straightens hair, removes body. I have layered, slightly longer than shoulder length hair that is fine textured but thick. As hair classifications go, it is "2A" or as I used to call it, "wavy-straight." I color it and it tends to be dry, especially at the ends. I usually style it by blowing it dry with a 2" round brush, which creates waves and lots of long lasting body. This Revlon One Step dryer-brush has gotten lots of hype and I was curious to see how it would work for my hair. I searched it out in retail beauty stores. I've held it in my hand and was concerned that the brush head, which is 4.25", would be too large a surface for my layered, mid length hair. It's a big and fairly heavy device and felt clunky. I was also concerned about the heat burning my hair. When Amazon Prime Day came, I fell for the super duper price and bought the One Step. The brush head is a flattened oval with bristles. The hair is supported well by the bristles which keeps the hair close to the openings in the brush. There is no knotting or catching of the hair which is able to run smoothly through and around the bristles as it dries. There are three heat settings: "Hot," "Warm," and "Cool." The controls are at the end of the brush handle, and while well marked, are nearly impossible to change while using the device. I'm used to a hand held pistol-shaped dryer that allows one to switch temperatures, air strength, or from hot to cool on the go...with one hand. I have yet to be able to do this with the One Step. You divide your hair into manageable sections and run the brush repeatedly through the hair from roots to ends until it is dry and styled. If you place the shorter end of the oval close to the scalp, you will get some lift. If you place the wider sides of the oval close to the scalp, you will straighten and flatten your hair. If it is long enough, you can wrap your hair around the brush as it dries. If you place the short end of the oval at your ends and hold the brush for a few seconds, you'll get a small wave or flip. I found that the One Step dried my thick hair very quickly, faster than with a round bristled brush. My hair was very smooth, glossy, and close to my head. I would have preferred more "root-lift" especially at the crown, but I have had professional blow outs that looked similar to what I got with the One Step. All in all, the results were surprisingly good...even for such a large brush head. Instructions warn that if you have fine or dry hair to only use the "Warm" and not the "Hot" setting. This is very good advice and it should be followed. Only use the "Hot" setting if you have very thick, wavy, curly coarse hair. NOTE: If you check out the product page, you will notice that Revlon has a comparison picture of this "One Step 4.25" Head" brush and a smaller headed brush device which it recommends for shorter or layered or wavier hair styles. This smaller device is called "The Titanium Max, 2.4" head." Before I purchased this "One Step 4.25 head," I went in search of the 2.4" version. A large box store that starts with a "C" and ends with an "O" has an exclusive on this item, and of course, it was completely sold out. This smaller headed item was not on the Revlon web site. I contacted Helen of Troy (who makes these items for Revlon) Customer Service. The CS rep seemed to know nothing about the "Titanium Max" but told me, incorrectly that the "One Step" on Amazon had a 2.4" head. She also told me that a 1.5" headed brush dryer might be released by around the end of December. So, Revlon knows a good thing when it sees one and is expanding the line, but has yet to update it's Customer Service department with any correct or meaningful information. NOTE: I revisited the C****O web site and was able to purchase a single "Revlon One Step Titanium Max 2.4" Head" dryer brush. It should arrive in about five days. I'll review it on the bottom of this review after I play around with it a bit. NOTE 11/5/2020: C****O cancelled my order for the Titanium Max 2.4 Head. Sigh. After a bit of research I discovered that the Hot Tools brand is also owned by Helen of Troy and has a One-Step brush drier in the smaller 2.4" size virtually identical to the one described (but unavailable) from Revlon. I settled on the Hot Tools Professional Black Gold One-Step 2.4" which I purchased from Amazon for twice the price of the Revlon One Step. I selected the Black Gold because it had a ceramic coating that is supposedly best for fine, easily damaged hair. The head is also removable and can be exchanged with a 4.2" head, should I wish to purchase one separately. I like having that option. I'm going to try out the new Hot Tools for several weeks and will review it then.
TITLE: Best For Long Thick Hair, but...

If I could continue with the older one, I actually prefer it, but maybe I will get used to the yellow tint to the new Kindle paperwhite. I had to upgrade my device due to inability to update software and could no longer upload books to my device. Same thing happened to my Kindle fire so I lost two devices due to not being able to update them. The trade-in offer was only five dollars for each old device so it wasn’t worth doing. In conclusion, there are some upgrades to the older version mainly the smoother scrolling and easier to see book covers in the library, but sadly, I have to say I prefer the older model. With all of the time that has passed between making both of these devices I thought the newer device was going to be much better, but it’s marginally improved only. I contacted Amazon regarding the yellow hue to the paperwhite pages and they sent me a replacement device. The replacement device is slightly less yellow with a more consistent color across the screen, so I am much happier with it.
TITLE: Good customer service.
VARIATION: Color: Black Option: Without Kindle Unlimited Offer Type: Ad-supported

BODY: I have purchased this tablet (in new condition) two times so far (and a third one in refurbished condition on way) So why I like this tablet? 1. At the same time I see this tab again go up on discount & I decided to buy all the three(tab, keyboard cover & pen) I am typing this review from this new setup 3. I am going to give the refurbished tab to my mom for her digital content watching when it arrives what I like & dislike so far? 1. The price to value is very high. The tablet is decent for its price segment & throw the keyboard cover, it has made a decent compute device(email, browse, banking etc) which is way better than the chromebook I have. I have sideloaded the google playstore & have access to millions of the apps which we use. Otherwise amazon's app store is stunted & not useful for making this a business device 2. The setup is small yet useful. Unlike iPad which is not only bulkier and heavier, my whole tab+keyboard+pen doesnt feel heavy at all. I like the display clarity even though its just 1080P 3. I dont like how amzon locks its default apps and also doesnt allow us to modify the launcher app. I am a heavy customizer & use Nova for making sure my phones/tablets look the way I want it to be. But amazon dont let me get there by default. The sad part is, their default launcher is crappy & built for showing the ad's. For someone who is fully into Amazon ecosystem, this device just works. From connecting to a saved WiFi network to logging into default account, amazon has made many things seamless. But for someone like me, who hates getting caged in the OS, I give four stars.
TITLE: Decent tablet (I use it as a portable compute device)
VARIATION: Digital Storage Capacity: 32 GB Offer Type: With Lockscreen Ads Color: Lilac Style: Amazon Fire HD 10

BODY: As someone who's been using the Etekcity Food Kitchen Scale for several months now, I can confidently say it's become an indispensable tool in my kitchen. Whether you're a fitness enthusiast tracking macros or a baker striving for the perfect loaf, this scale offers a range of features that cater to various needs. Pros: + Accuracy: For the most part, the scale provides precise measurements in both grams and ounces, which is crucial for recipes and portion control. + Sleek Design: The stainless steel construction not only looks modern but is also easy to clean and maintain. + User-Friendly: The backlit LCD display is clear and easy to read, even in low light conditions. + Versatility: Multiple unit options (g, oz, lb:oz, ml) make it adaptable for various recipes and needs. + Tare Function: This feature allows for easy measurement of ingredients in containers, which is a huge time-saver. + Compact Size: It doesn't take up much counter space and can be easily stored in a drawer. + Long Battery Life: Many users report the batteries lasting for months with regular use. Cons: - Sensitivity Issues: Some users have reported that the scale can be overly sensitive to touch, which can affect readings. - Delayed Response: There can be a slight delay in weight readings, especially when measuring liquids or adding small increments. - Auto-Off Feature: While designed to save battery, the quick auto-off can be frustrating if you're in the middle of measuring multiple ingredients. - Limited Weight Capacity: At 11 lbs (5kg) max, it may not be suitable for larger batch cooking. - Inconsistent Readings: A few users have experienced fluctuating measurements, especially with very light items. In my experience, the scale has been a reliable kitchen companion. I've found it particularly useful for baking bread, where precise measurements can make or break a recipe. The tare function has been a game-changer, allowing me to measure multiple ingredients in the same bowl without mental math. However, I have noticed that when measuring very small quantities (less than 5 grams), the scale can sometimes struggle to register changes immediately. This requires a bit of patience and occasionally a gentle tap to update the reading. Despite these minor drawbacks, I believe the Etekcity Food Kitchen Scale offers excellent value for its price point. It's not perfect, but it's more than capable of handling most kitchen tasks with ease. Whether you're counting calories, portioning meals, or perfecting your baking skills, this scale is a solid choice that won't break the bank.
TITLE: Precise Portions, Impressive Features: A Kitchen Scale That Mostly Delivers

BODY: The panels (5-stars) work well...when... ...they actually get around to shipping them to you. I've eeduced 2-stars because of the aggravation with shipping. The vendor/Amazon will claim that the item has shipped, for weeks, but the truth is revealed as UPS (the record of truth) always denies receiving the the item(s). You may get the item in 3 weeks. This situation is worse if the panels are on sale (10% off, etc) - even longer transit times. You may get the item in a month or more. So the panels are very good but shipping games are terrible. Also, the vendor hasn't figured out how to use packing tape (say 3-4 8" pieces), so that their overall good packaging won't pop open - with damaged items eventually arriving. To be fair, they will provide you with a UPS label to send them back. This is good. Those responsible for these Renogy fulfillment blunders should be replaced, yielding more profit for Renogy and more satisfied customers that spread the word. I am ready to buy 6-7 more of these 200W panels, but...what further headaches will I be dealing with? It is amazing how some of these companies manage to make a profit. This isn't rocket science! One smart person put in charge of fulfilling orders, and like magic, business would improve a lot. I will update this review when I attempt this drawn-out journey again. Update 10/4/24: This last panel ordered arrived quickly and in a nice new sturdy box. See what happens when the smart people get involved. Adding one more point!
TITLE: Good so far, very difficult to get them to ship on time

BODY: Who better to turn to during the spookiest month of the year than the master of Horror himself? It has been some time since I picked up a Stephen King book, and I decided it was time to go way back and tackle one of his earliest that has been on my list the longest, 'Salem's Lot. And let's be honest, absolutely devouring Mike Flanagan's recently released Midnight Mass had me ravenous for another good vampire story. I always enjoy when a story makes it clear up front that it will be more about the journey than the destination. The prologue of this book makes it quite clear where we'll end up, and so I was prepped from the beginning to just take the story as it was given to me and enjoy the ride, and the excellent characterization which King achieves. It's a very slow burn, but there's also somehow this sense of everything happening very quickly, when the dark realization creeps over you of just how easily a vampire and his brood can turn a town. And no one's safe, is the thing. It is both shocking and obvious how quickly things go from alright to beyond repair in a situation like this. And anyway there's just something about Stephen King. It is this something that has led to his astronomical success, and has kept people coming back to him year after year after year. He's just a natural storyteller, and his stories feel like an embrace. Even this early work has the occasional paragraph of such piercing linguistic beauty that you can't help but raise an eyebrow. So maybe you've missed the season, but the next time you feel like taking a visit to small-town Maine—the next time you feel like it's been a little too long since you've been afraid of the things that haunt the night—you know where to go. ‘Her mind was clear, still unimpressed with this talk of bloodsuckers and the undead. It was from her spinal cord, a much older network of nerves and ganglia, that the black dread emanated in waves.’
TITLE: Cozy up for a trip to the Lot.

I ran a quick check using the diagnose in the Samsung Member app on both of them, everything seems okay including the battery, and as far as I can tell, both of them are unlocked. There are two things I do want to point out. First, I believe starting from s20, there is no more 3.5mm earphone jack, so remember to get a new earphone or an adaptor. Second, for this model particular and maybe all s21, there is no SD card slot, so you only have 128gb fixed. The one star off was for the charger that came with it. You can tell it's one of those cheap ones the moment you touch it. You can also see that the port is not aligned in the photo. What's super funny about it is that, the brick works with other cables, the cable works with other bricks, but when you put this brick and this cable together, they don't work. If you guys are buying chargers like this, I will just suggest not to include a charger so you guys can reduce some cost. With that said, this is definitely an awesome deal. BODY: I recently purchased the Duck Brand Small Bubble Cushioning Wrap for a move, and it has been mostly excellent. Here’s why I recommend this product with a small caveat: Effective Protection: The small bubble cushioning provides excellent protection for fragile items. I used it to wrap glassware, electronics, and other delicate items, and everything arrived at the new location without a single scratch or break. Generous Length: The 175 ft roll is more than enough for most moving and shipping needs. I was able to wrap all my fragile items and still had plenty left over for future use. Perforation Issue: While the wrap is perforated every 12 inches for convenience, I found that the perforations don’t always tear easily between sheets. This can be a bit frustrating when you’re in the middle of packing and need to use scissors to get a clean tear. Clear and Versatile: The clear bubble wrap allows you to see the items wrapped inside, which is helpful when organizing and unpacking. It’s versatile enough for packing boxes, mailing items, or even storing fragile goods. Durable Quality: The bubble wrap is of high quality and durable. The bubbles don’t easily pop or deflate, providing consistent cushioning throughout the move. Easy to Use: Despite the perforation issue, the roll is lightweight and easy to handle. BODY: After two weeks of use, I can confidently say the Ubbi stainless steel diaper pail does exactly what it promises. The biggest standout feature—and what truly sets it apart from most others—is that it works with regular kitchen trash bags. No more being locked into overpriced, proprietary refills. That alone makes it a winner in my book. In fact, the ability to use your own bags can help offset the higher upfront price of the pail over time. Another big plus is the design. It's compact, sleek, and comes in tons of color options. Ours looks great in the nursery, and we don’t feel the need to hide it away in a bathroom. Performance-wise, it holds in odors very well when used properly, which is critical with a newborn in the house. That said, there are a few trade-offs. The opening at the top is quite small, so you have to roll or fold diapers up tightly to get them in cleanly. The overall size could also be larger—we find our one-month-old fills it up in about 5–6 days, which isn’t quite enough to make it to the weekly trash pickup. Once full, removing the trash bag can be tricky. It gets snug inside, and there’s definitely a risk of tearing the bag during removal—though to be fair, that’s likely a common issue across diaper pails. Also, they recommend not using water or cleaning products to clean, which seems unrealistic if a mess happens—wiping it down alone may not be enough. Another notable issue is securing the trash bag. The inner ring that holds the bag in place is the same small size as the opening, so the large trash bag opening tends to pop out as diapers accumulate. I solved this by tying a knot in the bag underside the ring, but it’s a bit of a workaround. Finally, I’ve noticed other reviews mentioning the plastic components can break over time, which does make me question long-term durability. Still, for the convenience, looks, odor control, and long-term savings on bags, this is one of the better diaper pails out there.
TITLE: Great Looking & Functional – But Not Without Flaws

BODY: After reading some of his later work and the reading this, his second book, I have to admit that his earlier work is much superior to newer things. The characters are more developed, the story is deeper and the picture he paints is much more vivid and beautiful. This book is no exception. Mr. King paints a beautiful story of horror as it pertains to the prospect of vampire infestation in a small town. More frightening than that prospect though is the town itself. All small towns have their smattering of odd characters, skeletons in the closet, and horrific events. King paints 'Salem's Lot as a town that has all of this. The characters are well established and have their own voice. He writes the story so descriptively that you can envision every scene, character, down to smelling smells and tasting tastes. Some of the elements are sad, as a new parent I was espcially moved my baby Randy's story, it actually brought great sadness to my heart. The only thing that takes a bit away from the book is sometimes it is hard to follow all the characters. There are so many of them that it occasionally takes you some time to remember their back story and their introduction earlier in the book. Overall this is a great book; enjoyable, fun, frigtening, sad, and wonderful.
TITLE: Stephen King at his best

BODY: I don’t really eat a lot of protein bars, and a lot of the ones I’ve tried have been fairly disappointing. However, I hadn’t seen these flavors before, and all three flavors sounded interesting even though I was fairly certain that the actual experience of eating these would fall short of the promise. After trying all three flavors, I have to say that I am pleasantly surprised. Now, these do have the typical dense texture you’ve probably come to expect from protein bars, and there are quite a few bars out there made with either almond or peanut butter plus a syrup from either tapioca or dates or both and a lot of other ingredients either similar or identical to the ones in these bars, so to a certain degree these bars taste very similar to a lot of other bars I’ve tried. However, even with all of that, they managed to capture the flavors they promised surprisingly well. I’m not saying that eating these will make you think for a moment that you’re eating a churro or Mexican chocolate or a peanut butter and jelly sandwich, but I feel as though you get those flavors in these. So often I’ll try a bar that promises to be this or that and doesn’t end up tasting anything at all like the listed flavor. That’s not the case here. I definitely got each of these flavors. I’d say that the Mexican chocolate was the most successful. While not super chocolatey, it had some chocolate flavor, and it definitely had the cinnamon and even some chili to it. The PB&J was the next best with this one using peanut butter and peanuts instead of almond butter, and it had some fruity chucks. The churro was fine, and even though there’s some cinnamon and sweetness to it, I didn’t feel as though it really hit that churro flavor. Maybe it’s because so much of the churro thing is the texture with the fried dough. As for whether these bars justify the price, that’s a bit difficult to say. For me, probably not. I liked them, and I’ll happily eat all of them, and I think they taste better than most other protein bars of similar formulation, but they’re certainly not to die for. With that being said, I don’t eat tons of protein bars. If you do, maybe these are worth paying for because they’re at least some different flavors that you might not have had before, and they taste decent.
TITLE: Texture is similar to lots of other bars, but I think the flavors here are better than most

BODY: The Apple AirTag offers an incredibly convenient and integrated way to keep track of your belongings, leveraging Apple's vast Find My network. Its setup is quintessential Apple: simple and intuitive. Just bring it near your iPhone, and it pairs almost instantly 📍📱. The precision finding feature, available on iPhones with the U1 chip, is particularly impressive, guiding you with visual, haptic, and audible cues to your lost item's exact location. For items further afield, the Find My network, comprised of hundreds of millions of Apple devices, can anonymously and securely help locate your AirTag. Its small, coin-like design is sleek, but it does mean that to attach it to keys or bags, you'll almost certainly need to purchase a separate holder or keychain, which adds to the overall cost. The replaceable CR2032 battery is a plus, offering decent longevity before needing a swap. While its reliance on the Apple ecosystem means it's primarily for those already invested in Apple products, for that user base, it's a highly effective and easy-to-use item tracker. The privacy features built-in are also commendable, designed to discourage unwanted tracking. A great solution for peace of mind, especially for those prone to misplacing their essentials. 👍
TITLE: Seamless Item Tracking for Apple Users

BODY: Ok, 1st month is complete. I think it’s a great vitamin. But, no dramatic results yet. My hair seems healthier. I have 1 thin/almost bald spot. I have had long, thick hair my whole life. After I turned 45, my hair has started falling out. I’m seeing a doctor, as well. I was found to have low vitamin D. My doctor is on board with me trying Nutrofol, and she has had patients report great results after taking for a few months. 1st month~ my hair appears and feels healthy (it was too dry/almost felt crunchy.. yes.. crunchy!) I’m hoping a spot grows back. Nothing yet. As for my hair falling out: it’s STILL falling out/hasn’t stopped. I would at least like to keep what I’ve got. No improvement, yet. So… improvement in hair texture but, no new growth. My hair … (definitely) continues to fall out. I will update next month. **(Nothing went on for at least 3mos.) 5-17~ (7-8 mos) I think it’s a great vitamin. I feel better. As for my hair/I’m not sure … no dramatic, magical results. I think my hair is more healthy. I’ve got new growth and it’s not what I was expecting… dumb on my part… it’s truly new growth… so, I have long hair but spots of 2-3 inches “new” hair… it looks odd/like a cow lick. I’m glad it’s growing but, it’s areas you have to tame/cover/pin down. I should know it’s not going to be a full length strand. Anyway, manage expectations. It is growing. My hair is STILL falling out. I was hoping I could keep what I have and stop the hair from falling out. Nope! It’s still falling out. I think, it’s working but I was hoping for more. 4 star ⭐️ Improvement but, no major change. It might be keeping me from being bald. Great multi vitamin but I don’t know if it’s worth the money..a little torn. I’m continuing for now. I committed to “trying” just for me, for 1 year. Right now: it’s good but I’m not sure about $90 a month part. … on the fence. No harm in trying, I’m just not sure if it’s worth it. I’ll update at 1 year. 😉
TITLE: Will update/add stars (monthly) if it works!

BODY: So, the first SE was delayed a couple weeks due to storms; OK with me. But when it finally arrived Apple immediately threw up a message that the battery condition was critically low and the battery should be replaced. Battery Maximum Capacity was only 79%. I deduct one star for this. Seller guarantees 80% minimum, so I sent it back and asked them to send me a replacement. Maybe it was 80% when they sent it?! I don't know. BTW all the phone exterior looked like new. The replacement SE also looks like new (and I am extremely picky---especially regarding screen condition.) I was really happy to see the Battery Maximum Capacity of this replacement at 93%(!) Way better than 79% you might agree. I have only one dedicated purpose for this SE and that is to run the Ecobee app to set their security system on and off. I mount this on the wall right next to my Ring keypad. Suits me fine: *2* security systems. (I was using an iPhone 7 but its screen is defective slightly, it's a lot slower and it's not compatible with Apple's newest software = too old.) Overall I am very happy with the replacement SE, which BTW arrived very fast.
TITLE: #1 returned; replacement great

BODY: I don’t usually write product reviews… But being stuck in quarantine and being pregnant for the first time, I realized how much I relied on the reviews of others to help me shop for things I needed. This is my first pregnancy, I’m 32 years old, and I also had spinal fusion surgery 10 years ago…I say all this because I had been getting severe hip pain during my pregnancy near the end of the first trimester beginning of second… And it has been persistent. I was sleeping with a pillow between my knees before I bought this pillow but I would wake up in the middle of the night with excruciating pain in my hips. I read others reviews, looked up the top 10 best pregnancy pillows… And I chose this one because others said it helped with their hip pain too. Here are my pros and cons: Pros: It definitely helped my hip pain I would say about 90%… And if you didn’t have a history of other issues then maybe you’ll be just fine! Now that I’m using it into my third trimester I’m noticing that it really cradles and hugs my body very comfortably because I’ve been starting to get sciatic pain and it definitely eases that pain in my lower back and butt. The padding is comfortable, and I washed the pillowcase a couple times so far… It is a little bit of a pain to get on, but with the help of one other person that’s fine. I was able to use it on our couch in a more comfortable position because the couch became uncomfortable to sit on with the big belly. So it’s versatile! Although I haven’t tried it yet… I plan on using this after the baby is born to help with nursing… So it saves me the trouble from getting a separate nursing pillow. Cons: It takes up so much space in the bed! We have a queen size bed and my poor husband is always near the edge no matter how much I try and pull it closer to me. It makes getting in and out of bed extremely cumbersome and annoying. Especially when you have to get up to Pee every two hours… Or you feel like you have to turn over to The opposite side. I feel like I’m wrestling a seahorse every night. You definitely feel a bit warmer because of how it cradles you… So keep the AC cranked! Storing it is difficult so I just kind of drape it across the other throw pillows on the bed. Overall, if I had to do it all over again I would still buy this pillow! The relief that it has been giving me with my hip pain was worth it by a landslide! If you don’t suffer from any hip pain during your pregnancy, you can probably get away with a smaller knee pillow… But I just couldn’t bear at myself! And just to test whether the pillow is actually relieving my hip pain or not, I’ve gone to bed without this pillow about 5 separate occasions and just put a regular pillow between my knees with one for my head… And I would wake up with the same excruciating hip pain! Then I would use the maternity pillow and sleep like a baby again. So that proved to me it was a great purchase.
TITLE: Helped with Hip Pain!!!! Would buy again!

BODY:
I usually buy Amazon brand

PRESTO Paper Towels

because they are just like BountySelect a Size. I tried this Amazon Basics because I was curious how different they could be, and since it was quite a bit cheaper I figured it was worth the try. Even bad ones are usually still "OK" so long as they arent like tissue. These arent bad, but they are thin. They work, but again they are thin. I cant think of what they remind me of other than an obviously cheaper paper towel. I prefer the slightly thicker and robust Presto, but truly these are "fine". They arent tissue. They dont fall apart, they dont leave bits behind. I think if looking for a truly budget friendly option without it being total junk, this is worth the try. Perfectly reasonable for light duty needs. If you like and prefer"Bounty", then give the Presto brand (an Amazon brand) a try. Or try these but know they will be thin. I may continue with this. Or I may buy both. Overall I am surprised that these are "good" despite how I felt when I first touched it.
TITLE: thin and light, but doesnt fall apart. Pretty good overall, but choose Presto if like Bounty

BODY: My 2 boxes of Amazon Brand - Happy Belly Coffee Pods - 100 count just came, and I'm drinking my 1st cup (of it, not cups total for the day - though I'm trying to not go above 3 cups daily). I'm having it like I do most, which is 10 ounces of brewed water, a splash of cold water, 2% milk, Walmart French Vanilla creamer, and Hershey's Simply 5 chocolate syrup - which is their most expensive choice, as it doesn't use high fructose corn syrup. It tastes fine - but I'm NOT a finicky coffee drinker. I just try to buy and drink those pods I like to some degree, (not bitter) and is relatively inexpensive at .25 - .30 cents each. This meets those lax standards. Of course, if you're willing to pay 2 to 4 times that, (.50 to $1.00) you can get some very addictive coffee pods made by Starbuck's! My other habit is to buy cheap coffee in the can, and add some flavored coffee to it, and brew in a reusable pod w/ 12 ounces of water (a large cup). Those can get the cost down to about .15 cents for a large cup, before creamer, and (many claim) is better for the environment. When buying Amazon's brand, note that you have to make sure you're making a one-time purchase, and not regularly, through their Subscribe and Save (unless you want that, as the price is slightly lower). Apparently, I clicked that radio button once, to see the cost, didn't change it back, and didn't buy any whatsoever. But at some point I did save it the Save for Later Cart. Online days later, when I went to buy it, it had kept the subscription choice from days prior, and I noticed it just before purchasing.

BODY: So I pretty much exclusively wear this brand. I’ve had SO many people think that I was wearing acrylics and they were amazed when I told them these were press-ons. With the right press on shape they also hold really well (usually within the first few days of initial application I always end up reapplying all the nails at least once but then after that these hold up well for 1.5-2weeks). With that said, some things to consider: -my biggest gripe with this brand is that they don’t put enough nails in with their packs to give you a second use out of the nails (idk about yall but I would never reuse a full set of nails after already wearing them for two weeks, doesn’t seem sanitary at all tbh). So when you buy a cute $15 set from Glamnetics, you’re only getting enough for one full application. I think that’s really cheap on the brands end, because I’ve never bought press ons from another brand where there wasn’t at least enough for two whole applications in the set. -they definitely do not have uniform sizing across their shapes. For example, I have very skinny/slender fingers with a small nail bed. The almond-shaped nails are a perfect fit for me, but any short oval/square tip type nails are clearly meant for wider nail beds (it’s to the point where I’m forcing nails onto my nail beds that are way too big for them, such as with my thumb- the smallest size they offer for the thumb is still too big). So just be aware that there is definitely inconsistency with the sizing, so if you try one shape and they fit great, but then try another shape and they don’t fit at all, it’s not you, it’s definitely a different size
TITLE: Nails look like real acrylics, but you only get one use out of each pack
