BODY: I’ve spent the last number of years using a slew of different phones from OnePlus, LG, Motorola, Huawei, Sony, and the last HTC. I haven’t used a Samsung since the S7. I’ve avoided them because while they were well built, they had software that was so bloated, it drove me nuts. So, what is it like going to Samsung after so long? HARDWARE Back in the day, only Apple rivaled Samsung when it came to the best hardware. Samsung can no longer make that claim. Granted, this phone is excellent, but OnePlus, LG, and Apple are all on the same level in terms of quality build. This phone feels heavy in your hand. I personally like that, but someone coming from an Apple might be very disappointed. Going light weight was not a big consideration here. It seems like they were more focused on packing as much as possible into this phone. That leads to a big issue. Samsung has been cowed into trying to be like Apple and removed both the headphones jack and sd card slot. I see no real reason for this beyond saving money. Apple and Samsung claim that they can’t fit it in, or that no one uses them anymore. Both of those are nothing but lame excuse. Both LG and Sony put both items into their last flagships. LG in fact released HD audio to go with the headphones jack. After using both phones, I REALLY miss both those items. They added a huge amount of functionality and made doing certain things so much easier. They did add the latest and greatest 5G and Wifi connectivity to this phone. That sounds great, but I haven’t noticed it all. I even live in an area where 5G is everywhere. There might be more speed, but everything in my area was already fast. It’s hard to really notice a difference unless you are running a speed test. So, this phone is S-Pen compatible. Great. It would be really good if it actually came with an S-Pen, but you’ll have to pay more for that. By more I mean $40 for an s-pen designed for the Ultra, that is missing some functionality. That’s right, you can’t do gestures. If that’s important to you, you need to wait for the S-Pen Pro to be released “later” in 2021. That one will have a BT connection. That really seems like a fumble on Samsung’s part. I can understand making the s-pen optional and not having a silo for it, like on the Note, but not having the full-blown version available at launch feels like they are missing an opportunity. If people get the regular s-pen, decide they don’t use it, what’s to motivate them into getting the pro version? The S-pen can be handy, but it only makes sense if you get a case that can hold. Thankfully Spigen makes one that is cheap and works well. On the plus side, this phone has the fastest, most accurate fingerprint reader I’ve ever used. It’s easily twice as fast as the one LG was using. The ultrasonic format really makes a difference. CAMERA When I first used the Huawei P20 Pro, I was blown away by how good that camera was. The things it did were unprecedented in a camera phone. I used a number of phones after that one, and none of them ever matched what Huawei could pull off. Samsung crushed it with this camera setup. The flexibility it offers is unmatched. From wide angle to super zoom, it’s all there. Some of the tricks they pull off with this camera are truly impressive. From how it combines photos for optimal exposure to the foolish 100x superzoom, the things you can do with this camera are insane. The 100x is nothing you’ll want to use a lot. It’s usable, but not overly clear. Still, the mere fact that you can take a usable 100x photo with this phone is mind blowing. When I say “usable” I mean a photo that is identifiable. It’s nothing I’m going to want to hang on a wall, but it might help trying to identify something. If there is one thing camera doesn’t do great, it’s exposure. It tends to over expose, but that’s it. That’s the only real issue I can find and it’s minor. You might see the 108MP sensor and think you can take some insane RAW photos with that. To a degree, that’s true, but with a sensor like that, you’d need a HUGE amount of light to be able to get good results. There is a reason why Samsung does pixel binning to get its incredible results. On its own 108MP in a sensor that size is not really usable, except in ideal conditions. I love a good wide-angle camera. LG was always a proponent of the wide-angle, and over the years, it’s really come in handy. It’s not something you use a lot, but when you need it, nothing beats it. You get two tele-photo lenses here. The 3X is great for framing your photos, and the 10x allows you to do things that no over phone I’ve ever used has allowed. The 10x really comes in handy for sunsets and times when you can’t get close to your subject. CHARGING I usually don’t do a write up on charging, but in this case, I think it’s needed. You have wired 25W or wireless 10W. I love that flexibility. It’s not all good though. You know what really stinks? Paying through the nose for a phone, opening the box, and not getting a charger. That’s right. No charger in the box. If you want one, you’ll be paying for it. It’s hard to wrap your head around the fact that they charge you over $1,000, but couldn’t give you a charger. I can’t remember the last time I got a phone with no charger. SCREEN Till now, OnePlus had the best screen I’d seen in a phone. It makes sense that Samsung would beat them, considering OnePlus got their screens from Samsung. This screen is amazing. It’s not just it’s looks either. They did some impressive trickery here too. The adaptive refresh is amazing. The big issue with a 120 refresh rate is the battery it burns through. With adaptive turned on, it adjusts. Reading a book or something similar – it drops as low as 10. This saves a huge amount of battery life. BATTERY LIFE It’s good. It’s not LG V60 good, but it’s good. There have been times when the LG V60 lasted me for two days with normal use. This one has the same battery, but doesn’t last that long. It will easily make it through the day and night though. SOFTWARE Welcome to the worst part about this phone. I know why Samsung doesn’t fix it too. To be honest, I hate the Samsung apps, I hate the excessive bloatware, and Bixby is the worst virtual assistant I’ve ever used. The UI is the more frustrating of the issues here. The main reason is tied to the fact that this is an Android phone. Why do you want to be so different from stock when it’s actually hindering the experience instead of improving it? I understand what Samsung is trying to do. They want to be Apple. They want their own ecosystem, they control, and reap large profits off of. That’s why they have their own assistant, apps, and UI. I’d applaud them for that, if they grow a pair and divorce Android. The only way all the apps, bloatware, assistant, and UI make sense if they are on their own. Just do it already. Become the third option, because as it stands now, your software is awful. It’s trying to sit on a fence, which means it’s not as good as it could be on its own, and it’s NO WHERE as good as stock Android. Now, if you are a handy when it comes to Android, you can remove a lot of the junk off this phone, via ADB. But, stop and think about that for a moment. You just paid top dollar for a phone, and now, you have to waste a solid hour of your time stripping off all the garbage they piled on there. How is that acceptable? If this phone was running software close to stock and didn’t have the bloatware issue, we’d be talking about a legendary phone. One of the best phones ever made. Instead, we have some of the best hardware ever combined in a phone, with steaming pile of dung laid on top of it. Again, if Samsung finally broke free of Android, I think things would be very different. They might also be watching Huawei very closely to see how everything pans out for them. Huawei was forced off Android and now runs its own OS and ecosystem. If it goes well for Huawei, I would not be surprised to see Samsung finally pull the trigger. They already have the OS. It’s been in their Smart appliances for years. I know I’ve bashed Samsung a lot here, but there are some things that do that are excellent. The first one I’ll mention is something that falls into the useful category. It’s their side tabs. The default is your recent apps or apps you’ve pinned. This comes in handy since I don’t like either of Samsung’s default launcher options. You can also add more tabs like weather and select contacts. I first used this with the S6 Edge and I’m glad it’s still around. Now for the thing that Samsung does best, and by best, I mean no one is even close. A deep dive into the sound settings will take you to an EQ setting. This is not impressive on its own, since most other makers offer this. What Samsung does that no one else does, is it gives you a hearing test and creates a custom sound profile for you. As someone who has hearing loss, the impact this has is incredible. The only thing I don’t understand is why it’s so buried. I mean you need to dig to find this thing. I find it rather ironic that Samsung is more on board with Windows integration than any other maker I’ve used. Samsung imbedded the features needed and allows you to use apps in Windows. The others I’ve used require you to download an app and you cannot use apps in Windows. This is a handy feature to have for anyone using an Windows PC. They also support the cut and paste feature. Copy on your phone – paste on your PC and vice versa. I have to relate one final frustrating issue with Samsung’s software. The last three phones I used all were on board with Google’s autofill for apps. That made signing into a new phone an easy experience. Samsung has its own thing. I spent quite a while hunting down passwords that I hadn’t used in nearly two years. WILDCARD There is a factor to consider, if you’ve only used Apple or Samsung products. You’ve been spoiled. By buying the most popular phones, you’ve always had a massive selection of cases and accessories. As someone who’s been using the fringe players for years, I’ve been stuck with only a few choices when it comes to cases. Sometimes it was very difficult to find cases, or I had to pay more than I would have liked. Having a massive selection does add to the value, since you end up paying less for cases as everyone tries to undercut each other. On top of that, you can find the crazy cases that include extra battery, wallets, and added functionality. If you’re using a Sony or OnePlus, you can only dream about such a thing. This becomes even more of a value I you are one of the people in a program like Amazon Vine. I was able to get three cases for this phone, through Vine. Before this, only once was I able to find a case for my phone. Another factor is that there are also apps on the Play Store that are dedicated to tweaking the Samsung experience. Some of them are quite useful. This is something you don’t find when using the minor players. VALUE Is this phone a good value? No. I’m sorry at its retail price, it’s not. The LG V60 might have a weaker camera and older processor, but it’s a far better value. The OnePlus 9 Pro, is a slightly better value, if we are talking about a full priced phone. If you want this phone to be a value, you need to land this phone for $900 or less. Anything higher just isn’t worth it. If you’re going to pay full price for this phone, you might want to look at the iPhone. I do not like iOS, but they have an excellent build, great camera, no bloatware, and fast updates. SHOULD YOU BUY THIS PHONE If money is not an issue and you love Samsung – yes. If money is no issue and you want the best camera – yes. If money is no issue and you want the best Android phone – maybe. If money is an issue and you want a great Android phone – buy the LG V60. If you want a clean Android experience – buy the Pixel 5 or OnePlus 9 Pro. If money isn’t an issue and you want the best phone – buy the iPhone 12 Pro Max. If you need to have a sd card and headphones jack – buy the LG V60. If you need to have a sd card and headphones jack, but hate LGs software – buy the Sony Xperia 1 II.
TITLE: One of the Best but Still Falling Short

BODY: This is a very solid tablet. The build is nice, the protective case is exceptional for little ones, it feels good in your hands, it's lightweight, and it loads quickly. I bought 2, for our 5 & 1 year old. We have been using the Contixo tablets for years (starting with our 9 year old). They are similarly priced and built, but the Contixo tablets use Google/Android. I know, I know. :) I knew this didn't have Google, Google Play, or web access when I bought it. What I didn't realize was how important that was for us, OR everything else that losing that functionality meant. The downside to Contixo tablets is that they are sometimes slow & buggy. The Fire tablet is far more fast and smooth. But, alas, it just didn't meet OUR needs. The MAIN things for us were: 1. No YouTube Kids (my kids love YT Kids & use it regularly), 2. No FamilyLink parental controls mgr...or similar program (I'm accustomed to that specific interface. I pull up the FL app, it shows me the location of the item on Google Maps, I can ping the item if it is misplaced, I can set screen limits, I can control what sites they visit, I can see their history, I can set bedtime, I can immediately lock the item, and so much more. 3. No reliable way to ping the tablet or make it ring when lost/misplaced. Our house isn't huge, but it is spacious & has many nooks & crannies. My kids put things down in the darndest places. I need to be able to quickly log into an app on my phone, press "ring/find tablet" and hear the item (so I can find it, similar to my cell phone). We've been looking for 1 child's TV remote for 9 months and still haven't found it (lol). So, that functionality is a huge deal for us. I also missed the simple home screen on the Contixo tablets. I can setup exactly what apps I want them to see on the home screen (like a cell phone). The Fire tablet had too many different options, suggested apps, & icons on the screen. I didn't really see an option to hide those or customize that view. Again, the Fire tablet is beautiful, it just didn't suit our needs. It felt like an extension of my FireTV or Prime Video home screen from our TV. That is too busy for my kids' needs. They want to click & open every show they see (& some of them are not free). If you just need a portable way for your kids to watch programs/shows from Prime Video, this is golden. Otherwise, meh. 4. There also aren't any games. My kids love the little coloring, spelling, and word games (Google Play Store) on their Contixo tablets. Again, worth mentioning three times, I am still very, very surprised about not having a way to ring the item if it was lost. I couldn't even install Lookout or a similar program to help. This is a child's item. They misplace things. At this price point, that should be a no-brainer. So basically if they lose it or lay it down out of sight, you are S.O.L. until you come across it. I saw many moms complain about these same few issues. I'm sure Amz will fix it in the future. I'll repurchase then. Returned both for now.
TITLE: Wanted to Love

BODY: When I first ordered this set, I chose "ship in manufacturer's packaging", and the hinges on 8 of 10 cases were broken! There is NO padding in the manufacturer's box. I use these boxes mostly for scrap paper, scrapbook paper, and book board off cuts. They're made from a soft-ish plastic, maybe slightly softer than the larger version that Michael's sells near the checkout. I didn't like putting a lot of paper into the large version, because it gets so heavy, so I ended up with a lot of wasted space. These thinner boxes let me categorize my papers and use my storage space more efficiently. The plastic scratches easily, but I'm not concerned--I already expected these to be utility containers, not show pieces. They'd be perfect to decorate with vinyl stickers! The lids would probably warp if heavy things were stacked on top, but they'll serve just fine in their own stack.

The Wheat Penny (1955) looks heavily circulated and worn. Nickle 1998 was in good shape. 1964 dime was in fair condition (slightly tarnished?) and 1970 quarter was in good shape. For the beginning collector, this is a fun set. It has a note that explains that these coin designs are discontinued. The value of the bills and coins (less then $5) makes me question the value of this set. However, theoretically, this should gain value if kept long enough.

BODY: We had a mouse invasion early this year due to Kids leaving doors open, in late winter - early spring. I had some classic wooden traps om hand, and a bucket with ramp and trap door. None of these worked with various baits. I decided to try these sticky traps. Know that mice can be easily removed from these sticky traps by placing oil (I use Olive Oil) under the mice and then holding the traps vertically. Every movement the mice makes pulls them away and the oil prevent reattachment. Usually the mouse is free within minute. It is important to see the mice and do this before they exhaust themselves and die or are too weak to release themselves in this manner or survive once released. Out of 50 mice I would say 40+ were released alive. My son liked this job. First let me tell you that those cartoon you watched as children of clever mice are not fiction. I though they were, but mice are amazingly smart creatures. I laid trap between rooms to contain. Containment is key. This is not a quick fix unless maybe you have very few mice, and then maybe. Mine was a WAR! I used a security system I installed using software that could capture movement and trigger an alert on the system which made it easy to look back at any movement caught. This was a great tool which allowed me to see where they were moving and devise a strategy to further refine the battle plan. Know that mice are able to jump, climb, walk sideways on a wall with the smallest of footholds. Some of my video is stunning. I found that in many areas I saw larger mice able to jump over a single width of traps and I had to place them two wide side-by-side in places. Some mice managed to get to the door frame climbing sideways and jump over traps as well. I do not like the system these use for connecting them together. Too much surface is not sticky between each trap. I use a razor to cut off much of the ends and connect the traps with packing tape on the cardboard bottom side. This also makes for easy separation from the rest when one requires removal to save a mouse. Do not underestimate these critters. Extend your lines of defense further than you might think. If you separate mice from water, food, they will get desperate and find a way if they can. We went 7 weeks without a sign of mice. My house is sealed well on the outside and these IMO were surely clever holdouts. We just discovered two in the laundry room just today and the war is back on some 16 weeks after we though the war was over. I was glad I kept a solid two plus wide line of traps at the door. Much wider than the door BTW, by about a trap length further on each side and at least double wide line of traps. The war is back on. I may update with more intel later.
TITLE: Learning Curve - My Tips after a Mouse War - How to Save a Mouse Once Caught

BODY: For our household, we're not fans of the taste of our local tap water, so a good filter isn't a luxury—it's a necessity. This genuine FRIGIDAIRE EPTWFU01 filter is the official tool for the job, and it does that job well, but it comes with a significant trade-off between its cost and its convenience. The Good - The 'Simplicity' Factor: The best thing about this filter is its sheer simplicity. Installation is a breeze: you just push the old one in to pop it out, then click the new one into place. There are no tools, no water lines to shut off, and no guesswork. The refrigerator's control panel automatically tracks the filter's life and gives a clear 'Replace' notification when it's time for a new one. This 'set it and forget it' system is incredibly convenient. The water tastes clean and crisp, which is exactly what you want. The Bad - The 'Cost' Factor: The main drawback is the price. These OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) filters are significantly more expensive than other filtration methods, like an external inline filter that you can splice into the refrigerator's water supply line. For the cost of one of these, you could often buy a year's supply of generic inline filters. It feels steep for a disposable item you have to replace every six months. Conclusion: Ultimately, you're paying a premium for a perfectly integrated and hassle-free system. While I've noticed that Amazon consistently has some of the most competitive prices for these genuine filters, the cost is still high. If you are someone who values absolute simplicity and is willing to pay for the convenience of a 30-second, tool-free replacement, then this is the right and only choice. However, if you're more budget-conscious and comfortable with a little DIY, you might find an external inline filter to be a more economical solution
TITLE: The Price of Convenience, and It's Worth It... Mostly

BODY: We love our Shark Navigator Cordless so much we have purchased three of them over the last ten years. It is one of the few cordless vacuum's out there that doesn't have the dirt bin in the handle. For those that have the dirt bin up by the handle, it added just enough extra weight to the handle to be uncomfortable for people with arthritis or other problems with their hands. It works great on either floor or carpet, and the bin is very easy to remove and clean out. We have pets that shed and we run this almost every day to pick up pet hair. It does a great job. The only two things we would have a complaint about would be the longevity, as the motor seems to give out after three or fours years of constant usage (hence owning three), and the fact there is no light mounted on the front to see the floor. You would be amazed at how much you can see on the floor with a vacuum light versus just having the room light on. We have tried other cordless vacs and for us this one is hands down the best.
TITLE: Lightweight and Easy to Maneuver

BODY: First of all, I'm happy to have this because our ice situation was dire. This has helped a lot, but I do have some criticisms about the machine. First, once the ice is made it does not stay frozen long term, it will melt if you just leave it sitting there. Due to the machine not being able to keep a consistent temperature once ice is made, you'll have a more moist ice mix, so if you put it in a container for your freezer, that stuff is going to stick together and you'll need to use an ice pick to break it apart. That being said, it is a good machine. It makes ice quickly, and would be good for a party or get together where you are frequently adding ice to drinks. I've been using a countertop reverse osmosis machine for the water so there isn't any residue build up or anything, and cleaning the machine hasn't been an issue. Overall, I'm happy. It has shortcomings, but it also gets the job done for what I need. It could certainly be better but I feel like this is pretty good value.
TITLE: Good but not great

BODY: I have a nice old school stereo microscope for doing SMD work, but I wanted something digital that would not only have a large screen for inspection, but give me the ability to snap photos for documentation purposes. For that first application -- inspection, this microscope does a respectable job. The onboard lighting is pretty good, and the image on the display is clear enough to get the job done. It provides a nice view for double-checking your solder joints, or quickly checking the part numbers on SMD components. Unfortunately, I've found that the images it takes look far worse than what is shown on the screen. Perhaps it's just because the relatively low resolution of the device's display hides the imperfections which are apparent when the images are viewed on the computer. Though it's not a deal breaker for me, I also had a terrible time trying to use the microscope over USB. In theory it should just show up as a standard USB video device (like a webcam). But while it was detected as such on my Linux machine, the video I got looked horrendous and ran at just a few frames per second. Admittedly though this might just be an issue under Linux, as I didn't test on any other OS. Easiest solution was to just use an SD card. On the plus side, I was surprised to find that the device has an internal battery and doesn't need to be connected to USB power to function. I'm sure it's mentioned somewhere on the product page, but I must have missed it. I fully expected the device to be tethered to the wall while using it, so the discovery that I could turn it on right out of the box was a welcome bonus. While I'm disappointed in the saved image quality, it's honestly hard to complain much at this price (~$35 in 2024). In truth, the device outperforms my expectations given the cost -- part of me expected this to be little more than a toy. I might need to look elsewhere for snapping high quality images, but I have no doubt that this device will still remain useful for me.
TITLE: Decent Live View, Underwhelming Photos

BODY: I absolutely love the Native Coconut & Vanilla deodorant, and this is my second time ordering it! The scent is amazing it smells exactly like the name, sweet and delightful. It glides on so easily and smoothly. This smooth application def. has to be because of the ingredients like coconut oil and shea butter. I work out a lot, and it definitely covers the smell effectively, even during intense sessions. It works so well at blocking out odor, which is a huge plus for me. However, it's IMPORTANT to know that Native is aluminum-free, which means it's a deodorant, not an antiperspirant. Antiperspirants use aluminum to block sweat, but since this one doesn't it won't stop you from sweating! I sweat a lot, and because it doesn't block that, I do find that the white deodorant can sometimes slide down my arms, especially during excessive sweating. The good news is even with the sweat, there's no odor! Like many deodorants, it will leave white streaks on your clothes, so if you're putting on a colored shirt, be aware of that. Overall, if you prioritize amazing scent and excellent odor protection in a natural this is a great choice. Just be prepared for sweat to be running down your arms if you're a heavy sweater like me!
TITLE: Amazing Scent & Odor Protection, But Be Mindful of Sweat

BODY: *SUMMARY: IF YOU HAVE LONG-HAIR DOGS ITS TRULY TANGLE FREE! WORKS WELL ON THICK RUGS BUT FOR FULL PRICE THE SUCTION IS TOO WEAK* Let me start by saying I have a husky who sheds A LOT. I have to sweep my house twice a day just to keep up with her hair shedding, if not it’s in piles around the edges of the house. Every vacuum that claims to be “tangle free” or “made for pets” are crap and hair gets tangled with every use. I was very skeptical this vacuum was actually tangle free (bc it does have bristles! Not just fins like the photos show) but to my surprise- NOT ONE HAIR has gotten tangled in the roller after weeks of use!! (See pictures) Got this vacuum for like 40% off during Black Friday deals. I think it was absolutely worth what I paid- but almost $300 full price is a bit much in my opinion bc it’s not that strong. Ive had much stronger for $150. But for what I paid, I’m very happy with the quality of the product, how lightweight it is, ease of use, battery life, & most importantly that it’s truly tangle free w/ pet hair. That alone makes it worth it for me. ** Things to know: 1. Truly is TANGLE FREE for pet hair (yay!!) only part that gets hairs stuck are the tiny wheels on the corners (see pic) but it doesn’t affect the functionality of the vacuum whatsoever. 2. Its very lightweight- I am 9 months pregnant & have problems with my blood pressure going up w/ little activity & have to be careful. I can easily pick this up & vacuum my couch with it or my rugs w/ no problem. 3. It’s LOUD - low setting isn’t too bad, but high setting needed for carpet/rugs is obnoxious & sounds like a leaf blower. For what I paid, doesn’t bother me. 4. It’s very top heavy & cannot stand on its own - but that should be expected with any brand of vacuum that is this lightweight cordless style. It can lean against the couch or wall (see pic) just fine. 5. Too weak with attachments- I wanted to use this to vacuum my car but the low setting or even high are too weak with the attachments & do a very poor job getting crumbs, dirt & pet hair out of thin vehicle carpet. It got the majority of dirt, but the pet hair & crumbs in the crevices couldn’t be sucked up because the suction is so weak. But when it comes to vacuuming crumbs & hair off my couch, it’s so lightweight I just pick up the vacuum and use it on the couch as if it’s the floor and the rollers get everything up no problem. Won’t be using the attachments much though. 5. Actually does well with thick carpet/rugs - my sons nursery has a very thick shag rug (see pic) that’s almost 3” thick. The vacuum is not anywhere strong enough to pull itself forward on the carpet, but if lean it back a little and use just a little muscle it glides easily across the thick rug (Again I’m 9 mo preggo and I can do this no problem). The rollers get deep into the rug and get about every trace of dirt and hair. 6. Rechargeable battery is nice & imo gives plenty of run time- I get about 40 minutes of use in my mostly hardwood home, during which I have it on high for almost half the time vacuuming thick rugs and my couch.
TITLE: Truly tangle free, good w/ thick rugs

BODY: First the positive info: The variety of colors in this pack are awesome. We had some really beautiful shirts come out of our group activity. Everybody was please with the colors. Also, I don’t think tie-dye can get much easier than using squirt bottles to apply the color. Very easy to use for a group of kids ranging from 6th to 12 grade. The only problem was estimating how much I would need for a group. This was my first year as the craft organizer at a youth group camp. I knew tie-dye shirts are a staple activity that the students LOVE to do. Tulip brand one-step dye was used in the past so I just decided to go with it this year too. I did a lot of research before hand to make good use of our funds and still have enough for all the kids. I decided to go with these bundles because they were $15.85 each at the time I purchased. I was a little bit surprised by how small the bottles were, though, and I was nervous upon seeing them that we woudn’t have enough. I read several questions regarding the Tulip dyes and it seemed like one bottle per person on average was recommended. I can tell you that is NOT the case for the bottles in this pack. Between five packs I had 90 bottles. We were only able to do 66 shirts and on those last shirts we were literally going through every bottle to use every last drop of dye we could. We had to turn a few kids away because we didn’t have enough dye. Now I understand the situation was a result more of my planning and not because of the product, however, I did find it hard to figure out how much I needed ahead of time (even with all the research I did). I would say for this pack plan on at least an average of 1.5 bottles per shirt. So if you bought one kit, you might get twelve adult size sm-large shirts done. You might even want to be safe and plan on only 10 shirts out of this pack. I hope that helps anyone in the future.
TITLE: Great Variety of Colors... Bottles are Small. Make Sure to Buy Enough for Your Group.

BODY: This is a fun game that can be played by a minimum of three to any number of people and is easy to play. The active player rolls the three dice and based on the L, R, dot or C that turns up on each of the dice the player would pass their chip to the left, right, center (pot) or in the case of a dot holds on to their chip. After your turn the player to your left then rolls the dice and has the same option, if you're lucky they have to pass a chip, two or three to you if they roll a single or multiple R's (right). The active player only rolls the number of dice equal to the number of chips they hold, but no more than three dice. If you are out of chips, you don't get to roll. You are back in the game if a player on either side of you rolls and must pass a chip your way. The game continues until all of the chips are in the center pot except for a single player holding chips, that last player does not roll and wins the center pot. I am disappointed in the quality of the chips, but the game is still fun to play, and you could substitute quarters or any markers for the chips if you prefer. The game box is very small and would be easy to pack for traveling.
TITLE: Fun game but poor quality chips

First off, huggies are my preferred brand of diapers for my little one because it was the only diaper that didn't irritate her skin or give her rashes. They are a bit costly though. However, I don't really see any major differences between this and the original huggies. They both feel soft to the touch and are quite absorbant. They are odorless and the only minor difference I noticed is the quilted pattern on the inside of this one and how thin they are compared to the original huggies. Also, they are slightly bigger than the original. I can't exactly determine if there is a difference in the fabric material used as they feel the same. But here are my take away. Pros: Highly absorbant. Holds a good amount of urine for a few hours without leaking or irritating my little one's skin. No leakage even for those runny stools or blow outs. The disappearing snow flakes indicates when the diaper is wet and full for easy and convenient timely changes. The material is thinner than the original pull ups. Sort of like the always ultra thin pads. Doesn't feel stuffed up compared to the original and has a diamond quilted pattern inside the inner back most likely to prevent leakage. The Disney Frozen character Elsa and snowflake designs are very cute and detailed. Perfect for all the little princesses out there. My little one loves it. The material used for fastening the sides has a good amount of stretch to it and it feels very silky and soft. My little one always seems comfortable while wearing them. Cons: Like any pull ups, when it gets too full it starts to feel cold and soaking wet to the touch. It's best to never allow it to get too full because the wetness can seep through clothing. BODY: To never be one of those people that bought an appliance that only makes one thing... Like those sandwich maker/toasters for example.... Now I officially have 2 items that were only made for one thing.... The first was a cutter/crimper set for my kids to use to make their own Uncrustables, which has been used daily since I got it for everything from PB&J, to meat/cheese, to tuna salad, to pimento cheese, to even cucumber sandwiches... They have gone sandwich crazy! Then I found this little item while looking for something to let my son cook an egg or 2 in the microwave, and in the midst of all the little plastic and ceramic cups with lids for the microwave I found these egg cookers. The red one was on sale for a lower price than the other colors and as our kitchen is detailed in red I took that as a nudge that we should try it, after reading through countless reviews first! I do want to say that someone on here posted that it didn't work with farm fresh eggs, that's not true! It works just fine! I think their problem was because they punctured their eggs at the top and not at the bottom where the air sack is like the instructions say to do. Make sure you are putting the little hole on the bigger end, the bottom, of the egg. It worked perfectly for us with eggs that were just collected over the past day or 2. We have only had this little thing not even a whole day, it got here this morning and already my kids have made everything from hard boiled, to poached, to "scrambled". My son used the omelette setting and try to make scrambled eggs by only using the fork to break up the yolks and stuff a little, then season and cook, then use his fork to break them up and fluff them a little on his plate. It worked great! They had eggs for lunch and supper today just because they like that they can to it themselves. They are 12, and I did monitor them while they did. We already go through about 20 eggs a week, now I worry that I won't be able to keep them supplied with enough at all! I made myself a soft boiled egg on toast for supper and it was excellent! Its the only photo I have to upload as I didn't think about it when the kids ate. All I added was a little Louisiana Hot Sauce sprinkled over the egg and buttered toast. I didn't mess with anything else as I was exhausted from working out in the yard today. Now I am catching myself not only thinking about get these as gifts for a couple of people I know that work a lot and don't have a lot of time for cooking, but also looking into some of the other little items Dash makes! I rarely mess with steaming rice as I am the only one that eats it, but the size of the one by Dash has me thinking. I absolutely LOVE the size of this egg cooker! Its simple, small, and I have a whole, almost empty top shelf in my kitchen that I could keep 2-3 little things like this easily up there with my big ol crock pot. I am very glad I decided to get this thing, so are my kids! We are always looking for things they can make for themselves without resorting to frozen tv dinner type stuff for when I am working late. My 19yr old daughter can make a few things, but not many, and the boys like making stuff themselves. If you are on the fence about this you should go for it! Everything we have made so far has come out cooked as wanted. I may pick up an extra one to take to the office for people. I think it would be a big hit!
TITLE: I always swore....

I bought XL because I typically wear large and wanted to be safe incase these shrunk too. The XL fit me perfectly (think if they made a hybrid of large and XL, not too big not too tight). Now almost 1 year later they are pretty much the exact same size and very limited fading after washing 50+ times. Also, I live in a super hot climate and these are more comfortable and cool than the typical moisture shocking material (like Under Armor). The material is thick, won't stretch easily, and shockingly wrinkly resistant. All in all is highly recommend these shirts to anyone without hesitation!

BODY: Perfecto para mi bebé y contenta con mi selección. Lo compré ya que mi bebé es más grande del promedio para 8 meses. Me preocupaba no conseguir un coche/carrito que le permitiese recostarse. Este stroller le permite recostarse perfectamente y tiene 4 posiciones diferentes de inclinación. El asiento es muy amplio dando oportunidad a ser utilizado con niños de más edad. El espacio de la canasta es bastante amplio. (Cabe un envase de galón de agua perfectamente, pero si le vas a inclinar completamente hay que removerlo). El bolsillo que tiene arriba cabe el telefono y hasta una botella de agua o si tienes que guardar tu comida en caso de apuro es perfecto XD. La movilidad es magnífica, el espacio que ocupa es mínimo (puedes pasar con comodidad por las puertas de las oficinas médicas). El portavasos se puede poner arriba a la altura del adulto y también abajo a la altura del infante para facilitarles beber si son más grandes. El parasol le cubre pero no lo suficiente. Lo bueno es que tiene un "lock" que evita que se retraiga con el viento. Y también puedes aprovecharlo para atarle la sabanita en ellos para que no toque el suelo. Lo único malo son las almohadillas que son muy cortas y son cocidas (no son de velcro) impidiendo removerlas a menos que se corten. Tuve que usar las almohadillas del anterior poniendolas por encima de las otras para que le cubra bien el área del cuello y no se raspe con el arnes. El envío lo hacen desde Hong Kong, esperan hasta el último momento de la fecha de entrega para enviarlo por servicio de UPS. Por si no pueden recibir correspondencia con UPS (como a mi) tendrás que estar preparado a ir a la oficina de UPS a buscarlo o estar al tanto de poner una dirección donde si puedan recibir mediante ese servicio. Es super fácil de montar y de cerrar.
TITLE: El stroller perfecto

BODY: I heard about this from some members of my fragrance group. Their glowing comments made me order it to see for myself, and I was NOT disappointed. The day it arrived, I had a dental appointment, so I put a bit on each wrist, a bit at the base of my throat and some under the hair at the base of my neck as advised. It Is POTENT. POOOOOOOTENT. My dentist said he smelled me as soon as I walked in the front door from his office in the back of the clinic. Every single front office girl there said they loved it, immediately. When I was brought back for my appointment, my dentist and his hygeinist both told me how good I smelled and asked what it was. They said I smelled like a super chocolatey, frosted cupcake. My partner, who usually dislikes any scent other than "au naturel" said YUMMYYYY as soon as I applied it, and told me he could just eat me up, because I smell like pudding to him. And then, we went to my parents' house, where they are also both kind of sensitive to fragrances to the point that they dont wear them. (I know, I KNOW... how did I wind up surrounded by a whole bunch of people who dont appreciate the wonder of fragrance and its ability to transport us to new places and affect new moods?!) So anyway, both of my parents said I smelled delicious and wanted to know what it was, so I told them. Next thing I know, my mom texted me to say that she ordered an oil (attar) roll-on for herself! Five days later, she messaged me to say that I wasnt wrong about this Choco Musk. ☺️ I guess she's been racking up the compliments too. The thing to remember is that these oils are created in an extremely hot part of the world, and they are made to bloom (perform well) in heat. So, living in the desert as we do, these Al-Rehab oils are performing their magic and winning over perfect strangers wherever we go. I am now a lifelong Arabian fragrance lover and it's all due to Choco Musk. Since the day it arrived in my mail box I have gone on to buy 20 other attars by various Arabian houses and I havent been disappointed in a single one yet. Guess Im addicted! If that's wrong, I don't wanna be right. Oh, and two weeks after that first dental appointment, I did laundry and the shirt I was wearing at that dental appointment was in the load of clothes. Guess what? Two WEEKS after I applied choco musk while wearing it, it still smelled of it EXTREMELY strongly. This stuff is legit and it doesn't quit. It truly lasts a very long time on my skin, but its nuclear longevity on clothing. Ok, last thing, I promise. One night I put a dot on each inner wrist before bed. Its such a comforting, warm, soothing scent that it helps me drift off to sleep. After I woke up, my partner took a nap before work. When he woke up, he asked me if I had applied choco musk to my pillow and I said, "No, why?" It turns out that it had transferred from that tiny dot on my wrist to our sheets and he could smell it. He said it smelled so good all night long and he really liked it, and that he, who has never liked any fragrance, is willing to wear it too! You could have knocked me over with a feather. I was so excited I put some on him and it lasted all through his work shift and smelled GREAT. Dont miss out on this awesome deal. Just make sure to leave some for me!
TITLE: Nonstop Compliments Wearing This Attar!

BODY: This thing is amazing. I can't believe I hesitated to buy this product. Here is my story: This past Sunday I woke up to my 2014 BMW X5's flat battery. There was a cold snap and within a couple of days my battery was dead. The last time it was functional was Friday night. I called my auto shop on Monday and due to pretty much everyone in the area having dead batteries due to the cold snap, I couldn't get an appointment for a week. I didn't know if I should jump it, or call roadside assistance and drive it around to make sure the battery was fine before next Monday. Instead, I took a chance on this wonderful device. It came yesterday and I was already impressed with just the packaging. It's minimal, sturdy, and accurate. Straight to the point packaging. I opened it up, and started charging the remaining battery. Apparently, it comes 75% charged and you have to do the rest. I used a USB-C cable from my computer and charged it full within minutes. By the way, the USB slots on this device are USB-C. I've seen a few reviews say that it's MicroUSB. The one I purchased is USB-C which was another plus since you can use this to charge your phone. Fast forward to this morning. I let my car sit for another day, which I thought may have been a mistake. I went down to try to use this device to re-awaken my beast... his name is Benny. Benny the Beemer. Anyway... I attached the positive and negative clamps to the posts under my hood. For most BMW owners, those posts are connected directly to the battery which (in)conveniently is in the trunk behind a electrical trunk latch. BMW did a solid adding these posts under the hood so you didn't have to mess with the trunk release. I turned the device on and the light appeared, but the LED's for Boost weren't "chasing" as the manual says. I figured my battery was completely deceased and I would have to call for RA or a tow. I checked the manual again and I missed the spot called "Manual Override". Now, as a sci-fi geek, this intrigued me greatly. Manual Override you say? Yes, if your car has a completely flat battery, so flat that the device cannot detect that it's even connected to a battery, it will not function within the safety limits. Meaning the device doesn't know it's connected to a battery so it will not provide the 2A charge for safety reasons. That's what the Manual Override button is for. I love it. As soon as I pushed that button, the car came to life. It was like Frankenstein's monster. Everything negative turned positive (NPI). See, the Manual Override sends the 2A charge without verifying it's connected to a battery. This lifts the dead battery to at least 2A so you can try and start the car. The car came to life, the alarm started blaring so I jumped into the drivers seat, said a split second prayer to the car gods, and VROOOM! Benny was alive. This device is amazing. I will be buying one for everyone in my family. Everyone should have this device in their car. If it can bring back my 2.5T 11 year old 3.0L twin turbo beast, it can bring backs yours as well. This device can handle anything up to 6.0L for gas and 3.0L for diesel. Buy this now, especially if you live in colder climates.
TITLE: One of my best purchases from Amazon

BODY: I bought the anezus Self Healing Sewing Mat, and let me tell you, it turned out to be an absolute gem for my clay crafting endeavors! Versatility Beyond Sewing: While this mat is primarily designed for sewing purposes, I discovered that it worked wonders for my clay projects as well. Its versatility impressed me right from the start. Ideal Size for Clay Work: The 12-inch x 18-inch size was perfect for my clay crafting needs. It provided ample space to roll and shape the clay, and the measurements on the mat were incredibly handy for precise cuts and sizing. Self-Healing Magic: The self-healing feature is an absolute game-changer! No matter how much I worked on the mat or made cuts, it seemed to bounce back without any visible damage. This meant I could use it over and over again without worrying about wear and tear. Non-Slip Surface: The mat's non-slip surface was an unexpected blessing. It kept my clay securely in place as I kneaded and molded, ensuring greater control and accuracy in my designs. Clear Markings and Gridlines: The clear markings and gridlines on the mat were a lifesaver for my clay projects. They made it super easy to measure and cut with precision, resulting in neat and professional-looking creations. Easy to Clean: Cleaning the mat was a breeze. Any residue or clay bits wiped off effortlessly, leaving the mat looking as good as new. Durable Build: The mat felt solid and durable, reassuring me that it would withstand frequent use for a long time. Final Verdict: In conclusion, the anezus Self Healing Sewing Mat has become an essential tool in my clay crafting arsenal. It exceeded my expectations and proved to be a perfect match for my clay projects. The self-healing feature, non-slip surface, and clear markings made my crafting sessions more enjoyable and productive. If you're a clay enthusiast looking for a reliable and versatile mat, this is the one to get! I highly recommend the anezus Self Healing Sewing Mat, not just for sewing, but for all your clay crafting needs. It's an investment you won't regret
TITLE: The Perfect Companion for Clay Crafting

BODY: Well I (sorta) did what's NOT recommended, and it worked for me. I'm NOT recommending anyone else do this, just relating my own experience in case it's of use to someone else. Three important caveats: 1) I used this NOT to "remove a scratch" but rather to remove the rest of an old anti-glare coating that was flaking off, 2) I did NOT do this on the glasses I wear all the time but rather to my emergency-backup pair that I ony use for a few days every few years, and 3) I did NOT use these glasses as my "corrective lenses" for driving. My old glasses were originally made in 2005. The polycarbonate lenses were still usable (no scratches, only a couple of nicks), but the anti-glare coating had half flaked off which made the image through the lenses rather fuzzy. (From my reading, problems with old anti-glare coatings flaking off from glasses made in that time-frame are quite common.) Note Armour-Etch would certainly WRECK glass lenses rather than polycarbonate lenses; glass lenses are very uncommon these days ...but they do exist. The warnings on the bottle sounded pretty serious, so I: got absolutely everything I could imagine ever needing (including a timer that had seconds) out and ready at hand; put on the goggles from my shop; arranged to work in my _empty_ stainless steel kitchen sink _below_ the level of the counter; and made sure I wouldn't need to scratch an itch anywhere (especially not rub my eyes) no matter what. (I probably should have worn "rubber gloves" too, but I didn't have any; I got by being ULTRA careful.) I put the jar of Armour-Etch in the sink, opened it, and stirred it slowly (from bottom to top like a can of paint) with a Q-Tip. Then I put a liberal coating of it onto both sides of the lenses (both sides had the anti-glare coating) using that same Q-Tip, which took something like 25 seconds. Then I started my timer for another 45 seconds, and immediately proceeded to put the lid back on the Armour-Etch and carefully throw the Q-Tip in my trash can (as usual lined with a plastic garbage bag). When the timer went off, I turned the water faucet on and rinsed _everything_ _liberally_ for a couple minutes (a very long time). I got all the visible Armour-Etch off the lense in the first 15 seconds or so, but just kept rinsing and rinsing. (I found it a little tricky to grasp the glasses firmly without touching any Armour-Etch. The one thing I'd do differently if I ever do this again is use a pair of pliers.) Finally I cleaned the lenses the usual way by washing them with liquid dish soap and rinsing them several times then drying them with a microfiber cloth. The lenses are clear and steady now. My emergency-backup glasses are now reasonably usable. (Note though it's possible to damage -or even destroy- your glasses [maybe even your eyesight] this way, and this off-label use is NOT recommended.)
TITLE: \"don't do this at home...\"

BODY: Aug 12 2015 Update: Had an issue with the motor & had to call cust service. Problem was handled very quickly & efficiently! Thanks! Orig REview: I researched until I was blue in the face because I do NOT like to sweep & I didn't want to buy a problem or a disappointment. Sweeping is a pain & a chore. But to my surprise every time I use this Shark it brings a SMILE! :) Wasn't sure what t to expect with this Shark,...there were some mixed reviews & opinions were helpful. Since I own several Dyson's including a Dyson 44 (I'm not happy with the D-44 or small storage of the Black & Decker Platinum 20v hand vac) I looked at buying the dyson59. Glad I chose this Shark,...though nothing is Perfect...& this Shark is not designed for all situations. PERFORMANCE: I don't try to sweep a large house,..its just not designed for that. It's great for quick, effective clean ups but has flexibility to do larger areas too. Shark give me flexibility to keep my full-size Dyson in the closet much much longer between major cleaning. That's nice. With a cord I have no batteries to run down unexpectedly. Nice. Yes I do have to find an outlet to plug it in, but it's got a very long cord which minimizes that minor annoyance. -The dust /dirt collection cup is much biGGer than the Dyson's I've tried & much easier to dump without a mess. Plus the dirt cup is easily detachable with one hand,..very convenient, well thought out,...a major plus. -The snap connections on the shaft of my portable Dyson are so loose goosey it's like trying to sweep with a droopy wet noodle & leaks suction. Not fun. -But the Rocket is ROCK SOLID! The locking couplers on the shaft are very solid w/no loss of suction. No wet noodle feeling at all. It's a lightweight metal of some sort more durable than plastic. But I resisted buying Shark for a long time because of the huckster feel to their advertising that made me resist buying. Shark's First Class Quality Engineering surprised me. OTHER PLUSSES: * * *NEW UPDATE.... When I need to store the unit I don't use the wall hook. Instead of mounting a plastic hook somewhere to hang the Shark, I lean the Sharp upright against the wall as one unit, nothing detached,....with the bottom vac head close to the wall. It's quick, easy, nothing to disassemble, no mounting hooks involved, convent storage anywhere while minimizing storage space. -Lean Storage works for me because of two small, strategically placed rubberized feet /knobs on the underside of the dust cup that helps grab the wall so it doesn't slide down. It also creates a 3-point stability between the 2 rubberized knobs & the sweeper head flat on the floor. In this 3-point position the Shark rests nicely balanced /stabilized with minimal storage space yet ready to conveniently swing into action quickly. -smartHE (smart Handy Engineering). The placement of rubber knobs is one example of what I call smartHE (Smart Handy Engineering) or smart, handy, practical engineering features that enhance Sharks performance, reduce stress & make it flat out easier /even fun to use. These small rubber knobs do double duty cushioning /stabilizing the plastic dirt cup when laying the sweeper flat on the floor. SUGG: would be helpful if Shark could enhance this upright storage feature with some pop-out rubber feet on the sweeper head & larger rubber on the bottom of the dirt bin. The 3-point storage is easy & simply needs a wall /closet etc to lean against. The rubber under the dirt cup keeps everything stable & in place,...automatically keeping itself from walking /rolling away from the wall or falling over sideways. I store it upright like this even on smooth hardwood floors. Very convenient & gives max flexibility to store it most anywhere out of sight, in an quiet corner, etc.. Nice! With a little practice it's easy to catch on to the technique & not have problems of it falling over as some reviewers have mentioned. -Vac performance works great on medium, short carpeting, hardwood floors & tile. Don't have to change height or anything,...just flip a switch that slows the beater brush on smooth surfaces. On deeper pile carpet I go to high speed. I read the directions. Easy Peasy & effective. -***UPDATE: The Sweeper's Headlight was a BIG SURPRISE. The angle & intensity allows me to actually SEE THE DIRT dirt /dust on my medium dark hard wood floors. Nothing in the marketing describes this benefit, but it's a real help to actually see where I've swept & see the dust in areas yet to be swept. NO MORE GUESSING! As a result I haven't really used any other attachment yet. The slower brush speed setting for wood floors attracts dirt & smart handy engineering keeps the spinning roller from damaging the wood floor. Never a danger of that. -Bot the motorized animal attachment for cat hair & misc on bedding. I can immediately see the results in the transparent collection container. Use Shark for steps too, but often too lazy to switch the heads & simply use my normal floor attachment for steps too,... with great success. * * * UPDATE... WHEEL NOISE & HARDWOOD FLOORS: Not an issue for me. The two large wheels have rubberized tread which deaden click clacking over wood /tile floors & superior to all-plastic wheels wheels on most vacs. -The two small spacing rollers on the front of the sweeper head are not rubberized & do make some rolling noise on wood. Some reviews reported the noise was annoying on hard floors but on balance was not an issue for me. * * * NEW TIP: IF YOU HAVE A BAD BACK or trouble bending over you can wind the electric cord without bending. While standing upright with cord in hand, simply let out enough cord to loop it over the lower hook when draw the cord back over the upper hook then let out enough length to loop under the lower hook & repeat. The cord is flexible yet stiff enough to make this work easily. With a little practice you'll catch on easily. Works for me. -Does have a nice LOOOONG cord. But I always come to an end somewhere where an just an extra 5 feet would be nice. It's one of those things where LOOOG is never long enough! An extra 5 feet would be handy if it can be safely done. -Durability & Longevity....only time will tell. But this Shark is starting from higher quality level /features than battery powered units like Dyson44 /59. That higher quality & attention to detail usually translates to durability & longevity. GREATEST STRENGTH: HIGH QUALITY & VERSATILITY. Does not pretend to be a full size vacuum. But delivers Big time features. Shark adapts well to most situations /hardwood /tile /medium & short carpet, under furniture & hard to reach places with excellent heppa filtration that's easy to clean & refuse. No special filters to constantly buy. So it's versatile, self contained & easy to maintain filters with peak performance. It is wrist arm intensive so if you're handicapped in those areas you may want to stay with traditional floor sleepers,,,but can always give it a 30 day test /return. -Usually avail at a lower price than battery powered competitors. With Shark there's no limit on run time,...no expensive batteries to wear out or repurcha$e. Just plug & run. -TOO MUCH: Suction & cleaning performance are excellent. Some sweepers I've used have TOO MUCH suction making it tough to easily move the sweeper across the carpet because it sucks down on the floor /carpet & won't glide. No problem here w/Shark due to smart engineering that manages the suction well & picks up the dirt. With a little care & technique I can sweep even loose throw carpets without it being sucked off the floor into the sweeper head. SUGG: it would be nice if Shark had a safety overload pop-out reset switch to protect the head roller & motor if something gets caught in the roller. CONS & More SUGGS: There aren't many. Plastic wall mounting hook is a little weak. But that's not an issue for me since I use the handy 3-point storage for leaning against a wall in a closet or empty corner out of sight yet close at hand. This makes sweeping quick & effective,...not a big production. An extra 5ft on the cord would be nice. Extra lights on the sweeper head that shine not only to the front, but to the sides for larger viewing area would be nice. Adjust the motor to get rid of the annoying whine. A simple quick wheel lock to keep the unit from rolling away from the wall when storing /leaning it upright. -A larger accessory bag that closes more secure with a couple fabric loops for hanging would be nice. I don't change accessories very often & store them in the handy bag on a hook in my closet. Some reviewers prefer having all attachments on the shaft at all times, but for my taste that reduces portability & performance which is Shark. Besides, I can use my big, bulky sweeper if I want all my attachments at my fingertips. * * * UPDATE: MY CAT ALWAYS JETS out of the room whenever I start a sweeper. To my compete surprise, my cat GRACIE just calmly sat there watching me sweep away! Who knew?! Ha! SHARK IS CAT ENDORSED... :). BOTT LINE: Brings a s-m-i-l-e every time I use it. Pushing my Shark I feel smart, refined & minimal. Now when going back & pushing my full sized vac I feel clunky & out of step. Just sayin. Unexpectedly gives me a smile & lift on what used to be a chore. After owning 3/4 Dysons, I finally chose to break away from all that overpriced hype & poorly fitting cheap plastic. It was hard to convince myself to do that. I know we're just talking about a sweeper here, but the feeling of freedom & discovery was worth it. This shark delivered solutions for all the annoyances that were trapping me with Dyson & other brands. Shark's smartHE (smart Handy Engineering) features are second to none. Superior fit /finish, exceptional cleaning performance, versatility, larger collection cup easily removable, smarter design, usually less expensive... this Shark is a winner in my book. Glad I gave it a try. Our cat Grace is much happier, too. HIGHLY Recommend. EASILY EARNS 5-STARS!
TITLE: BRINGS A S-M-I-L-E Every Time... :) Cat Approved! .....smart Handy Engineering. Updtd

BODY: A bit of a story here... last year I bought a refurb'ed, like new iPhone 13 from Amazon... and returned it (and bought one elsewhere, that was much nicer, and less money). This year - being entirely comfortable with the i13 level specs - I decided to upgrade to an i13 Pro... and - because of last year's experience - went elsewhere first. What I got from them this time was definitely not what I ordered (it was supposed to be "pristine"... what I got was scratched up junk). So I came back to Amazon, and ordered the only "used-like new" offering on the i13 Pro "list" that day. What I got, well, wasn't (i.e., wasn't "used-like new"). Again. Sigh. (I have a ton of experience with buying "used-like new" stuff from Amazon over the years, and I know what to expect... and this wasn't it.) But. What I received was a "refurbished-excellent" i13 Pro (the same category as the i13 I ordered last year... which was again, anything but, back then, lol). The category which is is now listed on the order form. (Why would they change the category? When it wasn't what I ordered? -Dunno.) HOWEVER. ... big "however". It was... almost... perfect: a few tiny scratches around the lightning port is all a minute examination found wrong (the previous owner had mostly used mag-charging I imagine). And... the battery health - while entirely within the spec for Amazon refurbished (i.e., "80% or greater") - was still a disappointment at only 84%. Still. Cosmetic-wise... acceptable. More than acceptable, even. Agreeably nice... obviously: as I kept it. (Note: I wouldn't review it if I hadn't kept it.) Note: For long term use of any iPhone, to avoid battery related issues, Apple recommends replacing the battery when its below 80% battery health... which repair cost from Apple is about $90 or so... iow, not a big deal. Look, I'm not the target demographic for a new Apple phone anyways; especially their Pro. So sending the phone in for a new battery, especially for what I paid for the 1TB version, is a-okay. And figured into the price. But - other than battery health... and those few tiny [mostly unnoticeable] scratches - I paid quite a bit less (saved a couple of hundred over the "other guys" offerings) at Amazon for a truly ultra-clean i13 Pro - with 1TB capacity - which is in otherwise almost perfect condition. And while its minor flaws render it most certainly NOT in the "used-like new" category... the savings over market costs for a 1TB version rendered my objections to the category moot: it was more than good enough as "refurbished-like new" lol. While YMMV, well... after all... in the final analysis... this one is a keeper. Thanks Amazon. Just keep in mind: all refurb's are "luck of the draw". Amazon is great about their return policy. Take a chance. You'll save money... maybe a bunch of money. Final word: recommend.
TITLE: Amazon came through with an almost perfect i13 Pro 1TB
VARIATION: Service Provider: Unlocked Color: Graphite Size: 1TB

BODY: I have used Norton for about 20 years and consider its cybersecurity protection the BEST hands down. I would not use any other. But... their marketing and installation rigors likely cost customers as they are unfair and unethical at best. They sell at reduced cost through 3rd party venders like Amazon and Best Buy but seem offended or amazed when its customers take advantage of the savings as opposed to buying direct from Norton - for about 3 times the vendor price. Norton 360 is truly worth more than what Amazon etal sell it for, but human nature says to take advantage of the best price available. Next, Norton will not sell multiple year subscriptions - really bad marketing. Further, they will not allow customers to buy new subscriptions early and add the term onto an existing subscription term. To make matters worse, they FORCE customers to enroll in their auto renewal program - which no one finds useful. Why: CC charges appear without previous notification, customers cannot take advantage of any sale prices and Norton can raise prices without customers being aware until after the CC has been charged. It is time Norton changes sales/marketing practices to march the quality of their products.
TITLE: NOTON IS THE BEST BUT THEIR MARKETING AND INSTALLATION PROCESS IS VERY POOR!!

BODY: The Gionlion 6000 Clay Beads Bracelet Making Kit is a comprehensive set featuring 24 colors of flat polymer clay beads, charms, and accessories for crafting friendship bracelets and jewelry. Designed for ages 6–12, the kit encourages creativity and fine motor skill development. I tested this product with a group of kids, focusing on usability, variety, and fun factor. Pros: ✅ Wide variety of 24 vibrant bead colors to suit any design preference. ✅ Includes charms, spacers, and strings for diverse bracelet and jewelry creations. ✅ Durable polymer clay beads that are lightweight and easy to handle. ✅ Comes neatly organized in a compartmentalized box for easy access and storage. ✅ Suitable for group activities, making it an excellent gift or party craft kit. Cons: ❌ Some strings included are slightly thin, which may not hold heavier designs well. ❌ Younger children may need assistance with tying knots and handling smaller components. Performance and Usability: This kit provided hours of entertainment, with children enjoying the mix-and-match possibilities for creating unique jewelry pieces. The beads’ size and smooth texture make them easy to string, and the variety of included charms adds an extra element of fun. The organized storage box was particularly helpful in keeping the workspace tidy. While the strings could be more robust, they suffice for lightweight bracelets and necklaces. Comparison: Compared to similar kits, such as those from Beadery or Kid Made Modern, the Gionlion kit stands out for its extensive color selection and inclusion of trendy flat preppy beads. While others may focus on traditional bead shapes, this kit aligns well with current crafting trends. Who Is This For?: This kit is ideal for children aged 6–12 who love arts and crafts, particularly those interested in jewelry making. It’s perfect for parties, playdates, or as a thoughtful gift to spark creativity and enhance crafting skills. Conclusion: The Gionlion 6000 Clay Beads Bracelet Making Kit is a delightful and well-organized set that encourages creativity and self-expression. With its variety of beads and charms, it offers excellent value for young crafters, despite minor issues with the string quality.
TITLE: Vibrant and Fun Bracelet Kit Perfect for Creative Young Crafters

BODY: I got a GIGANTIC pair of binoculars about 20 years ago when I was living in a large high rise apartment with a view for the first time. Always intended to get a tripod adapter (an outrageously expensive little piece of metal), never got around to it. Moved away and shoved the binoculars in a drawer. Figured I'd use them when I went hiking or out on a boat or something. Well, no, because they're too heavy and huge to lug around. Forget that. So I was out at a beach the other day trying to figure out if those were seals or birds we saw way out in the bay and I wished I had a pair of binoculars on me...but still not my giant ones. These came up on Amazon and I decided to give them a try. They're perfect. Tiny when folded down, light, fold out to fit my eyes perfectly and clearly magnify stuff blocks away from my place. Seem pretty well made - the hinge and the focus both worked smoothly and cleanly for me. About the only thing they lack are eye cups to keep stray light from getting in, which my giant binoculars had, but I used them in my brightly lit living room and they worked fine in spite of that. Probably not ideal for astronomical use, but I wasn't planning on stargazing with these little things, anyway. Will definitely tuck these into their little carrying case and bring them with me in my big backpack next time I go hiking along the shore, to the beach or on a boat. Wish I'd bought something like this 20 years ago instead.
TITLE: Nifty

BODY: I recently picked up the Ailun 2Pack Privacy Screen Protector for my iPhone 13, and I couldn’t be more pleased with the purchase. Here’s why: Privacy Protection: The privacy filter works like a charm. If you’re someone who is always on the go and doesn't want others peeking at your screen, this protector is a must-have. The filter keeps your screen visible only to you, which is especially useful when commuting or in public places. No more worrying about people reading your messages or emails over your shoulder. Easy Installation: The installation process was pretty straightforward. The pack comes with everything you need, including cleaning wipes and dust removal stickers. It took me about 5 minutes to apply, and I didn’t experience any bubbles or issues with alignment, which is always a plus. Touch Sensitivity: I was worried that a privacy screen would impact my phone’s responsiveness, but I’m happy to report that the Ailun Privacy Screen Protector doesn’t affect the touch sensitivity or display clarity at all. The screen feels just as smooth as before, and the touch response is quick and accurate. Durability: The screen protector feels durable and doesn’t scratch easily. I’ve had it on for a few weeks now, and it’s holding up well. Plus, it’s a great value for two protectors in the pack—ideal in case one gets damaged over time. Cons: If I had to nitpick, the privacy feature does slightly darken the screen at extreme angles, but this is expected with any privacy filter, and it doesn’t bother me as I typically hold my phone straight on. Conclusion: Overall, I’m really happy with this purchase. The Ailun Privacy Screen Protector provides great protection for my iPhone 13, both in terms of privacy and physical durability. For the price and the added peace of mind, it’s definitely worth it. Highly recommend if you value privacy or just want to keep your screen safe from scratches!
TITLE: Perfect Privacy and Protection for iPhone 13 – Highly Recommended!

BODY: We'd been trying for baby number two for 6 months with no luck. This was especially frustrating given I'd got pregnant straight away with our first. I was worried about my age and wanted some reassurance that I was ovulating, but I'd been put off ovulation predictor kits - this one because of the price and others because it sounded so open to interpretation to have to decide whether the line was dark enough or not to constitute a positive test. But after 6 months with no luck I decided to splash out on the clear blue test thinking was the price really so high to pay to maximise the chance of a baby? I was initially going to buy the standard clear blue ovulation test, but then saw the dual hormone one was on sale for only a couple of pounds extra - as it claimed to identify double the number of fertile days I thought it was worth the extra cost. The test is really easy to use. You insert a test strip into the electronic reader, wait for the right symbol to appear and then either hold it in your urine stream or dip it in a sample you've collected. A few minutes later you get your very clear digital result. I started testing on day 9 of my cycle and got the low fertility result for three days, then on day four of testing I got the flashing high fertility result. I got this for three days in total and then on day 7 of testing I got the peak fertility result. It was almost as exciting as getting a positive pregnancy test!! We made sure we did the deed on all of the 5 fertile days the test identified. Two weeks after I got my peak fertility result I did a pregnancy test and it was positive!! I am so pleased with this ovulation kit. A few points worth noting: It says if your cycle varies in length to use your last cycle to guide you when deciding which day to start testing. My cycle usually varies between 27 and 30 days, but last time was 35 days. So if I'd followed the advice in the pack I wouldn't have started testing until much later and I would have possibly missed my fertile days. My advice is to start testing based on your average cycle, especially if your last cycle was out of the ordinary like mine. You can always buy another pack with extra test strips in if you end up testing too early. I've seen a few criticisms that this test always gives a low fertility result the first time you test. However, when you think about it this is because it's such an intelligent test. Hormone levels vary from woman to woman, and the first test is about seeing what your own personal normal levels are. This is so the test knows what a peak is for you personally. I've also seen a few people criticise it because they got more than two days of the flashing high fertility result, when the pack advises you get two days of these followed by two days of peak fertility. However, the leaflet in the pack is very clear that many women will get more than two days of high fertility results, with 66% getting between 0 and 4 days of high fertility before getting their peak fertility result. It is also clear that about 8% of women will not get a peak fertility result after getting high fertility results for a week or more. So I think the pack is very clear that the results will vary from woman to woman. I've also seen criticism that you can't carry on testing after getting the peak fertility result. But really, what would be the point? The test is clear that it is for detecting the hormone surge that indicates ovulation is likely to be imminent - it does not claim to be able to confirm that ovulation has actually taken place. The only criticism I can think of for this test is that you can't buy replacement strips for future months and carry on using the same holder. This would make further months use much cheaper, but I guess that's why they do it! Thankfully it worked so well for me that I don't need to worry about this!
TITLE: The test is really easy to use

One of the standout features of the Fifine AmpliGame AM8 is its upgrade-able XLR connection, which allows for noise-free operation and seamless integration with other studio setup equipment such as sound cards and mixers. The handy tap-to-mute feature, independent of computer/Macbook programs, ensures privacy during live streaming, and the LED mute reminder eliminates the risk of forgetting to unmute. Me gusta mucho esta nueva presentación de Thriller y como no tengo nada que agregar a las millones de criticas positivas acerca de este álbum les describo como es el ejemplar que compre. Viene en un sobre transparente de plástico, en vez de cartón como generalmente sucede, y con un hoja donde aparecen las letras de las canciones y algunas fotos que supongo aparecían también en la presentación original de los años ochenta. En el lado A aparece Michael recostado sobre su codo con una chaqueta blanca, creo que era la portada original y en el lado B aparece Michael con una chaqueta roja y un grupo de Zombies detrás de el. Espero que disfruten esta edición de Thriller tanto como yo.

BODY: I bought 7 pieces of foam (2x24x72) to be used as temporary bedding inside of tents. Two pieces fit perfectly inside my tents. I bought 1 piece first to check the quality and durability and I was fairly impressed at the look and feel and firmness of the foam. My issue had more to do with the shipping. When I bought the 7 pieces, they were placed inside boxes that eventually busted out. I received two boxes damaged with corners opened up and even the tape falling off of one and the center area exposed. They had stacked all 3 boxes and taped them together. It was rather large and difficult to handle even though it was foam. I decided to return 4 pieces of foam (the two boxes) but realized later that I would have to pay for the shipping back. It wasn't worth the amount to ship back so I went ahead and opened the foam pieces up. One was dirty and another had rough cuts along the edges. I guess that is also why I gave "durability" a 4-star since it didn't survive the trip very well, but for casual laying and sitting, I think it would be fairly durable. I ended up gluing the foam and it created a good seal but it was crusty and hard like a scab. I would just place those ends along the tent walls. Anyway, I then wrote the seller and told him I would just keep the items instead of returning them as I had forgotten to let them know. THEY WROTE ME BACK! I couldn't believe it as I was just letting them know that I would keep the items. They were very kind and courteous and swift in their response. THUS, the 5-star review is mostly for their customer service although the product is good too. The seller I dealt with is "TOP SHELF VALUE INC." They offered me a replacement piece. I was very impressed with their response and I know that I will enjoy their product as well. I would certainly recommend them because if things "aren't right" with your product, I am sure they would work to help you make it "right". And again, what was "Not Right" about my product was the effect of the shipping process and not the product itself.
TITLE: USED FOR CAMPING TRIPS
