BODY: I got the minimum configuration unit--16GB memory and 256GB disk. I'd say the reports are true that circa 2025 this is a great value for a laptop, coming from Apple (though the prices for extending the memory and disk are pretty outrageous for how little of a bump you get, hence why I didn't upgrade.) It's the first time I've had a laptop that can do all-day battery life...how much it uses depends on what you're doing, but it's in a new league and I really can carry it without the charger. The slab design isn't particularly exciting and it's heavier than I expected for the size--I wouldn't describe it as "air" having handled the likes of LG Grams. But in the bargain you get something that feels very sturdy. I think the camera notch at the top is obnoxious and an extremely dated concept, but one gets used to it eventually. Performance-wise it seems to breeze along just fine, and at most tasks (outside of running x86 virtual machines) can beat a nearly-4-year-old ThinkPad P17 high-end workstation laptop with 64GB of RAM. If you do leave it grinding on a task, it can get hot enough that you can even feel it on the keys...which to me is more of an issue than that it throttles and runs tasks more slowly. But I don't use this as a main workstation, it's just a device for "when I need to do an apple thing" (e.g. create builds of computer code to test that they work on Macintosh). I'm happy enough with it--and feel it was a better choice to get this than a M4 mini. I considered the mini, but the convenience of being able to use and administrate it with an attached screen and keyboard just made it worth paying extra to have a little portable device I could apply to more scenarios. Though given that a lot of my work is in Linux Virtual Machines and using lots of memory, it won't be my my daily driver...and even on tasks when the performance can rival/exceed the ThinkPad P17, the keyboard and screen real estate aren't enough for anything but short sessions.
TITLE: Minimum config is an excellent value laptop, mem/disk upgrades pricey, good battery, can get warm
VARIATION: Capacity: 16GB Unified Memory, 256GB SSD Storage Color: Sky Blue Set: Without AppleCare+

BODY: I, too, have had neuropathy, not from Diabetes, but because of 4 herniated discs to my back, since the 90's. The neuropathic pain worsened to the point that I would have, what I called, "wheelchair" days, where I literally had to get around at home in a wheelchair. They've tried me on everything from antidepressants to seizure medicine; the last medication which I took was the Lyrica, which, not only did not help me but also gave me some side effects of confusion and forgetting things. I'm a nurse and I could not risk many mistakes at work with my patients. I'm also a research nurse, (by nature), though I did work as a research nurse for a number of years. I've tried just about every natural/homeopathic and home remedy that is out there but to no avail. Finally, my pain was so severe that while I was at the local nutrition store where I regularly shop, I asked if there was anything new with respect to neuropathy, and the young man asked me if I had ever heard of Lion's Mane mushrooms and how it works on nerves. I came home and researched it, then decided to try it; I ordered the Lion's Mane mushroom in powder form (they didn't have the powdered form at the nutrition store). The first 7 days, I didn't think that the pain to my feet could get any worse than I had been experiencing, but it did. I was ready to quit taking the mushroom powder, but then, I thought it through, as what I had read about how the mushrooms work to regenerate damaged nerves, so I continued to take them. By day 8, the pain subsided, but knowing what I do know about neuropathy, in that neuropathic pain can be very unpredictable, I started to chart the presence, level, and frequency of pain on my calendar. I started taking the mushroom powder, faithfully, on Feb. 11, and besides the 7 days of increased pain, I have not had any more neuropathic pain. I am back to walking, 2-5 miles with my husband. I do, also have CRPS (Complex Regional Pain Syndrome) from an injury I sustained to my left foot/leg in 2011; I lost 20% use of the peroneal nerve, for which I have a permanent spinal cord stimulator implanted, and also use Lidocaine 5% patches to that foot. I am hopeful that with continued use of the mushrooms, it can regenerate the peroneal nerve so that I will no longer have to use the AFO brace that I have to wear, 24/7. I've also, always, struggled with retaining information and with short-term memory ever since I was given a medication, Stadol many years ago. At one point, back in the '80s, I had a medical workup for MS, because of some of the symptoms I was having. My brain MRI showed that I had amyloid plaques in my brain; I then had to have yearly MRI's to monitor progression or increase in the number of amyloid plaques. Since I've been taking the mushroom powder, I have noticed that my memory is quite improved; I can retain information and I feel that I am more focused and have greater clarity. The "icing on the cake", for me, has been with respect to my bladder issues. I'm 65 years old and had been experiencing issues commonly seen with older women, like not feeling like I was completely emptying my bladder, plus, I was also experiencing some leakage when I'd wait too long to urinate. In fact, I had been seen by a Uro-Gynecologist because of my symptoms and he had scheduled me for a cystoscopy. Because my husband got sick and ended up having to have surgery (it was scheduled on the same day as my cystoscopy), I decided to wait to schedule the cystoscopy until after he recovered from his surgery. So, now that all my symptoms are, I am rethinking whether I still need to reschedule. All of that is completely gone! Gone! I will definitely continue taking the mushrooms and make them a daily part of my health regimen. I'm a missionary nurse and so I'm always sharing helpful information with others. As a person who has suffered from neuropathic pain since the '90s, I share my experience with everyone, especially those who have had neuropathic pain.
TITLE: Neuropathic pain finally gone!

BODY: Got the RING WIRED doorbell, the RING doorchime and Echo Show from Amazon. The Echo Show device uses ALEXA voice control which I find handy for various reminder, To Do and appointment stuff. Very good instructions for installation. installed quickly. They sell this doorbell as a standalone from the full RING security system, but I put in the system as well. You'd think that would be more complicated, but not really. Slick as ice. A few extra steps that weren't a big deal. I feel I should mention that while my existing doorbell transformer was enough to run the new doorbell, and while most people will also have no problem, there are reports by some others who had to upgrade to a new transformer. My first RING doorbell was installed just fine, but my second one will probably need a new one. All 'big box' stores sell them for $20-25 and are easy enough to install by a homeowner, but you might want a handy relative or neighbor who can be bribed with a six-pack to install the transformer if you even need it. Install the doorbell first, and figure out the need for a new transformer second is about the only way to know. I am hoping IF a bad guy/gal sees the distinctive RING doorbell, it ought to discourage them. Got the Chime as well. Lots of chime sounds available, but I liked very few. One chime is if someone presses the doorbell. No surprise there. I assigned a pleasant chime sound to work with it. A SECOND chime sound is when it detects motion. GOD help me, but my wife assigned a locomotive train whistle for that. Installation was easy enough. It helps to know your internet router's name and password. I got Alexa Show with the five inch screen from Amazon as a part of the package deal so I can see what's happening at the front door from my office. When motion is detected, Alexa notifies me and starts the camera's display on the Show monitor. If the TV is on, it displays it on the TV. The screen is quite readible since it is effectively an HD monitor screen. Neat! Because Amazon SHOW is from Amazon, make sure your Amazon password is strong. You won't want too many people hijacking your Amazon account.
TITLE: RING wired doorbell gen 2. should be helpful and a deterrance

BODY: I originally bought this thermostat temperature reading laser gun to keep an eye on what the temperature is going into the supply and coming out of the return because Summer is here and I want to make sure that my air conditioner is running efficiently in the way that it should be and performing my due diligence even though I'm not an HVAC technician I know how to do basic things and I know a little about a lot of things, so I cleaned the coils on my outside unit because they were absolutely filthy after going through the very windy winter months and every year about this time I take off the cover to expose the coils and I gently and carefully with a hose using my thumb as the sprayer clean each layer very slowly and carefully without damaging the coils and removing as much dirt as I possibly can and I usually take about an hour and a half to two hours doing that to make sure that I have cleaned it as good as I possibly can so that way I know it will have no problem sucking in air and it won't be struggling to do so and also checking my filter which I use the cheap filters so that way there are no air restrictions instead of the pleated cloth filters which causes more problems than most people realize, I use the cheap fiberglass filters which do exactly the same thing except they allow for easy air flow which is exactly what you want and then I check my evaporator to make sure that was clean and I just had to clean that off a little bit and maybe the only thing I have it done is taking a vacuum and stuck it inside of each register to clean whatever little bit of junk gets in there every now and then but I'm not too worried about that because there's never really anything in there, I don't have kids so there's no one to throw toys or trash or whatever in there and I also got myself a brand new thermostat because I believe my other one was on the fritz and it was giving me questionable temperature readings and my unit seem to be running longer than it should be and not cutting off when it should be so I did away with the seven day programmable bullcrap thermostat and got a simple single stage non-programmable thermostat with a cool off and heat button and that's that with a big digital display and a bright light up screen in the background and I've had that for about a week so I have been monitoring that quite often on a daily basis as well as making sure the temperatures are where they should be as far as going into the return and coming out of the supply and I have my thermostat set right around 68° and using this thermostat gun after letting my air conditioner run for a good 20 to 25 minutes to get a consistent temperature I then start measuring the temperature of the air coming out of each event which should be anywhere between a 15° and 20° difference than the temperature I have my thermometer set at and right now I have it set at 67° and as you can see by the picture I posted after I just took the temperature coming out of my vent in the kitchen it read 51° which is exactly right around where I want it and I went around to each vent in my house and check the temperature from each one and I have a basement so of course it's going to very because it's cooler in the basement so it rained anywhere from 48° to 52° and it just gives me more confirmation and knowing that my air conditioning unit is running efficiently and working as it should be and these are all simple things that any homeowner can do if they think that they're having any kind of issues, check the easy things first at any homeowner can check before freaking out and calling an AC repairman to come over and spending a whole lot of money that you didn't need to spend which usually happens 9 times out of 10... As far as I can tell this thermostat gun is about as accurate as I would expect it to be, I can't say that it's 100% accurate but I don't think I would be able to say that about any different kind of thermostat, but I tested it on several different things and it is about as close as I needed to be so I would definitely recommend this product
TITLE: As good as can be expected

BODY: I’ve purchased this screen protector four times over the past couple of years for multiple iPhone models, and it’s always been a solid choice. WHAT I LOVE: ✔️ solid, durable feel that holds up well ✔️ Practically invisible—doesn’t affect screen clarity or touch sensitivity ✔️ Comes with a handy install frame (this was my first time using it, and it made placement so much easier!) INSTALL TIPS I’ve always opted for the 3-pack, knowing I’d have backups if (when) I messed one up. This time, I finally got a perfect install on both protectors for both mine and my husband’s phones—no bubbles, no trapped dust! So here’s whar you should know: 🧼 Double-clean your screen: Take two passes with the wet and dry wipes. Even if it looks spotless, tap the dust-lifting sticker over 100% of the screen—don’t trust your eyes! 👀 Final dust check: Hold the screen at an angle in sunlight before applying the protector. A single dust particle can create a bubble that will haunt you. Then install the screen protector ASAP, before anything else can settle. 🔝Open up the protector carefully: know which side is up (it’s easier than you think to lose track if you don’t use the install stickers). A finger print against the sticky side = trash and start over. If you do plan to use the install stickers, test them out BEFORE you take off the plastic. It’s easy for them to get wobbly and drop it. I personally don’t use them. I just gently hold the screen protector by the edges. 📐 Install the cover at an angle: Don’t drop the screen protector it straight down: drop the top edge onto your phone first and slowly lower it down. Ideal install for the cleanest seal is from a corner, lowering down diagonally, but this is tricky, and you can still smooth out successfully if you install top-edge down. 🫧Dealing with bubbles: If you get one WITHOUT a trapped particle, gently lift from a corner until you reach the bubble, then slowly lay it back down and press as you go (but be careful, the screen protector is not intended to be pliable) 🖼️ Using the install frame: Super helpful for alignment! But tap for dust after placing the frame, in case it held onto anything. ⚠️ REMOVAL WARNING Be extra careful when removing a broken protector. I made the mistake of pulling from a cracked edge first, and a shard went under my fingernail—it took weeks for my body to push it out! Now, I always: ☑️ Start peeling from the least broken edge ☑️ Cover the cracked part with a damp paper towel during removal, and wear gloves. ☑️ Remove it directly over a trash can to prevent flying glass Overall, this is a fantastic screen protector, especially if you follow the right steps for installation and removal!
TITLE: Great product: see review for install and removal tips

BODY: I have a lot of cheaper tests I used when trying to conceive, but these are THE BEST if you want to test early. I am not kidding...I got a positive test when I was only 2 weeks 6 days! Of course, this all depends on when you ovulate and when implantation occurs, but I was super impressed with how little hCG was needed for a clear positive. I tested positive on a Friday and called my doctor's office on Monday for a blood test. They could not believe I knew so soon, and were doubtful my pregnancy would be successful because hCG only came back 44. I went back two days later and it tripled to 129! I include these numbers because it means my hCG level was SUPER low when I got my first positive test. I have never received a false positive and have never had an evaporation line appear. To use these I urinate in a cup and then place the test in the urine for 3-5 seconds. I find this more convenient than just urinating on a stick and is much more comfortable. Worth the money if you are trying to conceive and want to know very early on.
TITLE: THE BEST for extremely early results!

BODY: Delta 2 Max Where do I start got this device to test and share my opinion. I have the previous model Delta max 2000. Which has served me well and with zero issues to date. The Delta 2 Max has a lot of similarities to the original Delta Max 2000. But here are few differences to mention to begin the DELTA 2 Max is lifepo4 with a lifecycle of 3500 vs 800 for the original Delta Max. The capacity is little more 2048wh for the new Max 2 vs 2016wh for the original. So now that I have been using it and I have tried most of my appliances for example Airfryer 1800 watts no problem microwave, toaster and coffee pot all without any hiccups. I am happy to say Ecoflow made another solid and reliable power station. Who or what is this unit best suited for. I myself will be using this for camping and for home backup in case an emergency ever arises. This unit fits all case scenarios for most he only thing is the weight maybe an issue for some coming around 50 lbs it is kind of heavy. With that being said this unit will serve multiple roles for many people and scenarios. My final thoughts the Delta 2 Max is quite honestly the only unit most people would need and if more storage is necessary there is the option to add up to 2 extra smart batteries giving most people the ability to run a home conservatively for a day and provide some peace of mind. I am going to recommend this unit for anyone starting out and wanting a home backup or even camping that you won’t go wrong with this unit. So if you are in the market or undecided as to where to start. Begin with the Ecoflow Delta 2 Max as you can always expand from here. I highly recommend this unit. Also if anyone has any questions feel free to reach out to me. I would gladly answer and assist in anyway I can.

After using the YETI Rambler 20 oz Stainless Steel Vacuum Insulated Tumbler with MagSlider Lid for the past few months, I finally get the hype. This isn’t just another stainless steel cup — it’s a well-thought-out, rugged, and practical piece of gear that fits perfectly into daily life. Whether you’re commuting, working at a desk, road-tripping, or just hanging out at home, this tumbler checks every box. Temperature Control That Actually Works Let’s start with what matters most: performance. This thing keeps drinks hot or cold for hours — and I mean hours. I’ve filled it with coffee at 8 AM, forgotten about it until almost noon, and the coffee was still warm enough to drink without reheating. On the flip side, ice water stays cold well into the afternoon, even if it’s sitting in the sun or inside a hot car. YETI’s double-wall vacuum insulation really delivers. I’ve tried cheaper tumblers before, and they just don’t compare. With the Rambler, I never have to worry about my drink turning lukewarm too soon. Durability & Build Quality Like everything YETI makes, the Rambler is overbuilt in the best way. The stainless steel feels thick and solid — no flexing, no dings from regular use. I’ve accidentally dropped it a couple times, and aside from a scuff here and there, it’s held up perfectly. The powder-coated finish (I got mine in navy) is grippy, doesn’t sweat, and still looks brand new even after daily use. You can tell this thing was designed to last years, not months. The MagSlider Lid — Simple But Effective I was a little skeptical of the MagSlider lid at first, but it’s grown on me fast. It doesn’t make the cup leakproof, but it does a great job keeping splashes and spills to a minimum — especially when driving or walking with it in hand. The magnetic slider is smooth and pops off easily for cleaning. It’s one of those small features that actually makes a difference. For anyone wondering, you can buy a totally leakproof lid separately if you need to toss this in a backpack, but for everyday use, the included lid does the job really well. Fits Everywhere This may seem minor, but the size is perfect. The 20 oz Rambler fits in cup holders (car, stroller, office chair — you name it) and doesn’t feel bulky in your hand. It holds just the right amount for a decent coffee or a generous pour of iced tea, without being overkill. Ease of Cleaning Everything comes apart easily for cleaning, and there’s no weird corners or plastic seams that trap gunk. It’s dishwasher-safe, too, which is huge. BODY: I recently purchased the AstroAI Portable Car Air Pump (CZK-3631) and used it for the first time on my car. First, it is very easy to use. After connecting the device to the car's 12V socket, I set the pressure unit to PSI and the desired pressure in accordance with my car's tire pressure specification. I connected the inflation hose to one tire and noticed that the device indicated the tire's pressure was lower than the desired pressure. I turned the device on and waited. The device automatically stopped at the desired pressure, and I turned the switch to the off position. After completing the remaining three tires, I took the car out to drive to see what its tire pressure gauges would show. To my surprise the car's tire pressure gauges proved that the air pump was accurate. Compared to my previous air pump the AstroAI was: quieter; accurate (digital display versus analog); and the metal coupling on the inflation hose is preferable to the plastic encased coupling on my old pump. I believe the plastic casing on my old pump's hose was the reason why it became difficult to attach the hose to the tire's intake valve. Advice: The length of the power cord was perfect for my small hybrid car as it could reach all four tires. Consider purchasing and extension power cord for longer vehicles such as trucks, vans, and large SUVs. I intend to use this pump frequently, at least twice a month. I will see how long this device functions properly and will update this review if any issues are encountered.
TITLE: Review of AstroAI Portable Car Air Pump (CZK-3631)

BODY: This book has fascinated me for decades. The story of a small country town being over run by a physical manifestation of evil sounds both completely implausible and ludicrous (of course) but if you put this concept into the hands of Stephen King and his immense talent, it becomes a whole different story (no pun intended). Mr King's classic (but unofficial) trilogy of horror and the supernatural - CHRISTINE, THE SHINING and now, finally, SALEM'S LOT, are each pinnacles of the genre and yet are clearly unique in their own right. Some similarities do exist within the trio - i will let you find them for yourselves - but essentially they feature tales of battles between light and dark, good and evil, with a combination of both happy and sad outcomes to be found within. Superficially, one could say that THE SHINING is more frightening, as a family finds itself isolated, trapped even, in what turns out to be an aggressively hostile environment. There is literally no where for them to escape to. Conversely, SALEM'S LOT is set in an open town, with roads leading anywhere to freedom. But the genius of this story (well, one of them) is that each house in the town has the potential to become a mini "overlook hotel". Vampires can turn up anywhere. And in Salem's Lot, they do. Just like THE SHINING, this book starts out slowly. We meet the the town folk, we get to know the history of the place, and we consume a little bit of the atmosphere. And then Mr King takes his foot off the brake pedal, and without warning, we feel a trickle of fear crawl slowly up our spine. Turn the page and you have a small knot in your stomach that wasn't there before. And then he will knock the breath out of you and produce the literary highlight of the book so far: For example, taken from part 1, chapter 6, sub-chapter 1: THE LOT (II). "But when fall comes, kicking summer out on its treacherous ass as it always does one day sometime after the mid point of September, it always stays like an old friend that you have missed. It settles in the way an old friend will settle into your favourite chair and take out his favourite pipe and light it and then fill the afternoon with stories of places he has been and hints things he has done since last he saw you." You take a deep breath, turn the page and... "... And the wind begins to blow by the day and it is never still. It hurries you along as you walk the roads, crunching the leaves that have fallen in mad and variegated drifts... ...And if there are no cars or planes, and if no one's Uncle John is out in the wood lot west of town banging away at a quail or pheasant; if the only sound is the slow beat of your own heart, you can hear another sound, and that is the sound of life winding down to its cyclic close, waiting for the first winter snow to perform last rites..." And then back to the horror. The glorious, glorious horror. It's all just a horrifically beautiful tragedy. ********************** Please see below for my original review, which I posted approximately 2 years ago. *blushes* ********************** My all-time favourite book by my all time favourite author. This book should come with a free towel, to catch the atmosphere as it drips slowly from each page. A truly terrifying read which takes the reader so much further than THE HAUNTING OF HILL HOUSE and just about every other horror story ever written. May Stephen King live forever..
TITLE: IT'S JUST SO HORRIFICALLY BEAUTIFUL!!!

BODY: My scope just arrived late yesterday, so other than assembling it and turning it on for a few minutes to see if it worked, I haven't had time to truly evaluate it. What I can say is it's really quite amazing that technology has reached a point that a product like this could be delivered for the price I paid. Is what I see on the screen good enough for it to be useful to me? For now I'll say yes, as I plan to use it on my workbench to do watch repair, so I don't need something that can handle fast moving video scenes. The scope has the ability to show magnified views in three different resolutions: VGA (640), 720p and 1080p. Obviously, the screen isn't very large, but it's sufficient for my use, and the quality of the video picture is reasonably good, especially for the price! The view on screen doesn't lack for enough light, as the scope has a ring of LEDs around the lens and they're bright. There's no shadows in the picture from misaligned lighting elements, and despite just about everything on the scope being made of plastic everything works as it's supposed to. The entire setup is very light-weight, but it's relatively stable in use. The base on mine had a minute amount of "side-to-side" rock to it, which I "took out" by setting the base at the edge of my bench and pressed down a bit on one of the corners, flexing the two pieces of plastic used to make the base. I believe this worked because the two plastic base pieces are most likely "snapped" together, not unlike how so many laptop PC cases snap together. The difference on this scope is there doesn't seem to be any screws in the base at all, while laptops have a large number of screws. So how did flexing the base allow the scope to sit flat? Using a snap-together case with no screws to lock things in place, the two pieces of plastic can move a very small amount against each other when you put some stress on them, like what happened when I allowed one corner of the scope's base to hang-off of my bench and then pushed down on it. The base probably went into the shipping box with a flat base, but by the time it arrived it must have gotten bumped and the base may have slipped a little, leaving the slight rock to it. Regardless, the base is now flat, the scope is stable and unless something changes with it, I expect to get a lot of use out of it on my workbench. Does it have the best magnified picture? I'm sure it doesn't, but honestly, for the price these scopes are going for, I don't think you can beat it. You can pay a lot more on some other scopes and you probably won't get any better than what this little one does. If you want an inexpensive way to look at small things in a much bigger manner, give this Elikliv scope a try!
TITLE: So far, so good.

BODY: I never thought a small speaker could make such a big difference in my daily routine until I got my hands on the Echo Pop. This little powerhouse fits perfectly in my cozy bedroom, yet it delivers impressive sound quality that fills the space beautifully. It’s like having a mini concert hall right at home, and I love how it blends seamlessly with my decor, thanks to its sleek charcoal design. One of the best things about this compact smart speaker is how it effortlessly transformed my space into a smart home hub. Controlling my smart plugs and lights with just my voice has been a delightful convenience. No more fumbling for switches in the dark or worrying if I left something on when I head out. Plus, Alexa’s ability to manage tasks like setting timers, checking the weather, and even reordering essentials is a real lifesaver for someone like me who’s always on the go. The unexpected benefits are what really set the Echo Pop apart. I’ve been having a blast exploring Alexa’s skills, from playing calming sounds to help me unwind after a long day to testing my knowledge with fun music trivia. And for those moments when I just want to kick back with a good book or podcast, Alexa makes it incredibly easy to stream my favorites from all the major platforms, including Spotify and Audible. Overall, I’m thrilled with how the Echo Pop has integrated into my life. It’s not just a speaker; it’s a little assistant that brings ease and enjoyment to everyday moments. The thoughtful design, privacy features, and commitment to sustainability are the cherries on top, making this not just a smart choice but a feel-good one too. If you’re looking for a way to simplify your routine and enjoy quality sound in a compact package, this is definitely the gadget for you.

BODY: Because I do not have time to stop and brush after each meal or snack, my go-to breath freshener was Eclipse winterfresh gum. LOVED this gum!! However, the more I embarked on a healthier lifestyle, the more I did not want the aspartame in my body. I was hooked on my gum, and it was tough letting it go but I needed something healthier. So, I found TheraBreath (and Pur gum), decided to give it a try, and hoped for the best. My only thing, living alone, was how would I really know that it was working? I felt the freshness after using it, for sure. There was something very different about that freshness. But I wasn't sure about it lasting after a coffee, snack, or meal. One tip that I can give: you might get better results if you use after brushing teeth and tongue from eating breakfast. If you skip breakfast, brush, then use it, your body is still in fasting mode and releasing toxins. You may not get the best results. Anyway, my test was to use it before bed. Because most nights I sleep with the sheet over my head, I knew that if I yawned in the middle of the night, or right before rising, I could tell if my breath was still fresh or rank. You get that backdraft, so to speak, b/c the sheet is there to push your breath back in your face, lol. I did different experiments with this. I would always brush teeth and tongue, but some nights I would brush both just once and use the mouthwash. Not the best results. Some nights I would brush teeth and tongue twice and use the mouthwash. Much better results. And on days where I may have had something with mild onion or garlic and only brushed teeth and tongue once, I could still smell the residue. If twice, much better results (I always flossed btw). So, in essence, do not use this as a replacement for oral hygiene. Nevertheless, this is a great product if used in conjunction with proper oral hygiene. I have it now as a bi-monthly subscription because I never want to run out!
TITLE: Gives Me Confidence W/Clients & At Church

BODY: I’ve tried a half-dozen “cheap” screen protectors and always ended up with dust specks or a crooked fit. This Ailun three-pack finally broke the streak. The plastic alignment frame clicks over the phone, so the glass drops into place exactly centered without me hovering and hoping. First try was bubble-free; I pressed along the middle and watched the adhesive chase the air to the edges. Clarity is basically invisible. TrueTone and Face ID work like nothing is there and the edges don’t snag on my Spigen case. Touch response feels unchanged, even when I’m swiping quickly in Call of Duty Mobile. Durability got a real-world test last week when my phone slipped off the stroller cupholder onto rough concrete. The protector cracked cleanly in the corner like a car windshield, but the OLED underneath was flawless. Peeled it off, wiped away the dust, slapped on glass number two in under five minutes. Only quirk: the protector stops a millimeter shy of the curved bezel, which looks odd until you remember it avoids the dreaded “halo” effect with cases. For the price of a gas-station coffee per piece, having two spares in the drawer is great peace of mind. Highly recommend if you own an iPhone 12 or 12 Pro and want easy installation with real drop protection.
TITLE: Install tool makes it fool-proof and the glass actually saved my 12 Pro from a sidewalk face-plant

BODY: I live in a large complex, and have had issues of rodents traveling in between the apartments in my building since I moved in. They travel in between the gas line for the stove. Management has been largely ineffective at eliminating or controlling the rodents. I have an extreme phobia of rodents. A couple of months ago, I saw five mice in my apartment at the same time scurrying across my floor, so I knew that I had to take matters into my own hands. I immediately went on line to search for a solution. Before purchasing this brand, I was not familiar with this brand. I settled on this brand based on the number of positive reviews along with photos of snakes and other critters that were caught using these traps. These glue trapped seemed to be promising based on product reviews and photos. Because the only place they are entering and exiting my apartment is through the gas line to my stove, I decided to create a mega glue trap. I cut the excess paper from the traps and glued 3 traps together. I then proceeded to attach another row of 3, and yet another row of 3, and then I slid the super wide and long trap under my stove. I immediately caught 2. I called maintenance and had them remove the trap with the rodents on them, and glued 9 traps again in the same manner as before. This method has been very effective for me. This is the only way I intend to use this product. I can say for the first time in over ten years, I am no longer terrorized by rodents. Before I took matters into my own hands, they were making nightly appearances almost every night without fail. And, every time I saw them, I would scream at the top of my voice and run. Management was putting down traps similar to these that they assembled into boxes. They were also using the black plastic traps with the bait openings. The mice would just avoid those traps. They were 100% ineffective and I could not get their exterminators to purchase the large rat glue traps. Creating a wide glue trap was more effective for me given how they were entering my apartment. The odds were 100% that when they entered through the gas pipe, I would catch them because my glue trap covered almost the entire surface area under my stove. But in addition to that, these traps have a strong off-putting smell that, I believe, is deterring the mice from entering my apartment through the pipes. They have an over-powering smell that I find to be nauseating. I notice it every time I enter the kitchen. Fewer mice are entering my kitchen, which I believe is due to this smell. These have been 100% effective for me when glued together to create a large glue trap. The only downside for me is that I no longer have access to my stove for baking, and I am an avid cook. Because I have a counter convection oven, I have other baking options. However, I miss cooking in my oven. But for now, this is the best option for me. I am no longer terrorized by the rodents in my apartment.
TITLE: No Longer Terrorized by Mice

BODY: Wow, this stuff really works! We had an infestation of the tiny little black sugar ants 🐜. I didn’t want to call a pricy pest company so we decided to try the Terro Liquid Ant Bait stations. We had to be careful because we have pets so I opened one up and placed it under a cabinet near where we had seen most of the ants. Within a few hours the “Ants were marching 10 by 10” to get under that cabinet to score a taste of the sticky gooey goodness. DO NOT KILL THE ANTS when you see them. They are taking the bait back to their nest to share it with the rest of the ant buddies . Within 2 days we no longer saw any ants at all. This stuff did its job. I left it for 3 more days just in case. When I got ready to dispose of the bait station it was filled with dead ants. The station was stuck to the floor because the ants I assume had spread some of the bait when they were coming out of the trap. I suggest you put something underneath the bait station to prevent it from leaving a sticky mess. Is was easy to clean up so it didn’t hurt anything. It is toxic to pets so hide them where pets can’t get to them. This is definitely worth the money I spent. I highly recommend them for easy fast ant control. This was a ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ experience for our family. No more pesty ants.
TITLE: No More Ants 🐜 roaming our home.

BODY: I am always a bit doubtful that the results of any product will live up to an online demo. In this case, the demo was true-to-life. I applied the gel as directed (be sure to wear gloves), lightly scrubbed the gasket using a soft toothbrush, shut the door, and 4 hours later the mildew was GONE. It wasn’t loosened to be wiped away - THE MILDEW WAS GONE! I used a microfiber cloth to remove the gel that remained, and, as a precautionary measure, ran a cycle to ensure all the gel was cleaned away. Unfortunately I don’t have before and after pictures as I did not anticipate these stellar results. I am so impressed with this product I ordered the washing machine tub cleaner they sell. The price is reasonable, the smell is not overwhelming (it reminds me of a chlorine pool), application and cleanup required a minimum of effort, and the results were FANTASTIC!
TITLE: Better than expected! Front load LG washer gasket is like NEW!

BODY: My previous TV, a flat-screen gifted to me in 2011, already ready to go, died last week. All of my adult life I have made do with hand-me-down or used TVs/monitors. My dead TV was set up in my office so I could watch TV while working on the computer. I fed the TV with a laptop, via HDMI. I had previously poured a giant glass of ice-water on the laptop so the screen doesn't work very well, which made it perfect for feeding the TV. I have large VOD libraries on both Prime Video and Vudu, so when looking at the Smart TV options, access to Vudu is important to me. So I originally decided against this TV because it's an Amazon Fire TV, and my Fire Tablet, (that I use to watch TV/movies in bed), doesn't allow me to put Vudu on it. But when looking at all of the options, this one was the right size, and it does have HDMI inputs, so I could still feed it with my broken laptop, so it was the obvious choice, with the great sale price. I searched the apps and found that Vudu was an option. I also downloaded Peacock, Paramount+, Movies Anywhere, Tubi, Plex, Crackle, MSNBC, The CW, ABC, NBC, CBS, and YouTube. Signing into to each of them on my computer and using codes worked really well. So the only thing I had to actually type my password into using the remote was my Wifi. It all works great, and switching to the HDMI input is easy, if I ever want or need to do that. Like if I ever want to watch something I only have on DVD or Blu Ray. I will add Disney+, Hulu and Netflix when the WGA writer's strike is over and I resubscribe to them. I don't know if my previous TV was HD, but when I've visited my parents when they've played Star Wars, Lord of the Rings and other things in HD on their HD TV and it has all looked gross. The Battle of Helms Deep was a bunch of amorphous clay blobs, and the Death Star was a bunch of dudes in bad costumes hanging out. I don't know if that was the motion smoothing thing that Tom Cruise was mad about a few years back, Either way, I've seen none of that gross stuff. I've watch a little of the new Children of the Corn, several episodes of Stargate Atlantis, a little of Indiana Jones and the Crystal Skull and Jurassic Park, and it all looks good. If I look closely I can see what I'm guessing is filmgrain, but the picture is clear. I recently watched an episode of Snoops (1999) that was uploaded to YouTube from probably VHS. There's a brief scene that features Dick Miller. I posted a screenshot to Twitter, but anyone who looked at that pic has to more or less take my word for it that it is indeed Dick Miller. But on this TV I could make guesses about the shade of lipstick worn by Teyla Emmagan, if I knew anything about lipstick. This is all to let you know my level of knowledge so you can judge how my level of happiness would measure up to your level of happiness. My only other observation in the few hours I've had this, is that the TV stands are at the outer edges of the TV. My previous TV had a big center stand, when meant I could perch it fairly near the edge of the table next to my desk, at a 45 degree angle (ish). So with the stands at the edges, I've had to push the TV farther back, and put sturdy things under the stands to give the TV better purchase on the table. Which is to say, you really need enough table under the entire TV to support it. I saw a review that said their TV didn't come with the screws for the stands. Mine did. They were in a separate small bad, the same way the remote batteries were in a separate bag. So far the TV seems absolutely great.
TITLE: Even Vudu...

BODY: I got two of the Mega Collector's kit because there's a degenerate gambler buried deep inside of me and I've recently been addicted to watching card pack openings on tiktok. Here are my thoughts: Straight off the rip I want to say don't buy these if 1) you expect perfect PSA 10 bulk cards as some of the ones they provided were super beat up (but that was expected on my part) or 2) think you're going to get something amazing to sell later. I don't think that's a viable strategy (although ripping packs in general in hopes to sell them isn't either but we all know we're gonna do it anyway) however if you do want to get back in collecting and don't mind a roll of the dice this was a lot of fun. I got one card of value out of the random packs, an IR Tyranitar. One of the random Holos they gave was a Radiant Geninja, which super jebaited me because the TCGplayer app thought it was the Prize Pack version but even still it's a dope card. The included Trick or Trade packs threw me off a bit as I didn't know they only included 3 cards, but I ended up getting a Mimikyu holo and that's my fav pokemon so idgaf about there only being 3 cards. I also got a super cute Flying Pikachu V from the bulk they provided which will go into my now newly started collection. I honestly will just get rid of the bulk as that's the worst part of collecting imo. If you're planning on buying these as way to get something super high value that will shoot to the moon I would hold off. But if you're like me and just wanted to see what you could get I think these could fun to go through, very nostalgic for me. BODY: The Crossfire 10x42 binoculars are even better than I expected. Definitely "keepers". I doubt there are any better 10x42 brands/models at the same price point. They are sturdy (no shaky eye cups or adjustments). They are brighter in low light than any binocular I've used. We compared them to my son's excellent and much more expensive "Leo'" 10x42 and we were unable to see any difference in clarity or sharpness. The Crossfire close focus distance is about 8' (not 6 as advertised), which is fine. I really like the case and other accessories in the HD bundle.. The only thing I don't like about the Crossfire is its length, which is longer than most 10X42 models (like the Vortex Diamondbacks). But, I wish I'd had the opportunity to do a side-by-side comparison with the Diamondbacks. The binocular neck strap and the Glasspak harness/case cannot be used at the same time. And most of the time I don’t need either when I’m using the binos. I added split rings to the binoculars and 1 1/2” swivel snap clips to the shoulder strap and to the straps on the Vortex case that are used to connect to the binos. Now I can easily connect the strap or the case to the binos without threading individual straps on & off. The swivel snaps and split rings are available for 20 cents (package of 30@ of both) on Amazon. Why didn’t Vortex think of that? 😕 I also connected the eyecup cover to the binos with an elastic strap so the cover is with me even when I’m not using the neck strap or case.
TITLE: Great value-Great product

BODY: I gave this five stars in hopes others could see. I bought these and received just 3 days ago. was excited to try because my bonus mama said her drinking electrolytes was giving her energy. I chose this brand and the first day I drank it and by night I had a awful headache (I rarely get headaches) didn’t think anything of it I thought maybe from something else. The next day (yesterday) I drank it again and by the afternoon my heart was racing so much I was agitated and had to keep taking DEEP breaths cause I couldn’t get rid of a massive anxiety feeling in my chest, this had happened one other time in my life and it was from a trash preworkout I drank! I still didn’t think it was from the drink! My husband hit it on the nose and said “that drink is the only new thing you took!” But it’s electrolytes so I was like no way! 😏 I also had a dull headache yesterday but that high anxiety and irritable feeling was INSANE! So today I once again make this drink in a 60oz water and drink it throughout the day I finished it in the evening and all of a sudden my pee had a weird reddish tinge to it last 4-5 times I went and when I finished my bottle that dull headache came into play again but got stronger (currently still have it) and it took me out for the night and migraine medicine didn’t even help it. I was laying here going to go to bed but thought I had to research this drink more like my husband said and I found in Reddit people saying it gives them massive headaches and takes them out so then I went to the 1 star reviews and BINGO! Symptoms others had were 1. Racing heart, 2. Irritability 3. Massive headache 4. Panic attacks and one even said a trip to the ER and the dr said they had a overload of sodium and electrolytes in their system... I only drank one a day and with 60oz of water. I am bummed I really wanted to like This stuff and never did I think it would put me out like this! So glad I decided to read up on it real quick before bed. I won’t be finishing this pack. Just beware of these things if you choose to buy! EDIT: so I did t want to waste them and my understanding is they were just too strong so I have been drinking half a pack in my water and have been fine so maybe just start with half a pack if you want to but these. BODY: I have been using Magic Root for over 15 years. I have tried others, just to compare, but I always came back to this product. I read some of the negative reviews, and I am not sure if it's because everyone's hair is different or if I use a certain technique that makes it work perfectly for me. I am 58, and have been graying since 25! I use this to extend my color treatments to avoid over processed hair. Here's how I use it: 1. I dry my hair completely. 2. I create parts to target my grays only. 3. I give a very light spray of Amika or Moroccanoil dry shampoo directly into those parts. I feel that this is a good base for which to spray on color and have it last, as well as keep it on my hair and not my scalp, which tends to get oily by day 3! It also creates a nice fragrance for my hair, as the Magic Root does not have any scent. 4. I shake my Magic can for a solid 10 seconds as most liquid products will have various ingredients that settle differently. 5. I learned to hold my hair down to best see the grays, and to spray using brief strokes, holding the can at least 3 to 4 inches away. If I accidentally oversprayed a spot, I merely rub it in with my finger tips and then wash them off with soap and water. That's it! Again, I've been using this product for a very long time so while my technique may sound comprehensive, it takes me approximately 2-3 minutes to part, lite spray dry shampoo, re-part, flattening the area to get targeted coverage, spray on Magic Root, and done! Sometimes, like on hard to see areas, I will lift the hair and spray up into the part. Most important for me, is to use short strokes of spray holding the can a few inches away. A thorough shaking of the can just before spraying is a must if I want an even, well-blended color, while disguising all gray strands. And while this does wash out with Shampoo, it has never gotten on my pillow case or even run onto my face or neck, even if I was sweating. I use the golden brown as I am a dark blond with light blonde highlights. My natural color is/was medium golden brown. I found that going one shade darker than my dark blonde color, enhances my contrast. Also, I have used this to correct erroneous highlights and/or to add darker highlights for a darker look. IMO, it is a great product and part of my routine hair care. I hope this review is helpful!
TITLE: I love this product, and here's why!
VARIATION: Color: Light Golden Brown Size: 2 Ounce (Pack of 2)

BODY: This cleaner honestly is a MIRACLE cleaner, I'm not even kidding! It saved me and I seriously can't say enough good things about it! I wanted something that was safe enough to spray indoors that wouldn't hurt my cats with chemicals, yet something that was going to be powerful to eliminate the spraying that some of them had randomly (after many years of living together already) decided they would start doing around my house. Being that I had more than one cat it was hard to detect which one the culprit was, and which of the exact areas of my house that they were "competing for" - all I knew is that I could smell it all of a sudden so it must be happening that one of them (or more of them) were mad at or with something - for reasons that I had tried everything to fix and figure out prior, to no avail. After a month or so - there were a few areas in my house that were clear that one or more of the cats had sprayed - I would clean the area's that were clearly used as a "marking battleground" with soap, vinegar, cleaning products - you name it, I tried it...literally nothing worked - the smell was ALWAYS still kind of there after I deep cleaned, and I swear my cats would watch me clean it and think "yeah right, that's not going to work." Sure enough, after spending hours cleaning, they would go back to that same spot the next day to continue competing. It got to the point that no matter how hard I cleaned the smell just wouldn't go away no matter what I did, and I was starting to feel uncomfortable about finally being able to have people over to my house again since the pandemic...it was beyond frustrating. I stumbled upon the Rocco & Roxie cleaner on Amazon (after spending hours over many days scrolling through options and reviews of other cleaners that claimed to do the same things that this cleaner does - none of the others that I had look at on Amazon seemed like they were going to be a good fit.) Then - thank GOODNESS, the fates led me to this cleaner - I read the reviews for Rocco and Roxie and all of the descriptions seemed to fit what I also needed it for, and seemed to have very satisfied customers after using it. I bought the small bottle and anxiously waited for it to come in the mail, (I really couldn't wait - hoping beyond hope that this worked...but not wanting to get my hopes up too high.) As soon as it was delivered to my house I seriously ripped open the box and tested it out....and folks it was seriously like someone waved a magic wand in my house - I went around the house spraying their "usual spots" that I had figured out, and it was like they were never there - the smell was gone (the few stains that were there - poof - GONE!) Thinking it was too good to be true, I waited for the spots that I used it on to dry to see if the smell was still gone and - it was that good - the smell in any part of the house that used to smell was GONE! I was so amazed that I started spraying some other spots in the house that I suspected but wasn't sure about. I went through the entire bottle in less than a day - spraying various spots in my house. Almost instantly the smell of pee and cat spray went away! All of my hours upon hours of cleaning over the last months hadn't even touched these spots from a smell standpoint and with one spray of this cleaner the smell in each part of my house - was gone - it was THAT easy! Just as amazing, if not more - none of my cats are not in any way interested in going back to those spots any longer - and I haven't had an issue with them spraying or competing for "their" spots in the house since I sprayed it the first time - this cleaner also really does completely take out the scent that only cats can smell too, which make them go back to re-mark an area that they already marked to continue to assert that the area is theirs - none of them have gone back to any of the areas since I used this cleaner! I have also used it on just about every type of material; hardwood, wooden chests, carpet, clothing, couch fabric, cabinets, outside - none of the areas that I have used this cleaner on have stained or look any different condition wise than they looked before I used it! I think that says a lot about the harmful chemicals that aren't in this spray and more to what has been scientifically put together in this spray to work against the science that creates any kind of smell in the first place. The day that I received the small bottle in the mail, after going through more than half the bottle of it it so quickly - I immediately jumped on Amazon and bought the big gallon option. In the same order, I also bought a small, cheap funnel on Amazon. Whenever I need to fill the small bottle (which isn't often at all now, because it's gotten rid of the smell and my problem of my cats even trying to re-mark,) I use the funnel and put it at the top of the small bottle and pour the contents of the gallon jug of this cleaner into the small bottle so that I have the option of the spray bottle to "arm" myself with when I need it. Whatever the makers of this amazing cleaner have created to fight the enzymes that the description explains that this cleaner fights (like others don't) really truly works - they have created something that they have truly tested and worked the Science of because - it WORKS! I really don't need to use it at all much any longer (every once in a while one of the cats will try again in a new spot not used before and as soon as I find the spot - or smell it and seek it out - I arm myself with the spray bottle and douse the spot in this miracle cleaner and that spot in the house isn't even a thought to them any longer, or a smelling nuisance to me any longer.) The cost might be a bit more than most of the cleaners out there but TRUST ME, if you're going through the same thing I did - or just need to get rid of any kind of smell (not just pet smells - though those are the worst - but this will work on ANY smell you have in your house) - Rocco & Roxie is WORTH it's weight in gold and then some - it's priceless! It does something that no other cleaner can do! It really does act tough on stains and odors and gets down deep to never make the offending smell a problem again. I have even used it on my garbage can when it started to smell a little strange - I poured some of this cleaner in the bottom of the can, added a bit of hot water - scrubbed it a bit and voila - smelled as fresh and new as if I had just bought it! The smell of the spray itself isn't offending at all either, (I actually think that it has a bit of a a floral smell,) it doesn't smell like chemicals at all and after a few hours you don't even notice that you even sprayed it - it just smells like your house again. It can be used on literally ANYTHING that has a smell and it will fight against whatever the smell is and whatever that smell has left behind to make that smell, as if it was never there to begin with after you spray this cleaner on whatever it is! The piece of mind in knowing that I have this close at hand whenever I need it for anything, is SUCH a relief - it has saved me - my house smells like my own again and I can have people over without worrying that my house smells. If there is any kind of odor in my house caused by anything I know that this spray is just a few steps away to be able to solve it! It really is THAT GOOD! And the makers of this cleaner are private sellers that aren't a big corporation - which I love even more - supporting a small business that is doing amazing things! You can tell they take pride in their product (I received an email after I placed both orders of this asking me to please let them know if I had any problems and to send any feedback if I had any because they take customer satisfaction very seriously - I don't doubt that for a second! ) Do yourself a favor and don't look further than this cleaner, spend a little more to get something that truly, truly works! You won't regret it and I can bet that you will be buying more of it like I did after using it for the first time, just so that you have it on hand! Bravo Rocco & Roxie Bravo!

BODY: My husband and my adult son go through so much ice that my refrigerator ice cube maker cannot keep up with them. We decided to buy this icemaker not only for them, but also to make extra ice when needed for parties. So far I’ve been using it every day for over a week now because my husband and son go through so much ice. I cannot say enough good things about this product, I have had icemakers in the past so I know a little bit about them. This icemaker is much quieter than my last one, it makes ice very fast. The size choices are large or small, I choose large, the ice seems to expand when it actually goes into the freezer for nice size cubes. Ignore any negative comments that say the ice cubes melt, no kidding they melt, this is not a freezer, you are supposed to take the full basket of cubes and put it into your freezer. This is not meant to keep the ice cubes cold. I can fill up my freezer ice bin in a few hours using this machine. The ice that my refrigerator makes stays in the door. The photo I added of my full ice bin in my freezer is ice from the machine only. I love the retro color to boot, it matches my bread box. Definitely worth a five star review in my opinion. UPDATE: It's now December and still going strong with 0 issues. It gets used everyday for hours at a time. GREAT purchase :-) *UPDATE* It's been one year since my review and this ice maker is still going strong, not one issue! *UPDATE #3* It's been almost 2 years now since my original review and this machine is still going strong with daily use. I didn't mention in previous reviews that you should clean out the machine from time to time otherwise you can get sludge build up which is perfectly normal. If/when this machine finally breaks down, I am definitely going with this brand again.
TITLE: Excellent ice maker!

BODY: Now that summer is approaching I wanted to do some nature walks and bird watching, so I searched for a pair of light weight, comfortable binoculars that are comfortable to handle. The 12x42 Powerful HD Binoculars by Adasion fit the bill. I only did a test run in and around my home looking around, adjusting the lens to test for comfort and clarity. What I found with my very limited casual assessment: Pros: Binoculars are packaged with lens caps and a lens cleaner, a strap, a cellphone adaptor, and a carry bag. Plus a tiny instruction booklet. The lens adjusted easily. The textured grip on the binoculars feels good to the hand. Cons: The instruction booklet was skimpy and did not do a thorough job explaining how to use the cellphone adaptor. (An online search provided some help there). Nor did the booklet explain how to attach the strap sufficiently. (Also solved via an online search.) It is important to note that at 1.2 pounds, these are not light weight, but they are within a comfortable range for portability. These are decent binoculars and it appears they will serve the intended purpose.
TITLE: Good Size and Feel

BODY: These locking pin backs are STRONG. I collect enamel pins for my camera backpack, which gets bumped and jostled a lot. Without these pin backs, the pins fell off if they were bumped the right way. But with them, the pins stay in place. The pin will break before the back comes undone—one of my pins actually did break, and the needle stayed in the backpack with the locking back. I’m not at all upset that the pin broke, because if I hadn’t had the backs on, the pin would have slid out of the backpack and been lost anyway. These pin backs are perfect for what I needed them for, but they can be a little difficult to remove. If I ever need to rearrange my pins, some of the backs stick pretty badly. I wouldn’t recommend the locking backs for something you plan on removing often, unless you have plenty of time to fight with the pin. Yes, it can be annoying in the moment that the pin back won’t come off, but ultimately it’s preferable to a weak locking back that could slide off and make me lose my pins. However, this issue seems to depend on the individual pin back—some of them are easy to remove, and some of them are more difficult. Either way, backs do their job well, and I haven’t had a single one fall off of the pin.
TITLE: Truly locking—your pins are safe!

BODY: I originally saw this film in its very short theater run in 85 and was blown away by it. Little did I know at the time all that had taken place just getting this film released and when I finally got the original Criterion 3 DVD set with the director's cut and the whole story, I was even more impressed by what Gilliam and the others who contributed to this film had accomplished. The print of the film on the DVD had some flaws, as they were apparently unable to obtain the original and/or restore it, but it is still a great acquisition for anybody who doesn't have Blu-Ray capability for whatever reason. This latest version is almost flawless as far as the actual movie, although the extras are still in the same SD, grainy, scratched state as the original 3 DVD set. This one comes on two 50 GB Blu-Ray discs and contains the same content as the 3 DL DVD set, but the 1080P movie is excellent in quality, and OF COURSE as far as content is one of the greatest movies of all time. I rank it in my top ten and I am damn critical as my top 100 currently only has 84 movies listed! I would ask anybody who has simply heard that this is just a really weird movie and not to waste time on it, to forget that and check it out. It is more timely than ever and will NEVER become dated due to the prescience shown by the creators. I agree with Gilliam that Kim may not have been the best choice for Jill, but the script and sets are so spectacular that this is a minor point and she still pulls off a very admirable performance. It is only when one looks at the enormity of the other actor's accomplishments that she seems to come up a bit short. I love To Kill a Mockingbird, but the casting of Jim (Phillip Alford) was absolutely horrible and the movie is still a great one. Kim was exponentially better in her role than Phillip in his. To sum up - GET THIS before the price goes up (I got it for less than $21 on pre-order), but get it regardless of price. If you do and regret it, pass it along to someone who might realize the sheer greatness of this masterpiece.
TITLE: Waiting for this a long time,

BODY: I typically stick to just one well-known bulb manufacturer starting with a "P" these days. But, after having a few of their budget-line bulbs fail, I decided to try a different brand. We used to buy cheap Sylvania incandescent bulbs in the past, and I don't remember ever having issues with them. Saw these LED bulbs and figured they were worth a shot. One thing I've learned is if you want leds to replace the look of incandescent bulbs, then you want to make sure they're 2700-2800K. Anything 3000K or over is going to start looking blue. And if it's 4000-5000K it's going to make your room look like a morgue. So, bottom line is, stick to 2700K if you can for normal indoor light fixtures, because that's the color you want (we didn't have to worry about this with incandescent as they were just that color!). As for longevity -- who knows. I'm just glad these are inexpensive. I have had some led bulbs I bought that are still going after a decade of heavier use, and other led bulbs that have died within 6 months. So far, so good with Sylvania led bulbs though. I'll update here if I have any issues.
TITLE: Nice Bulbs At A Great Price
VARIATION: Size: 8 Count (Pack of 1) Color: Soft White

BODY: Update: 5 mos later, everything below is still true! After exhaustively searching Amazon, Walmart, Target, etc., and considering name-brands, I settled on this kettle, with low-ish expectations. My main priorities were: a lid that was removable, not on a hinge; a volume that worked well for 1-2 people, and would hold up under daily use. I originally wanted a shorter spout, but am now very happy with the curved, but not overly-long spout for pouring over filter coffee. After daily use for 2 months, it is still working as well as the day out of the box. Things that have not been a problem: It has never failed to shut itself off upon boiling (and I click down on the tab more than once each use). The lid fits very securely - snug, but not hard to remove - and therefore never any leakage. The steam holes in lid are easy to position away from handle, and so hot fingers not a problem. Likewise, handle is sturdy and easy to hold. Interior is easy to clean. Tab to press down is plastic, yes, but not flimsy. Never had any odor, even from outset. Yes, the outside of kettle gets hot with boil.... but that's what the handle is for! Water stays hot long enough, if you don't like to re-boil. Boil time is plenty fast. And noise? Not even noticeable. IDK, my cats yowling to be fed are 10X the noise! I like the orange light that indicates it is on, and which turns off when boil completed. Only one quibble: wish the cord was about 3-4 inches longer. Overall, very happy with purchase. Hopefully will last for many months more.
TITLE: UPADATE: Still excellent after 5 months

BODY: Die Lost Albuns sind eine Sensation. Die 7 Alben zeigen die kreativen, oft experimentellen Wege, die der Künstler seinerzeit nicht ging. Darunter sind atemberaubend berührende und aufwühlende Tracks und atmosphärisch dichte Alben. Diese Schatztruhe enthält echte Springsteen-Klassiker wie Follow that dream oder Waiting on the end of the World. Da sind die rohen Meisterwerke der L.A. Garage Seesions mit dem treibenden Fiebertraum Fugitives Dream oder dem verstörenden "The Klansman" neben fragilen Schönheiten wie "Unsatisfied Heart". Düster und betörend traurig hingegen die von Drum Loops und Synthesizer getragenen Blicke in die Abgründe von Liebesbeziehungen der Streests of Philadelphia Sessions. Ein dichtes und berührendes Album, das einen besonderen Platz verdient hat. Perfect World, das nicht lost, sondern exklusiv für tracks 2 erstellt wurde, enthält das grandiose, vor Jahren live vorgestellte Another thin line und einige völlig unerwartete Juwelen: If I could only be your lover ist ein betörender Love Song mit der Elvis Stimme von Bruce. Und auf You Lifted me up verbreiten Bruce und Patti gute Laune mit hymnischem Gitarren Pop. Die internationale Musik-Presse überschlägt sich völlig zurecht mit Lob für diese Sammlung. Bruce hat in all den Jahren viel mehr experimentiert als er veröffentlicht hat. Country-Rock, Noir-Retro-Pop, Mariachi-Sound. Das alles traut er sich nun zu. Endlich. Zum Glück. Diese Box ist ein künstlerischer Triumph und eine unglaubliche Schatztruhe. Viele Jahre war tracks 2 ein Mythos, der unter Fans kursierte. Jetzt ist der Mythos Wirklichkeit und übertrifft alle Erwartungen.

BODY: It didn't take much convincing to see this in the theatre after watching the trailer. A quick 95 minutes that doesn't play around for one second. Wish that I could've seen it a second time with how limited the viewings were by where I live. But my god, Warfare is a gem of a film. One that, in my opinion, will not only be studied in the years to come for educational purposes, but also for its filmmaking and craftsmanship into how utterly realistic it is. The one thing I didn't realize until after it was finished is that no soundtrack exists whatsoever for this film. There is no luxury of trying to feel okay or have any cues as to what could happen next. You are immediately immersed into a world within itself, and you'll feel like you're alongside the soldiers who find themselves in an intense and dire situation. To be clear, Warfare is not like any other popular war film that would come out of Hollywood — this is not Saving Private Ryan nor Apocalypse Now. If you are a veteran who wants to watch this for the first time, please proceed with caution. There are moments that are very triggering and will possibly bring out flashes of PTSD, which speaks to the level of practical effects and sound design that Mendoza and Garland accomplished. It is a completely unfiltered, raw and extremely realistic story of what these people went through. It is not intended to make you feel good. Fantastic performances that don't even feel like they're acting at all. This is one of the best films of 2025 and at the very least, it should be nominated for best sound design. Super happy that we'll be able to own a 4K version of this. If you happen to have a media room with a 5.1 or 7.1 audio setup, this will literally blow you away. I can't remember the last time a film like this, nevertheless a war film, had such a visceral impact on me, especially in the theatre. It has set a very high and completely new standard for me in terms of sound design. The guns, explosives, radio, etc. you will undoubtedly feel in your body, seat and mind long after the film is finished. Warfare will definitely be a treasured film in my collection for a very long time.
TITLE: Easily one of the best war films ever made

BODY: Reminds me of simpler times. Pros: (1) Not rechargeable and uses cell batteries that should last for about a month or so, if it’s anything like the original tamagotchis; rechargeable batteries have a shelf life so when they’re done, they’re done. (2) It’s a mixture of several tamagotchi connections (Japanese and English versions) and is the most like the v3 in style and functionality, so there are a lot of things to do. (3) There’s a lot of fun games to play, items to collect, and adorable characters to raise; the screen stays on so you can see different animations of your tamagotchi hanging out all day. (4) The connection function adds more games and interactions to “connect” and grow relationships and marry. (5) I find that the tamagotchi isn’t quite as needy as an adult. (6) Materials are sturdy but I have a crochet cover on mine and I plan to put a screen protector on it. Buttons are firm and responsive. (7) The sound isn’t very loud and piercing; it seems like they lowered the tones and cut them shorter when compared to the originals. (8) The new shells are fun, but the bubbles is my favorite in this wave; based on an interview with Tara from Bandai, there are going to be more waves of shell releases every season. Cons: (1) No pause, but you could take out your battery or go to the clock “set” function to “pause” or hibernate; or you could change the time to line up the tamagotchi’s sleep schedule with your schedule. (2) The contrast is low, meaning the background is dark and the pixels don’t have enough contrast to be seen against the dark background, so you have to be under a bright light to see the screen or tilt it at the right angle under a bright light; the originals had higher contrast and the screen was not as dark. (3) Japan released their own tamagotchi connection shells that I found more attractive than the US global release shells (e.g, Melon Soda); I wish the Japanese shells were released globally. I am ecstatic that Bandai brought the connection back; I’m looking forward to seeing more shells and perhaps updates to the functionality so that we could experience the v4-v6 connection features (e.g., getting jobs = more games, raising multiple tamagotchis, themes) as well. Thank you, Bandai!
TITLE: Pure Nostalgia

BODY: You're reading reviews for this vacuum because you have two concerns: hair and weight. Consider your problems solved. First, hair. As you can see from the attached image, I have the kind of long, extremely thick hair that strangles vacuum rollers dead in nanoseconds. This vacuum's roller has a groove carved into the plastic so you can quickly and easily slide a knife or scissor blade in, severing every hair with no hassle. Instant cleanup, no destroyed roller. The suction is no joke. There are two settings: one that is as powerful as my old vacuum on max power, and one that is TURBO CHARGED. This will demolish ground-in dirt and hair even from high-pile carpet or rugs. The suction is so powerful, even carpeting and rugs that have been pounded completely flat have the fibers physically pulled upwards, so even if your hairy little monster of a pet likes to destroy carpet or rug fibers, after vacuuming they feel plush and wonderful. Con: on Turbo mode, you will smell burning hair. The motor really is not kidding around and will set itself on fire before it allows the tiniest speck of dirt to remain on your floor. Second: weight. It's remarkably lightweight and very easy to heft around even with tiny elf arms. The maneuverability of the vacuum head is excellent and can work around such tight unwieldy places as between chair legs. All the pieces pop apart and reassemble with no trouble, so storage and transportation will never be an issue. Con: almost all of the weight is in the top handle, where the motor is. This can make it a tiny bit difficult to get the vacuum head to stay on the ground properly, and makes it nigh impossible for you just to stand the vacuum up anywhere and leave it there. Being top-heavy does it no favors. General design con: the canister is somewhat small and connected directly to the motor so you can't just pop it out, empty it into a bin, and pop it back into the vacuum. It's very easy to empty, but you are still going to have to heft the motor around with it, and the motor is obviously still going to be plugged into the wall. Good news is that the motor/handle/canister all pops free of the other bits with a simple lever press, so it's not like you need to carry the entire vacuum with you when you head to the bin. Bonus pro: there are a huge number of attachments, heads, and specialty bits and bobs in with the vacuum. I don't know what half of them are used for. Some are obviously for cleaning off stairs, or ceiling fans, or getting into crevasses, but some of them, who even knows. Literally whatever kind of application you'd like this vacuum for, there's probably a specialized nozzle for it. This is the cleanest my stairs have been in four years. Final pitch: look, I get it. I have a A Thing about money where no matter how worthy a purchase it is, a triple digit price tag makes me flinch. I put off getting a new vacuum for months and watched my own mental health spiral, knowing a contributing factor was that I was living in rising masses of dirt and grime. Danger had its element, too; a pie plate exploded (don't ask) and glass shards found themselves all over my kitchen floor, and some ended up in my feet. No vacuum to clean up the shards I couldn't see and couldn't get up with a broom and dustpan. I know being surrounded by painful messes we're powerless to fix is a 2020 mood and all, but this metaphor is a little too on the nose. I caved and bought the vacuum. I cranked her up this morning. This is the best I've felt in months. I know the price tag is a little steep compared to other lightweight vacs, but it's powerful and versatile enough that it's absolutely worth the expenditure. If you're looking for a worthwhile vacuum that will demolish human hair, pet hair, ground-in dirt, and particulates from any flooring you could possibly have, all while staying under $200, this is it.
TITLE: Unstoppable Machine, Vacuum Cured My Depression
