BODY: There will never be another artist like Michael Jackson. He had everything. The sex appeal, the voice, the experience, the connections- Michael Jackson epitomized everything about a pop artist. Despite his recent history and the jokes that go hand in hand (which haven’t been funny for years), Michael Jackson proves on his albums that no matter what he does, he has something great that everyone will remember him for. Thriller stands out as one of the best albums in his career, showcasing some of his most legendary songs ever. The success of Thriller is flat out scary. The album skyrocketed to the top of the charts and stayed there for 80 weeks, one of only 3 albums to reach that level. Today, it stands as the second highest selling album in the United States and according to some sources, the highest selling album ever in the world. Seven of the nine songs on Thriller became singles, and three of them are among Jackson’s greatest hits. Thriller shows Michael Jackson branching off into a large amount of genres, including rock, R&B, funk, and just straight up 80s synth pop. Despite all the different areas of music, Jackson almost subconsciously inputs his undeniable catchiness and appeal. He took a larger songwriting and production role on this album, allowing him to really play to his strengths. He shows an obvious influence from artists like James Brown, George Clinton, and Stevie Wonder, but he also pulls in some even bigger star power. He called on Paul McCartney, immortally famous Beatles member, to do a duet with him. They “collaborated” and produced The Girl is Mine, the first single off of Thriller Jackson actually wrote all of The Girl is Mine, not allowing McCartney in any of the writing. McCartney simply adds his voice and more importantly, his name to the song. Unfortunately, even with the talents of two of the greatest pop artists of all time, The Girl is Mine fails to impress. The song is a slow, dreamy love song, allowing the vocal talent to stand out. However, the poppy hooks fail to catch the attention of the listener and the music itself pails in comparison to most of the album. Regardless, The Girl is Mine found instant success, maybe due to the star power in the song. It peaked at #2 in the pop singles chart. However, The Girl is Mine was only the beginning of the success for Thriller. Jackson then released Billie Jean, a much more uptempo and overall better song than his previous effort. Jackson also wrote Billie Jean entirely, and proves his undeniable songwriting talent on the song. It grooves a simple drum beat and a fantastic, jumpy bassline. Melodically, a synth chord progression outlines the verse, but more and more instruments add in to the climatic chorus. The chorus features extremely catchy vocal harmonies and a superb funky guitar line. That line later expands into an instrumental bridge, and the guitar stands out as a certain highlight of the song. Billie Jean shot to #1 in both the Black Singles and Pop Singles charts. Thriller contains two more extremely important tracks, the first being the title track. Known more for its dance moves and music video, Thriller is about as epic as a pop song gets. The song draws from Tower of Power horn licks and Funkadelic pop sensibility. The huge brass statement that leads the song into its main groove simply states that Thriller plans to go beyond the normal limits of a pop song. The song outlines the story of a cheesy horror film, which the video conveys perfectly. Immediately following Thriller is Beat It, which also brings in some extreme extra star power. Jackson called on Eddie Van Halen to play guitar on this song. The song draws heavily from the hair metal wave taking the rock nation by storm. The song actually peaked at #14 on the rock charts as well as #1 once again on the Pop Singles and Black Singles charts. Van Halen’s guitar playing is just as lackluster as McCartney’s appearance, but at least Van Halen isn’t as legendary as McCartney. He bursts out his typical fret-burning solo that somehow never impresses me. However, it’s a common misconception that fast guitar playing equals good guitar playing, so fans ate the song up. The riffing plays fairly stereotypical, but this isn’t on a Van Halen album, it’s on a Michael Jackson album, and Jackson lets his audience know that. His poppy vocal harmonies and lyrics make this song for what it is. Overall, Thriller is composed much like a sandwich. The bread covers the top and bottom, the less interesting part of the sandwich. On Thriller, tracks 1-3 make a good first impression but after hearing the entire album, they just do not live up to the middle of the album, or the meat of the album. The three huge singles, Thriller, Beat It, and Billie Jean all stand out even today as some of the greatest pop singles of all time. Finally, the last half of the album feels much like the beginning. Human Nature and The Lady in My Life are both good tracks, featuring some great bass and Jackson’s more sensitive side, but still never live up to the catchiness and fun of the middle of the album. However, as the entire album is listenable all the way through and each track has at least its moments, Thriller stands as one of Jackson’s greatest efforts, giving him an eternal legacy in pop music.
TITLE: There will never be another album like Thriller

BODY: As someone who is primarily a keyboardist but also plays guitar, I often find the standard guitar tuning to be limiting and arbitrary, and I'm always looking for ways to extend the range. I bought a baritone guitar and really enjoyed using the lower tunings, yet some of my tunings , such as C# standard don't really work very well on a 27" scale baritone or a 25.5" scale guitar. There was either too much tension on the shorter scale guitar or too little on the longer scale guitar. Intonation issues all around. I had just about given up hope on setting up a guitar with this tuning and just using a capo on a baritone guitar tuned to B standard, but I thought I would give this set a try, and wow, it's perfect. Even tension across all the strings and none of the sitar like buzz that regular medium gauge strings gave me. I use my guitar for alternative rock, dream pop, and post-hardcore, and I love the way these strings feel. If you're not so picky about your strings, maybe you'll get by with one of the easier to find sets, but it's awesome that D'Addario offers these at such a low price. I've been buying their strings for years, yet I'm still impressed at the range of strings they offer and just how well their strings balance. I can't say enough good things about them. So happy to find a set that works for my guitar.
TITLE: Perfect for a slightly higher tuning on a baritone guitar

BODY: Last year's winter blackout in Houston made me feel the need to do some preparation, at least to maintain emergency lighting and smartphones and necessary heating in the event of successive power outages. The gasoline generator was not my choice, I thought it was too noisy. After searching online for a while, I decided to buy a high-capacity power station, and just happened to see that the GRECELL T-500 on Amazon had a $100 discount, and many reviews were very positive, so I didn't hesitate to place an order. When it arrived, it was nearly half charged, and it took about three hours to fully charge. Then I used it to charge my two mobile phones, and the two mobile phones had about 30~40% power left, and after they were fully charged, the total power of the power station still showed 100%. Then I used it to power the small heating pad on my chair, using 1st gear, and after 6 hours of continuous use, the remaining power was 76%. I continued to use it to power the electric blanket on my bed when I slept at night, using 3 gears until I woke up in the morning and showed that there was 43% of the battery left. My wife and I went on vacation for more than ten days at Christmas, and it accompanied us every day, which made us feel at ease, and I thought that if we went out camping in the future, the lighting 、smartphones and sleep warmth should be well guaranteed. Hopefully, its quality is stable and it can be used long enough. I would also like to recommend it to my friends.
TITLE: It makes me feel at ease

BODY: I bought the Jackery 300 after struggling over whether to buy the 300 or the 500/550. I was able to get through three 8 hr nights running my Z2 travel CPAP on the Jackery Explorer 300 (From a full charge). The Z2 auto specs say that the Z2 consumes 20 W. Based on what I got out of the Jackery 300, the Z2 must be the drawing closer to 13 watts. On the web, I found that standard CPAP machines used 2 to 3 times as much power as my little travel machine. Therefore, if you use a humidifier CPAP and you are drawing 60 W, you would only be able to run that CPAP for five hours on the Jackery 300. You would need the Jackery 500 to allow you to sleep one full night at the 60 watt PowerDraw. I bought the Z2 for a travel CPAP and now I use it full-time. I use nasal pillows and the CPAP auto that I had didn’t shut off when I got up in the night. The Z2 shuts off when I take it off. So now I have one CPAP that I use full-time at home and that I can use for travel. Since purchasing the 300 I have now purchased a Jackery 550. I am very disappointed in the 550. The main reasons I’m disappointed are: The 550 only has one AC outlet versus the 300 has two outlets The 550 has three standard USB charge points. The maximum wattage per USB port is only 12 watts. The Jackery 300 also has a USB-C port that charges at 100 Watts. This will quickly charge a laptop. I really like that you can use a standard USB-C charger to charge the Jackery 300 through the unit’s USB-C port. Since the Jackery 500/550 do not have a USB port that excepts a charge, you have to use the brick with the two little big wires coming out all of it to charge the Jackery 550. This is very cumbersome compared to using the USB-C port to charge the unit.
TITLE: Great for CPAP - Well designed

BODY: Star date 11, 20, 2024 - pens just delivered via Amazon; our mission is to go where no man has gone before: attempt to replace the Sharpie Ultra Fine point. I'm trying out these pens as a possible replacement for the Sharpie Ultra Fine tip pens that I have long used. I love the Sharpie pens when they are new, but they quickly dry up and also have a very bad habit of leaking ink all over the place from the tips - and that gets old real fast. Since I didn't know exactly what size Micron pen would most closely duplicate the look and feel of the Sharpie Ultra Fine tip, I decided to just blow $20 and buy the full set. Sure, you can ask Google and/or read all about this on Reddit, but until the pen tip hits the paper, it's all just speculation as far as I'm concerned. Anyway, I'm presenting my informal findings thus far here for your consideration. From what I can see the Micron 02, 03 and 05 are all top contenders for this spot - with the Micro 03 perhaps edging out the competition just slightly. It's a very close call to be sure - and a lot has to do with how much pressure you apply to the tip while writing - but the 03 size seems to be pretty darn close. The "PN" point (at the top there) is an outlier and most like a small size Japanese calligraphy pen tip than the rest. All the others are much more like various sizes of the Sharpie style tip. The 12 size is like the Sharpie on steroids - it's big. When you start getting down into the 01 and below sizes, the tip is so tiny that I think I'm more in fear of hurting the tip than writing anything with it. The 003 size is like trying to write using the broken off leg of an ant dipped in ink or something.. it's crazy tiny. I mean if you need to make very tiny notes or citations, then I guess it would be handy; however, for daily writing notes, I don't think it would be the first pen I would grab (nor would the 005 or 01 sizes be either). I'm gonna roll with the 03 size and the 05 size for now and see how I like it. As long as these pens don't leak ink all over the place and/or dry out in a month, that will be a nice improvement over the Sharpie pens... time will tell. * NOTE: I will update this review once I have had a chance to use these pens for a bit. So far, they look promising.
TITLE: Nicely made Japanese pens from what I can see so far.
VARIATION: Color: Black Size: 10 Count (Pack of 1) Pattern: Ink Pen Set

BODY: I begin each morning of my work-from-home schedule by filling a commuter cup with coffee and then walking from the coffee machine to my desk. On the very first day with the new roll, I made a mistake in my assessments of the lid and painted a caramel arc over the surface. I had a reflex that caused me to pull what seemed to be half a sheet, which was just enough to keep one hand on the cup as the other rubbed. Because each hole is near enough to one another, I was able to choose between a third-sheet, half-sheet, or complete square without having to resort to fighting with serrated edges. This was the first visible advantage that surfaced instantly. The puddle was absorbed by a single thick half-sheet, which was then folded once and swept the remaining places into dryness. There was no second layer, and there was no trace of lint fibers beneath the sunlight in the afternoon. At that same time, the roll was awarded a permanent place on the holder. The apple juice incident occurred on day four, when my daughter squeezed a juice box while the straw was still inside, causing a golden fountain to be launched onto the tile. By tearing off two tiny portions and pressing them to the pool, I was able to see the liquid pulling inward rather than spreading out in a sideways direction. I attempted to lift the penny that I had put on the wet pad since I was curious about it. A significant measure of wet strength for such a lightweight sheet is the fact that the towel was able to hold the penny for a sufficient amount of time to transport it two feet to the trash can. It is important to note that the towel did not break up until I threw it away; there were no papery snowflakes left behind for me to chase after with a dustpan later. When I read the label about the perforation pattern that could be selected, it seemed like a gimmick. In actuality, it turned out to be the conservation movement's unsung hero. Cravings for breakfast? There is a single thin strip. Splatter of grease from bacon? A layering of two medium-sized rows. A watercolor accident involving a toddler? The whole square of three blocks. After seven days, a quick count revealed that my typical frequency of rolling out one roll per week had decreased to around two-thirds of a roll. Before the smaller portions were physically at my fingertips, I had no idea how often I used complete sheets "just in case." I had no idea how often I used them. I used a moist sheet as a disposable scrub to degrease the knobs of the stove around the middle of the second week. Even after the fibers were drenched with warm soapy water, they remained braided in such a way that they could twist around the grooves without being shredded. In the latter hours of that Saturday, I put another sheet of acrylic paint dots that had splattered across my workstation via more testing. The rough surface provided just the right amount of friction to lift blobs that were only halfway dry, so saving me from having to use a cloth that would have needed to be washed.
TITLE: Paper Towels

BODY: Being a senior newbie in ownership of the first tablet ever, I really like this tablet. I've had it now a month and being disabled, I have been using it faithfully every day for many many hours per day. Since I've never had a tablet before I can't really compare it to another. But, there are a couple of issues I have been having and don't know if this is normal behavior for a tablet or not. First issue that is really bothered to me is that after watching a lot of videos from streaming most of the day that suddenly the videos start getting "choppy." This means that I'll be watching a video and it hits a snag and stalls briefly and then starts up and continues to do this nonstop. It gets so irritating that I have to stop watching videos completely and turn the tablet off. No, this isn't a WiFi buffer issue. I have a similar issue with my flat screen TV because it's not a Smart TV, and it's older. With the TV, though, I'm lucky if I can watch more than a movie before the subtitles go off sync with the video and have to restart. I'm suspecting that the video stalling issue might be other than the tablet, but don't know for sure. But, I'll have watched videos most of a day before this stalling starts to happen. Since I can't be certain if the issue is with the tablet and not the streaming service, I give the benefit to the tablet. The second issue is with the video selection on the tablet. I'll have the screen with a bunch of squares of different videos to select from and when I select one on the screen, it responds by opening up something completely different that is on a different part of the screen. Again, I don't know if this is a tablet issue for sure, but it is a little annoying when I'm trying to decide on what I want to watch next and something else pops up that I didn't select. Also, when I'm watching a video, the tablet doesn't respond always when I touch it to pause it. Again, don't know if this is a tablet issue or not. BUT, I love both the video and audio quality of the tablet. I got the tablet for the purpose of watching my streaming videos and it's been a wonderful experience. One thing that really pleasantly surprised me was the audio!! Absolutely fantastic! It's like having surround sound when watching videos! I got the case to go with the tablet as well and it's really nice. It took me a bit to adjust to the case in maneuvering it in my bed when laying down, but it's satisfactory. After all, these things aren't really designed with the disabled in mind. But, it is a nice quality case cover for the tablet. I have a few cats, so the case cover protects it very nicely against their tendencies to lay atop the tablet. It's a good protection for falling off the bed when I've fallen asleep! So, for my first experience with a tablet, this is great. Makes me wonder what took me so long to buy one!
TITLE: Gorgeous video quality
VARIATION: Digital Storage Capacity: 64 GB Offer Type: With Lockscreen Ads Color: Ocean Style: Amazon Fire HD 10

BODY: I absolutely love this JISULIFE handheld mini fan! It’s surprisingly powerful for its size and runs very quietly, which makes it perfect for use in public spaces like libraries, concerts, or during travel. It fits easily into a pocket or small bag, and I think it’s a fantastic value for the price. There are a couple of things to be aware of. It can be a bit tricky to open at first—the ridge where you lift it requires using your fingernail, which can be a little tricky. When closing it, you need to gently align the fan blades and center them; otherwise, they can get stuck. That happened to me once, and I almost damaged the fan trying to fix it. So just take a moment to close it carefully. I also like that it doubles as a power bank and flashlight. Just keep in mind that the power bank feature doesn’t hold as much charge as a standard power bank (which is understandable given its small size). To turn the flashlight on, you hold down the fan button. Even with those small quirks, I still gave it 5 stars. It’s a great, versatile little fan that’s perfect for hot summer days, and I’d definitely recommend it!
TITLE: Great Little Fan — Compact, Quiet, and Super Handy!

BODY: I have to admit, I got this not expecting much from it but to hold me over as my usual go to coffee had risen in price. Love that I can order it especially during these winter months we end up snowed in, not having to risk it bc I can’t go without coffee was a huge plus. Upon receiving it I figured I’d treat myself to an extra latte to try it out. I’m in no way exaggerating when I tell you I was shocked speechless. Speechless bc I couldnt stop sipping it in disbelief of the euphoria I found with every gulp. It was so aromatic with a full body taste with ZERO bitterness and bite. It’s so hard to find a good full bodied roast without any bite these days yet it’s both strong and smooth. It reminded me somewhat of chock full o nuts brand bc it had that smooth nutty taste but not one you’d notice if you weren’t trying to grasp each note of the flavor. I usually drink Gevalia Colombian but after trying this especially bc you can’t beat it being both cheaper and better, I’ll never buy anything but. In fact I have an order on the way but got a pack of gevalia to hold me over and it was such a downgrade so I’m counting down the days for my second order to arrive, if you’re contemplating just try it. You will in no way regret it and on the plus side you’ll be saving money when coffee prices are going to continue rising. It’s the best roast I’ve tried and I’m very picky when it comes to coffee. Very simple to use you can’t go wrong. I use 2oz shot worth of water through my kcup and I do that twice so I have 4 oz from 2 kcups as the base in my latte and froth a combination of half milk and half creamer of choice in the frother up to the max line and it makes a better latte than I can buy every single morning, afternoon, and evening.

BODY: The thick wooden puzzle came shrink wrapped and in perfect condition. My 1 year old grandson loves it! It is such a great learning puzzle because it teaches through a picture of the shapes. I almost didn't buy it because of the negative reviews on Amazon and decided to call Melissa and Doug company to find out if their superior quality had changed over time. The company informed me that the pictures on the puzzle piece are not painted; it's a picture with a high quality laminate on top. I decided to take a chance, and I am so happy I did. It is good to know from other reviewers that the puzzle pieces do not hold up to a long time in the mouth. (In my opinion, puzzle pieces shouldn't be used as a chew type of toy anyway.) My grandson prefers to put the knob in his mouth and it is reassuring to know that the knob is screwed not glued, so I don't have to worry about the knob coming off. My grandson did put the actual piece in his mouth for a few seconds and it did not fall apart. Since the puzzle piece is not a solid wood piece, I imagine if left in the mouth for a long time it could warp. At this age, I'm planning to use the puzzle with supervision anyway, so there won't be a chance for my grandson to use it as a chewing type toy. I highly recommend this puzzle. I also bought a Melissa and Doug transportation puzzle that I am going to save for when he is a little older.
TITLE: Good Quality Product

BODY: I bought this fan to replace an old, noisy box fan in my bedroom. It was really easy to set up, and I had it running and the controls figured out in no time. I really like the functionality of this fan, and the multiple settings. It oscillates, has 4 speeds, timer, and the display light automatically turns off after a minute or so. You can also turn off the sound for the beeps when you're changing speeds or the settings (hallelujah). Plus, the remote is a game changer. I can turn it on/off, adjust the speed, etc. All from the comfort of my own bed! The fan speeds have a good variation and it moves the air around my room when it's standing still. I have it up on my dresser, and even elevated, it cools my whole room. I also wanted to write this review to shout out Tina at Dreo customer support. After a month or two of having the fan, it started to make a weird sound--like a bird chirping. It was happening sporadically, but it was becoming more frequent and louder. I reached out to customer service and after sending in a video and some information, it was determined I'd need a replacement fan. Tina was quick to respond to my issue, and quick to help me resolve it. She was very polite, and honestly it was super easy to make the claim. My replacement was shipped out ASAP, and they did not need me to send the fan back (although I would have if it was necessary). It's hard to find companies with solid customer support, so Dreo gets 5 stars for their awesome fan and customer forward mindset. Noise aside, the fan is wonderful and I'd buy definitely buy another Dreo fan in the future. Highly recommend!
TITLE: Solid Fan and Customer Support!

BODY: I'm not sure if I got a unicorn Colorsoft or whatever, but I've not had ANY of the negative issues other reviewers noted. That said, I DID order this two months after the release. I kept my expectations realistic for e-ink and it's limitations goin in. This Colorsoft replaced my wife's 2014 Kindle Voyage (which still works BTW, if with a bit of an aging battery), so my expectations and experience are also based on a 10 year gap in e-reader upgrades. First, I had zero, ZERO yellow ghosting or tint at the bottom of the screen. No matter how I set the brightness and warmth sliders, a pure white background shows no tinting at all. Anywhere. Insofar as color temperature, with the warmth turned all the way down, this screen is wicked white, especially with the brightness cranked. Whiter than the old Voyage. Brightness-wise, it seems right on par with the Voyage, but MUCH more uniform. The new Paperwhite gets just a bit brighter. For clarity, from what I can see, black text and whatnot look just as clear as they do on the Voyage, but that IS a 10 year old reader that uses the original e-ink Carta technology. A great 10 year old reader, but still old. For me, it wasn't a downgrade at all. It's 300 ppi and it look like it. For color images and text, there's a SLIGHT hit on resolution, but unless your face is pretty much within eight inches of the screen, it's not noticeable. Color images will NOT look as good as and LCD or OLED screen. Period. It's not going to be an e-ink iPad replacement. Colors are a bit muted, but honestly look MUCH more saturated than on other color e-ink readers I've used, including those with the same Kaleido 3 screens. It's all in the software, and Amazon has the device auto-tune things to either standard or vivid settings. I go with vivid, myself. Why not? Color resolution drops to 150 ppi, but that's plenty from a normal reading distance. Between the matte glass, muted colors, and color resolution, think really, REALLY good newsprint quality or good (but not great) magazine quality. It's good, and like I said, the black and white sharpness doesn't seem to suffer at all. And that's what I expected. The screen gets bright, REALLY bright. And it's uniformly bright. In a well-lit room indoors, it's normally set to around 10 or 12 out of 24. In an insanely well lit room, I've had to crank it to as high as about 20. Even in that insanely well lit room, 24 was too bright. And outdoors, particularly in sunlight, the brightness can be turned off. It's e-ink. Reflected light is this thing's strength. As far as other features go, this is pretty much a carbon copy of the newest Paperwhite Signature Edition. Same 32GB of storage, same Mediatek MT8113 2Ghz CPU, same 2GB of RAM, same Qi wireless charging, the same 2300 MAH battery, and the same IPX8 rating. The screen is the same size, but has color. The device also weighs about .2 ounces or 6-7 grams more than the Paperwhite. Think the weight of about 6-7 small, metal paperclips. Compared to the non-Signature Paperwhite, there's the addition of double the storage, no special offers by default, wireless charging, and auto dimming (all of which which the Paperwhite Signature also has). For color reading (magazines, comics, etc.), this trumps the Paperwhites in every regard. If all you'll EVER do is black text reading, this may not be your best option. The Paperwhite Signature Edition's e-ink Carta 1300 screen is made for that. It's slightly shaper, slightly brighter (since the front light doesn't need to reflect through an additional color layer of the screen, in BOTH directions), and refreshes slightly faster, since there's only one layer refreshing (that being said, the Colorsoft actually refreshes faster and cleaner than my Voyage ever did). And, the Paperwhite will offer about 50% more battery life, since the simpler B&W screen is doing a LOT less work. That said, I've had my wife's Colorsoft off the charger for over eight days, with her reading at LEAST a half-hour each day, and it's just now down to 80%. Or a drain of about 2.5% a day or so. No complaints. Speed-wise and interface-wise, they're identical... great and snappy. Me personally, I can see a slight difference in the screens for B&W reading, but just barely, and only up close. Being as my wife likes the book cover art and will be reading magazines and the like on this, the color upgrade was definitely worth it. If I knew for CERTAIN that she would never read or view anything in color, the Paperwhite Signature would have been the better purchase, as it offers EVERY feature of this, except the color screen, has a SLIGHT increase in clarity, and is $80 cheaper. But for color, this is it. Compared to other color e-readers I've used, this one has BY FAR the best text and image quality, refresh rate, refresh cleanliness (ghosting), color saturation, and background clarity. No contest, all around. Compared to a great tablet, this still has tons of advantages, like no refreshing of the screen (e-ink produces a static image) and SUBSTANTIALLY more battery life. AND this is a dedicated e-reader. It does nothing else. Something to be said for that. So far, I'm impressed and very happy with the purchase.
TITLE: Exactly what it should be.

BODY: Ice maker is a matte finish black color, white inside and white basket and scoop. I eat ice all the time. Instead of snacking on food or sweets I crave ice. I have had several ice makers before but done of them had a handle or auto cleaning features. I don’t use the handle because I keep the ice maker on my kitchen counter. I have used my cleaning feature 3 times already. It’s an easy process. I fill my ice make with water and a table spoon of vinegar. The cleaning feature go for 30 minutes . I then empty the water from my machine by pulling the plug under front of the machine , the water drains out. I then fill my ice maker with purified drinking water. Instruction states to use bottled water or filtered water and not to use tap water. I use purified drinking water because I don’t like the taste of spring water. Instructions recommend cleaning machine weekly to prevent plaque build up. Wipe down outside of ice maker with wet cloth, wipe the side vents . Clean ice scoop with soapy water frequently. Instructions also state to throw away first 2-3 new ice making after cleaning the machine. I discard one full bin full of ice. So far I haven’t experienced ice with any after taste or metal taste. Ice don’t melt fast in this machine. As some other I machine that I have had. I’m happy with this ice maker so far. I just hope that is don’t break down and stop making ice in one years and half time.
TITLE: Ice made quick,2 option for ice size, carrying handle

BODY: **Owlet Dream Sock Review** The Owlet Dream Sock has been a game-changer for our family’s peace of mind. The setup was simple, and the sock fits snugly yet comfortably on our baby’s foot without causing any irritation. It’s lightweight and doesn’t disturb our baby’s sleep, even during long stretches. The fabric is soft, and the adjustable design means it grows with your baby, making it a long-term investment. What sets the Dream Sock apart is the wealth of data it provides through the Owlet app. From sleep trends and heart rate to oxygen levels, the insights are detailed and easy to understand. The notifications are prompt but not intrusive, alerting us to changes without causing unnecessary anxiety. The app is user-friendly, and I love being able to track sleep progress and patterns over time. It has genuinely helped us create a better sleep routine for our baby, and the confidence it provides is priceless. Overall, the Owlet Dream Sock is an incredible tool for parents who want to stay informed about their baby’s wellness. It’s worth every penny for the peace of mind it brings. BODY: This is my second Worx trimmer. I also own the corded vacuum/blower, and a lid to put over a yard waste can that hooks up to the corded vacuum/blower allowing you to vacuum up leaves and mulch them without carrying a heavy bag on your shoulder. Honestly, I have to say I am super impressed with that item and I am impressed with this trimmer. My first Worx trimmer lasted about 8- 9 years before the battery started to lose its power. It was a great trimmer and I struggled between buying a new battery or just getting a brand new trimmer and I am glad that I bought the new trimmer. This one is just as good if not better. Much much lighter than the original. The two extra batteries are awesome. I can literally finish my entire yard with one battery. I also like the edging function, although the original Worx trimmer was much easier to use as an edger, because the wheels were in a better area, and seemed to allow for better balance while edging, but this one still is a pretty decent edger. You just have to get it in the right spot and it really makes your lawn look a heck of a lot better than if you didn’t edge. We don’t use any chemicals on our lawn whatsoever, so it isn’t the most beautiful lush lawn but when I mow and edge, it looks fantastic. The automatic feed button seems as if no extra line is being pulled out but upon inspection, it really is. No, it’s not going to be wonderful if you have a huge lot and are trying to weed whack very tall and thick weeds but if you have an average size lawn that is not covered in a ton of super thick weeds, then this is really all you need. It’s certainly not like a gas powered edger, but works fabulous and you don’t have to deal with a cord which is even better. The replacement line is very easy to install. The original one I had was a pain in the butt to install the line because sometimes the spool would unravel in your hand and just would be a pain in the butt as a lot of trimmers have this issue. I also think they still honor their old “free spools for life” program in which all you have to do is call them up and spend eight or nine bucks for shipping and they send you 12 spools (I think it is 12). I don’t really have anything bad to say about this machine. I do think that the charger for the batteries is flimsy but if you store it properly, you shouldn’t have a problem. I guess I just like their tools and they work for me. I’m glad I bought this and I’m sure when I need a new one (hopefully not too soon) I will buy the same brand.
TITLE: Love mine

BODY: OMG. What a difference these blades make. I have a 17 Y/O 42” riding mower (picture) and put these blades on it. It’s like going from a chainsaw to mowing with a Rolls. YES, it is that dramatic. I started the mower after changing the blades and immediately noticed the sound difference. I engaged the belt, mowed a few feet and thought the belt had not engaged the blades. So I disengaged, backed up and noticed that the lawn was perfectly cut. I engaged the blades again and listed carefully to the smooth sound coming from the blades. It was a very soft hum. Like a low whisper. The before and after difference is astonishing. The mulching action with these blades completely disintegrate the grass clipping. Forget bagging or re mowing to get rid of the clipping-pile rows. There are no clippings left! Seriously, these Gator style blades work crazy amazing. They fit my 42” deck perfectly, are extremely quiet, ( Hum rather that a roar) lift the grass almos like a vacuum. I will never use another blade again. A couple of caveats, they suck like you are mowing with a vacuum. A sort of “Flowbee” for the lawn. For that reason. Do not run over any objects, like a garden hose because it will destroy it. The second one is it will suck sand/dirt if you run over some. Yes, I recommend them, will buy them again, and would give them as a gift. Absolutely amazing blades! Great product, price, quality, performance, and function at an extremely low noise level. Get them! lol
TITLE: Before you buy mower blades please read!

BODY: Pros: It's strong, reliable, easy to apply, and works on almost any surface. Meh: It's like glitter - even when you don't touch it, it somehow finds its way onto you. Be prepared to have rock-hard glue "callouses" for a few days. XD Cons: Drying times may vary based on the material being glued. It -can- take a LONG time to dry, or it can dry in an instant. It's really hard to predict. Also, it dries a "dusty" clear and leaves a permanent white powder-looking spot on everything that comes within "an inch" of it while drying. (I don't know the actual distance, but expect it to happen.) Tips: You can use a sharp edge (exacto knife, razor, etc.) to remove excess glue and haloing from surfaces. This works especially well on glass. Just make sure that the blade is on its side so that you don't scratch up your surface. The halo effect also disappears when water or oil is applied, but only until it dries. Rubbing alcohol, fingernail polish remover, etc. works well for getting most of the glue off of your skin while it's still fairly fresh (freshly-hardened). It won't get rid of all of it, though. :)
TITLE: My Go-to Super Glue!

BODY: I have tried 3 styles of lavalier mics for iPhone/Android/PC/Mac. These have the rectangular, "thumb drive" style mic. These were cheapest I found, that included dead cat windscreens. There is a pretty loud pop/buzz when I turned mute on or off. That is somewhat annoying, but another one I used did the same thing. I recommend NOT having the speaker use the on-mic button and just mute on the device or edit the audio after the fact. The sound quality of these lavs is actually very good for mics this inexpensive. The mics and receiver worked perfectly. My iPhone, using the USB-C to Lightning adaptor (included), paired and I was ready to record. I had basically the same experience using Lightning adaptor on iPad, iPhone, as well as USB-C (no adaptor) on Mac and Windows. I was immediately able to record using Twisted Wave (INCREDIBLE iOS recorder/editor, BTW!), GarageBand, and Audacity. I don't have an Android, so I couldn't test that. Dead cat ("fluffy") windscreens are definitely superior to black foam. There are use cases where the dead cat is not best so it is nice to have both. Noise cancellation wasn't exceptional. My mini-fridge fan was LOUD when the mic was too close but fine farther away. I don't think exceptional noise cancellation should be expected in this price range. Dead cats: The fuzzy/hairy/fluffy, gray covers They look good and do their job (less wind noise, even from breath). The ONLY complaint I have with the dead cats is getting them BACK ON. Another reviewer recommend a pair of "dull scissors" to spread open the elastic. I used needle nose pliers instead and that worked very well. TO PUT THE DEAD CATS BACK ON: -stick the tip of the needle nose into the elastic opening at the bottom. -carefully and gently open the needle nose to make a space for the lab mic -insert mic head into the opening in the elastic -once it is in slightly, you will have to work it all the way down until it fits in the groove for the elastic about 1/4" down the mic head There you go, easy way to put the dead cats back on. Now you don't have to fear swapping the foam/dead cats for a given scenario! SUMMARY: Very decent mics. Good voice quality. Virtually no setup, plug and play. Mute button is noisy. Noise cancellation isn't amazing. Overall very good for the price. These have the "thumb drive" rectangular shape, rather than the tube shape, if that matters to you. They work as expected. ADVICE: I used a different style that was just a few dollars more. The price was originally the same, but these dropped a few dollars after I bought the others. (10 vs. 15?) The others had 2 extra foam tips (total of 4) and 2 extra mic clips. They were the tubular style lav body rather than this rectangle style. If they are the same price as these, I would recommend them because the extra foam and clips could prove useful and the sound/quality was very similar. If these are cheaper, then you can decide what is best for your needs.
TITLE: very good sound and ease of use. Dead cat and foam tips. excellent value

BODY: As a commercially licensed professional drone operator I get asked constantly how to get started in drones. My first recommendation is to get a drone like this Holystone to practice on. Learn to fly it forward and backward, side to side etc. Then turn the drone 90 degrees and fly the drone forward and backward, side to side. Then turn it 45 and do the same flight pattern. When you can fly through the house via line of sight like that, THEN you can feel confident in flying the $1K drones that take pictures. And if you crash this drone, put the spare blades on or super glue the frame and you're flying again. Worst case scenario you buy another one to replace it and you're out less than $50! Best beginner option out there!
TITLE: Excellent beginner practice drone

BODY: Whenever asked by recent high school grads what advice we want to share related to college, the first thing we say is to "get a sturdy, insulated water bottle" and we still live by that to this day. Because when we were in college for the first 2 years, it was so easy to forget to drink water. It didn't cross our minds that staying hydrated would just drastically improve most things (mood, focus, energy, etc). College was always hectic and it was also easy and convenient to waste money at a vending machine and get a quick coke drink. But ever since owning a water bottle, it has been a physical reminder to just sip water instead of getting a sugary drink. That helped us out a lot in fighting the lingering Freshman 15 weight gain too. My wife and I were among the early owners of Owala bottles when they first came out and way before they became trendy on TikTok and became known as koala nose bottles. They were selling them at our local Sam's in a 2-pack 32 oz black and grey set. It was (and still is) an awesome water bottle and lasted us for years, even after college. Super sturdy and durable, we still have our first bottles. But because of accidental drops, the paint chipped, had scratches, and a few dents on the bottom. It still works in keeping our water cold and hot or cold and we use it to carry ice when camping or having picnic dates at the park. My wife's favorite water bottle color is white: sleek and clean design, very good looking. So I ordered this 24 oz for her as a surprise. She was very pleased. It's a smaller size than the 32 oz and she can hold and grip it better since it's smaller. This is our go-to water bottle. The only brand we use and recommend to all our friends and families. Even ordered two more for my mom and dad. I'd put ice with water in it the night before and it's still ice cold the next morning until the afternoon, and the ice still hasn't completely melted then. It's very well-insulated and very functional. When my wife was recovering from covid, I made her green tea and poured it into her Owala bottle. We didn't know it was going to retain the heat so well, even after 3 hours and would've burned her if she didn't test it first. It felt like boiling water when she dipped her fingers in. The exterior felt neutral, not warm or hot, so we didn't know. Overall, this is a heavy-duty water bottle and works incredibly well for insulating both hot and warm water. Even when dropped on wood, cement, tiles, or concrete, it retains its shape albeit a few small dents on the bottom. My wife and I will continue to use our Owala bottles and will buy new ones when the time calls. As long as Owala is still in business, they've earned our trust and we will continue buying from them.
TITLE: My go-to water bottle, I take it everywhere I go

BODY: The package was rather heavy but somewhat thin looking. After opening I realized all pieces were perfectly aligned with each other to create a protective enclosure to avoid damage - very clever! While I have to agree with my fellow reviewers, the instructions were rather confusing, but a quick inspection of the pieces was self-explanatory and I set it up in no time. By itself the pieces seemed fragile, the plastic somewhat thin, but once put together the patio box seemed just right. All pieces interlocked seamlessly and I don’t predict any leaks unless the box will remain sitting in water for some time. Again, agreeing with my fellow reviewers, it is definitely NOT made to sit on and the claim needs to be taken off the online description, but it makes a perfect end table! The handles are perfect for lifting but the rings to lock the box are a joke, the hinges rather fragile. Anyone trying to steal items just has to break off the small interlocking rings, bypassing a lock. So I’ll leave my box unlocked to avoid possible destruction. My patio is rather small and looked cluttered with gardening supplies, outdoor furniture covers laying about. To my surprise everything fit with room to spare for pillows & such. Yay! To be prepared for heavy rains, snow & ice, I ordered a cover by JUNGDA (on AMAZON prime) and will raise the box on bricks in winter to further protect my things. All in all, this patio box is a perfect addition and helps to keep tools, toys, gardening implements out of sight but within easy reach at a very reasonable price. Frankly, I can see many more uses for this box, indoor: extra bedding, craft supplies, odds & ends, seasonal goods and more. As soon as I’m done with setting my patio up, I will post pictures.
TITLE: Perfect size for my patio

BODY: This cord was so nice looking I hated to do to it what I bought it for. I cut off both ends, then cut it exactly into two pieces, removed about 3 inches of the beautiful outer covering, cut the green wires flush with that, then stripped about 1 inch of the remaining 8 ends and connected 2 speakers to complete a 7.1 sound system in my den. Why? Because wires labeled "speaker wire" are too expensive and have no advantage, unless you are interested in such exotic things as "oxygen free" etc. A fun fact for those who don't know: electricity does not run "through" a wire - it runs "on" a wire; it travels on the outside surface of wire, just as you walk "on the surface" of a sidewalk, not "through" the sidewalk. The larger the wire, the more surface electricity has to move and work and stay cool.
TITLE: Great product

BODY: What can I say?? I love Spindrift… I drink it almost every day and have many varieties at my house at any given time. I was so excited to see they Formulated a Mojito version that is nonalcoholic… I drink sparkling water every day because it got me over my soda, drinking addiction… I love the fizzy water… That’s why I Apparently drank a lot of soda.. It wasn’t the sugar, It was the fizzy water. So when I switched to them many years ago, and I found Spindrift, I was hooked!!! I think I found them before they even had hardly any flavors… And now they’ve expanded it into so many different flavors it’s so fun Wonder what they’re going to do next! The flavor and smell are so wonderful with the mint and lime together… They blended it perfectly… Of course it’s sparkling water so it’s not going to taste like a full Mojito… It’s meant to be a lighter Taste. The larger multi pack of these on Amazon Are cheaper than going to my local stores to get it… I wish they got the other flavors for summer on Amazon and I would be buying them here too. The best part about Spindrift, No matter which flavor you pick, There are small amount Of Ingredients and the calories are super low as well as the sugar content… They use all natural ingredients, including fruit, Veggies, And herbs… Nothing fake and you know what you’re drinking. It’s cheaper than many of the other competitors and also a lot healthier I think. Every time I try to switch to another or try another seltzer, water or sparkling water, I can taste the fake chemical flavor… And some of the other ones are way too sweet or just have no sweet… This has a splash of flavor and natural fruit, sweetness, but you barely taste it. I will always recommend Spindrift to my friends and colleagues…
TITLE: Love spindrift, love nojito

BODY: Ever since I started getting into fragrances a couple months ago, Ive been hearing nothing but good about the Khamrah scents. And for the record, I am not a fan of Angel’s Share, It smells like if you dumped a bunch of sugar into some cheap honey and heated it up on the stove. Fortunately however, I succumbed to the hype and purchased the Qahwa flanker because it was said to be the more masculine of the three. Guys, this thing is incredible… Imagine: +25% Angel’s Share +25% Replica Coffee Break, but darker coffee +20% Replica By the Fireplace +20% Airy, sweet booziness +10% Red berries Gourmands never really appealed to me, but this thing, it’s different. So seductive and attractive, yet calming, cozy, and reassuring. I can guarantee that your partner will try to eat you. Perfect evening, winter, or fall scent. I did also want to add that Qahwa, at least to me, comes off much more like a men’s fragrance than a woman’s. The only thing feminine about it is it’s sweetness, but it is a deeper and warmer sort of sweetness, which the dark coffee perfectly offsets. Which by the way, a lot of people think the coffee note in Qahwa is subdued, I completely disagree. It is very present. Performance is strong too, so go easy on the trigger unless you to throw up and die… If you like the smell of coffee and a warm vanilla/berry sweetness with a little bit of booziness in the background sounds appealing, this is the safest blind-buy on the planet. 1000% recommend 👌
TITLE: What a blind-buy…
VARIATION: Size: 3.4 Fl Oz (Pack of 1) Scent: Vanilla

BODY: Hours and hours and hours of endless laughter have been poured into this game between my friends and I. We can play this game non-stop for an entire night! If you are familiar with Cyanide and Happiness then you should know exactly what to expect from this game! We also like to mix it up with Rando (like Cards Against Humanity). This game is very simple and similar to CAH. Instead of there being black cards and white cards, every card has a comic strip panel (just one panel) and the idea is to make a 3 panel comic strip. The judge flips over a card from the top of the deck then chooses a card from his or her hand to play before or after the card drawn (then picks up a card from the top of the deck to replace the played card). Then each played picks a card from their hand that they think the judge will find to be the best punch line, the third panel in the comic strip). They place this card face down, without showing the judge and draws a card to replace the played card. Once everyone plays a card the judge shuffles the cards they were given (so they don't know who put down what) and then looks at the potential punch lines. The judge picks the winner and the winner gets a point. We enjoy playing this typically with a mix of the drinking rules, but it is fun to play sober as well!
TITLE: Laughter

BODY: This is one of my favorite albums of the past couple years and is a must-have on vinyl. Tame Impala, the Australian psychedelic lo-fi band, has been making super crunchy music for a long time. Currents went in a completely different direction for them though, opting out of their lo-fi guitar sound and going mostly synth-oriented. This might sound like too abrasive of a change to stay within the same sound / genre that they are known for, but it actually fits into their catalog perfectly. Think of it as the inverse of when Daft Punk went instrumental instead of electronic. The same brilliant music just in a new and different format for them. As with any vinyl, this sounds better than any type of digital format. I feel like a band like Tame Impala you really notice the difference though because they are so crunchy. This is a must-have for any vinyl enthusiast who keeps their collection up to date with current music.

BODY: So far I’m absolutely loving this baby sit-up chair. I did a TON of research, trying to find something that would allow my very inquisitive 4 month old to look around all he wants without having to be carried or sat up on my lap. At 16 pounds it get tiring supporting a wobbly baby on my lap for any length of time and he hates being in the baby swing because it doesn’t hold him up enough. He’s still not old enough to sit up on his own and with the Bumbo I’d seen my nephews throw themselves backwards when they were upset or overly active and I’d definitely experienced the Bumbo nearly tipping backwards. I’m not interested in any concussion risks (it’s surprisingly easy for kids to concuss themselves on tile floors). So this was perfect! The Bumbo rings in around $39+tax and this particular style at $37+tax so I was happy to spend a couple less dollars and get a product I felt was safer. This isn’t tipping to the side or backwards or forwards. It’s sturdy and safe. I love placing my four month old in it on the play mat with my older and I don’t have to worry about my 2 year old tipping the baby over when she gets close and even if she leans on it because it’s sturdy. He’s already less fussy and happy to participate in play time with his sibling. He can spend about 20-30 minutes at a time before he cries for a break and I’m sure that amount of time will increase. The only problem with this sit-up chair is that it can be a little tricky to get baby in and out because there are no clips like in a car seat that would make it easier to get baby’s body in and then clip the part that holds them up. A perfect model of this would have a clippy-thing at the middle of the sitting parts that can be released when baby goes in or out. This isn’t inconvenient enough to remove a star based on all the other benefits. Love this product - frees up my arms and lets my baby sit up as long as he wants!
TITLE: Baby Loves Sitting-Up and Seeing Life!

I had a Black Alexa thinamajig sitting on my mantle for ages. It has a flat bottom, as you know, so it just sits there, passively, waiting to do your bidding. Unless you have cats. I have cats. Two of them. I've had the same two cats and the same Alexa ball for years. They never bothered each other. Never knew each other were there. Then one day I came home and asked Alexa a question but she was gone. POOP! No Alexa. Her spot on the mantle was vacated. Alexa was no more. Where could Alexa have gone? My furry roommates were not forthcoming and nothing appeared under the furniture so I bought White Alexa. Fast forward six months. I stumble from my bedroom and literally trip over Black Alexa, reappeared after being AWOL all this time. The cats had been using Black Alexa as a ball and hiding her in dark corners I couldnt see. Black Alexa now lives in my bedroom where she can't be moved and White Alexa is much easier to find when the girls want to play. Amazon created White Alexa for people with cats. If you have cats, just go ahead and get White Alexa. You're going to end up doing it anyway.
TITLE: ALEXA EXCELENTE

Not too strong on my delicate nose (which I worried about, because I get migraines) and I plan to really test it out when I get out of the shower. BODY: I’m sitting in a very long line at the border and thought it would be the perfect time to share with you the most amazing tool I have found! This might sound melodramatic, but I am one hundred percent serious that this item has been life-changing for me. I could give you tons of testimonials, but I’ll just share today. I was driving several hours to the border. I live in Mexico and my doctors are in San Diego. I stopped at a gas station to use the bathroom and stretch. Today, of course, the bathroom was locked. Since I am 20 weeks pregnant and had completely crushed my water bottle, waiting to pee was not an option. But I was prepared! I have used this children’s portable potty, honestly, at least 50 times—likely more. For three years, our family traveled the country as full-time RVers. During COVID, I cannot tell you how many bathrooms were closed—hundreds all across the country—but not for me because I had my portable children’s potty. I bought this probably five years ago while potty training one of my children so that we would have a backup in the car. I don’t even remember the first time I used it; I’m sure I felt uncomfortable, but I don’t anymore. One of my friends was having a blast making fun of me until we went to the park together and the bathroom there was locked. She could not wait and begged me to let her use the potty in my car, and I did. After that, she bought one for herself. Even if you think this is completely ridiculous and you could never use a little potty in your vehicle, get one. The day will come, and you will be so thankful that you have it. As I sit in an extremely long line at the border right now, I am so thankful that I was still able to go to the bathroom and can sit here without being in pain. Best purchase ever!!!! Disclaimer: I am not responsible if you miss! It works best in minivans and large vehicles. I use mine on the floor of my passenger van, but we have used it in the seat to give some children privacy. Oh, and I’m 145 lbs currently and it still holds me—although I’m sure there’s a weight limit, so don’t get too comfortable. 😂
TITLE: Best purchase ever! Life-changing!

BODY: I never thought I would be writing a review on shaving cream, however this product deserves praise. Let me start by admitting that I have not tried very many different shaving creams in my lifetime. Although I do have fairly sensitive skin, more so when I was in my teens and twenties, I just never really put much thought into the type of cream I used. I always believed that the type of razor mattered most, and thus I always gravitated toward the latest and greatest Gillette had to offer, finally settling on their Fusion 5 blade razor. As for shaving creams, I have always purchased the gels from Gillette or Edge. I have used foams from time to time but never really cared for them and only really used them if I had to purchase a travel size of if they were provided at a hotel. I began researching different shave creams/gels when I decided to purchase bulk toiletries on Amazon and a simple search for shave creams returned more options than I had time to consider. I quickly narrowed my search down to some shave soaps and this product based on reviews alone. All of them had excellent reviews and probably all would have been worthy of purchase, however I did not have a badger hair brush so I decided to give Cremo a try. The price point on Cremo was much more attractive than the soaps and only a few dollars more than the gels I had been using. The Cremo arrived a week ago and in that time I have shaved three times. I followed the instructions to a tee, choosing the hot water with a cloth option since I do not like to shave in the shower. The Cremo Cream lathered nicely on my skin, almost invisible but not so much that I couldn't tell it was on. It lubricates my skin exceptionally well and permits my razor to glide smoothly allowing an extra close shave. I apply Cremo twice per shave, the first time so that I can shave with the grain, then I apply it once more and shave against the grain. Rinsing off the cream was very easy and fast, and my skin felt very smooth to the touch and refreshing. It also has a very nice smell which I have come to appreciate. The verdict for me is that the is a very satisfying shave cream and I definitely will not be going back to the gels that I used in the past. One more thing I really appreciate about this is the tube it comes in. The gels I used in the past came in pressurized containers that always ended up messy and sometimes even rust would form on the canister no matter how hard I tried to keep them clean and dry. The squeeze tube that Cremo comes in is very easy to keep clean and it does not ooze out after use. For those who are interested, I used this cream with a Gillette Fusion 5 blade the first two shaves and a Edwin Jagger DE89 Lbl for the last shave. While searching for shave creams I stumbled across double edge razors and decided to try one out. You can see my review for the double edge under the Edwin Jagger DE89 Lbl. I have only shaved one time with the Edwin Jagger and I already know that I am sticking with it. The Edwin Jagger combined with the Cremo Cream was the best shave I have ever experienced. I now know that creams and razors matter and only wish that I had considered these great products years earlier.
TITLE: Very impressed and extremely satisfied!

BODY: Prior to these, I'd never purchased a pair of nice headphones. All the ones I own have been those very cheap (but not terrible) Panasonic ones. So normally, I do a couple months research before any major purchase, and I was doing that with these coming up as being amazing over and over. One day, these were on a lightning sale for about 50% off and I figured I'd just take the plunge. I don't know much about audio, so I figured it wasn't going to totally kill me to grab these and if I didn't notice the difference, I'd know. They came with both rubber and foam tips, as you can see in the pictures. They also came with a splitter (see below) and a clip, but the clip is kinda bad. (A lot of people mentioned it was bad, which made me feel a little better for thinking that. It *feels* nice, but it's not very functional as a clip, or shaped well. It's mostly just a nifty piece of metal.) The headphones feel really sturdy, (according to the website, they are wrapped in some sort of kevlar) which is the best thing for someone like me who is constantly afraid of splitting / breaking / etc. The cord also seems to be easier to untangle than a normal cord, somehow. The case is leather with red lining, which is interesting and perfectly matches my gaming laptop, and the splitter looking thing, I have learned, is for airplanes. I hadn't known how pleasant sound could be until this most recent gaming laptop purchase + Nier Automata. It really opened my ears (heh) so I figured I should maybe get some headphones that cost more than $8. These do sound, frankly, *amazing*. I'm really happy I have them, I feel like there's a much larger variety of sounds from these headphones when compared to my previous pairs. Even with Bluetooth 3.0 (most of the time I have them connected to a bluetooth piece, but that company hasn't released a bluetooth 4.0-2 or 5.0 device with the same capabilities yet) it's still a great audio experience. Overall, I highly recommend these, especially if they go on sale again. I already have a friend looking into buying them after my glowing post on Facebook. Even if you don't know a lot about audio / music, these will still sound amazing.
TITLE: I'd never purchased a pair of nice headphones. All the ones I own have been ...

BODY: I was a bit embarrassed to be excited about ordering a portable charger; however, early COVID scare, a two-day power outage, and a long-overdue weekend Harley trip converged to make me: (1) order both the Jackery 500 model and the larger 1000 unit, and (2) to rethink mobile power in my home entirely. These units are game changers. I haven't touched either for over three weeks and they maintain 100% charge. I keep the smaller unit in my Harley saddle bag with a trickle charger and small tire inflator. Yes: I still have vehicles without their own wi-fi, built in power stations, etc. - and I won't be upgrading. I will need this type of power capability forever. QUALITY: In this review, I will answer for both units (500 and the 1000): I gave all fives for overall, portability, stretch, and weight. These are not "feather light"- they are light for WHAT THEY ARE. They are durable, sturdy for providing hours to days of predictable power for computer, phones, fans, etc. If stretch refers to power length, I have not been a "super power user" (e.g., running refrigerators, etc.) but I use these for "critical communications" (Computer, internet, phones, etc. for several days of selective charging). Many other reviews are excellent for detailing the actual power usage, charge time, etc. NOTE: I purchased the Jackery brand in part based on their service reputation; I found that this company is extremely engaged with customers, responds to queries immediately and solves problems. I lost a charger day 2 (confused it with an old computer charger and tossed it); Jackery replaced it for me within days and followed up to make sure all was working. Stellar customer service. On a personal note, my wife likes an attractive home, yard, garage, and camping trailer: these units are BEAUTIFUL. Small touches. Well laid out panels. Simple delivery of that essential power that got us on the road again with confidence. I am buying smaller units for each of the kids and their families for our camping, outdoor events. My BEST purchase of the last year or two, hands down.
TITLE: Hands Down the Best overall purchase I made this year (and longer...)

BODY: I have been sleeping on air mattresses for about 5 years now. Yes, every night. The reason is that I cannot afford a decent new mattress and before switching to an air mattress, I had used mattresses. Most developed "sink holes" and those who sleep on an older mattress know what I mean - a spring or something inside has shifted and that spot...well, it's a dip that you have to figure out how to avoid when sleeping. But the basic reason is because I am an older lady that lives alone and when I have moved, I have moved myself so as not to bother people. I have mostly flat pack furniture so I can break it down and move it myself. Having an air mattress fits right in with that ease of moving my own stuff. The IDOO I had was a great deal, though I had last had a King Coil 22" that I really liked EXCEPT for their "pillow" area (the part at the head is inclined and that bothered me since I am an "arm sleeper" - put one arm up under my pillow, under my head and hang it off the bed). Other than that part King Coil is a great air mattress bed. Now for this Intex - I have had a couple of different Intex items and find they make quality stuff - first was a smaller, rectangular pool that wasn't too hard to set up (but I did need a friend to help with putting it up) and I currently have their inflatable loveseat that unfolds to become a bed. It's a nice extra piece of furniture/bed, but wish it had the internal pump which it doesn't. Still, it is a serviceable spare seating and sleeping piece that can be put up quickly enough. NOW, for this bed - back to my 22" height and I am SO HAPPY with that part of it. When you are older, getting UP off something is harder than scooting down off that same thing! Meaning that I have no problem getting on a bed (no matter what the height), but in the morning, when I'm groggy and not 100%, trying to push myself up off the 18" wasn't horrible, but it wasn't fun at all. With this Intex 22" queen, my feet dangle just a bit off the floor and pushing myself off and DOWN is just so much easier. I was also relieved that the pump is at the top (or could be the bottom, depending on how you orient the bed) as my outlet is at that spot on the wall behind the bed. The IDOO had the pump on the side so the cord had to be put up under the bed...I didn't like that. This Intex, like all the air mattresses with an internal pump, it inflates (and deflates) very quickly. Around 5 minutes. What's easy is taking down an air mattress - most of them come with a carry case as this one did - and if you flip the sides in, then do a fourths fold up, it will fit in the bag and isn't too terribly heavy even for me to carry. The IDOO developed a leak and instead of trying to patch it, as I have slept on it for about 2 years, I deflated it, put it in the bag it came with and threw it away. This Intex, I had it out of the box an fully inflated in less than 10 minutes. Made the bed and laid down on it - aaahhhh! So, if you are in need of a spare bed for overnight guests on occasion OR you have had it with your lumpy old "regular" mattress and having sticker shock over the memory foam, purple, pillow top and other replacements, you might want to try an air mattress. Even if you only use it long enough to save enough money for one of those $500-$1000 mattress, this $75 mattress will allow you to sleep well, night after night after night.
TITLE: From an IDOO 18\" to this Intex 22\" queen air mattress

BODY: Why did you pick this product vs others?: First things first... I use this mic with an Android phone. I do A TON of social content and needed a lav mic to use while shooting video updates of the dogs I train. With a background in radio I will tell you that I am PICKY with mics. I am impressed with this set. Love the different wind screens! Also, I noticed that while this mic is programmed to reduce overmodulation, it will still happen if you get pretty loud. If you're not going to invest in limiter/mic processing equipment, you're still pretty safe using these as long as you're not doing too much screaming. I find the mic does a solid job with fidelity and picks up just the right frequencies to give your voice a much more natural, professional sound...unlike the other mics that make you sound like you're on the phone. They go from near zero power to fully charged (using a 25w super fast charger) in about 45 minutes. That being said, I've lost mic power in the middle of recording twice. While the led will blink when low on battery, it's not something you notice while you're actively recording. I wish there was a better battery monitoring system. Other than the battery status issue, this is a rock solid mic you'd be crazy to pass up. Grab it.
TITLE: Warm recording quality and great price

BODY: I never write reviews but I had to for these. We found ourselves with some nasty critters under our refrigerator after we got back from a vacation. Called an exterminator immediately but was unable to sleep at night knowing that our kitchen had roaches so I bought these in the interim to hold us over before the pest control guy could come. Put these out at 11pm one evening and by 7am the next morning we already had some dying. It says it only works on the small ones (German, what we had) but much to my surprise (horror) it also left us with a disgustingly huge American cockroach carcass on the floor within 48 hours. So, they definitely do share with the “nest.” They do seem to die a slow painful death which is kind of hard to watch, but it’s better than the nightmare of turning your kitchen lights on at night and seeing something scurry away immediately. When our pest guy came 72 hours after placing the traps he said there were a lot of dead/dying ones underneath the fridge already so that also gave us some peace of mind. Buying these and will be leaving out every few months to ensure they don’t come back. They have an odor upon opening but not after you scatter them in their designated areas. *Our dog was very interested in them when I put them out so we had to make sure they were completely out of reach.

BODY: I recently got my hands on the Amazon Fire Max 11 tablet, and I must say it has exceeded my expectations in terms of power, versatility, and affordability. This tablet packs a punch with its impressive features, making it a solid choice for both work and entertainment purposes. First and foremost, the Fire Max 11 tablet is powered by an octa-core processor, providing a smooth and responsive user experience. Whether you're multitasking, streaming HD videos, or playing graphics-intensive games, this tablet can handle it all without breaking a sweat. The performance is truly remarkable, especially considering its refreshingly affordable price tag. With 4 GB of RAM, the Fire Max 11 ensures seamless multitasking and effortless switching between apps. I found it capable of handling multiple applications simultaneously, allowing me to be productive and efficient. Whether I was browsing the web, editing documents, or checking my emails, the tablet kept up with my demands effortlessly. The 14-hour battery life of the Fire Max 11 tablet is truly impressive. I was able to use it extensively throughout the day without worrying about running out of power. This longevity is perfect for long flights, road trips, or even a full day of work without needing to reach for the charger. In terms of storage, the Fire Max 11 tablet offers a generous 64 GB capacity, providing ample space for apps, games, documents, and media files. If you find yourself needing more space, there's also the option to expand it with a microSD card. Having this flexibility is great for storing large files or downloading your favorite movies and TV shows for offline viewing. The design of the Fire Max 11 tablet is sleek and modern, with a stylish gray finish that gives it a premium look. The 11-inch display is vibrant and sharp, making it a joy to consume content, whether you're reading ebooks, watching movies, or browsing social media. The tablet's size strikes a good balance between portability and a comfortable viewing experience. One of the standout features of the Fire Max 11 tablet is its integration with Amazon services. It seamlessly connects with Alexa, allowing you to control your smart home devices, ask questions, set reminders, and much more using just your voice. This hands-free functionality adds convenience to your daily life, making the tablet a versatile companion. Overall, the Amazon Fire Max 11 tablet delivers exceptional value for its price. With its powerful octa-core processor, ample RAM, impressive battery life, and generous storage capacity, it offers a well-rounded user experience for both work and entertainment. If you're looking for a reliable tablet that won't break the bank, the Fire Max 11 is an excellent choice. Pros: 1. Powerful octa-core processor for smooth performance. 2. Ample 4 GB RAM for seamless multitasking. 3. Impressive 14-hour battery life for all-day usage. 4. Generous 64 GB storage capacity with the option to expand. 5. Stylish design and vibrant 11-inch display. 6. Integration with Amazon services and hands-free Alexa functionality. Cons: 1. Limited access to Google Play Store (Amazon's own app store is available but with a more limited selection). 2. May not be suitable for users who heavily rely on Google services. In conclusion, the Amazon Fire Max 11 tablet is a fantastic choice for anyone seeking a powerful, versatile, and refreshingly affordable tablet. It offers an exceptional user experience, making it an excellent investment for both personal and professional use.
TITLE: Amazon Fire Max 11 Tablet: Unleashing Power, Versatility, and Affordability! Review:
VARIATION: Digital Storage Capacity: 64 GB Offer Type: With Lockscreen Ads Color: Gray Style: Amazon Fire Max 11

BODY: This wrap is a HUGE help to me right now! I have two daughters, a 4 week old and a 14 month old. I had a baby carrier with straps and buckles that I tried to use with my first daughter. She didn’t like being in it but I saved it for future kids. My second daughter loves being held and being close to me so I tried that same carrier with her and while she would go in it, I knew she was uncomfortable in it and would end up not staying in for long. I decided to give this wrap a try thinking she might like it better since it’s just all fabric with no straps or buckles and she loves it! As long as I get her in it while she’s calm and content, she will fall asleep and stay in it for a few hours giving me my arms and hands back to handle and care for my 14 month old and do whatever else I need to do that I couldn’t if I were constantly having to hold her. I learned that you can also breastfeed your baby while they are in the wrap too. While I’m no longer breastfeeding for various reasons, I did try bottle feeding her while in it and I had no issues at all! It may not go as smoothly every time, but it certainly made things a lot easier the one time I did try it not having to take her out and fully wake her up and risk her not wanting to go back in if I need to get some things done. But I would only bottle feed her in it if actually needed to and wouldn’t make it a habit because she would still need diaper changes and time to just be out of the wrap. However, I can see it being beneficial if I’m out and about with her in the wrap and need to feed her. Overall, I love that I’m able to do pretty much everything I need to do while my daughter is in this wrap. It’s comfortable to wear for up to 2-3 hours at a time, which is how long I typically keep my daughter in it before taking her out and giving her some free hours to stretch out, do floor time, and spend time with dad and breathe a bit because baby can get hot in the wrap over time. So far I’ve had no issues. The fabric is very long especially if you’re short like me (5’2) so when putting the wrap on, it does drag on the floor. I don't particularly like that but it’s not a deal breaker. Other than that, I haven’t found anything else to not love about this wrap! Highly recommend!
TITLE: Great buy!

BODY: I've had the Ring Floodlight Pro for 8 months now, so I figured that it was time to leave an honest review. First, I've seen some posts stating this is only a 1080P cam which is incorrect. You can go into the video settings and change it to 2K. Next, this can be installed under an eave, and that's exactly how I have mine mounted. Zero issues with water entering the housing, and the camera housing has enough range of motion to capture everything in front. I have the lights set to cut on at dusk and off at dawn. The lights are plenty bright, and in the event I forget to turn on my separate garage mounted lights the camera still have enough light to record in full color. The 3000K color temp is also ideal and easy to match your outside lights to it. It's worth noting that if you're using Smart Alerts then you are likely missing numerous events. I highly recommend that you turn Birds Eye off, turn off smart alerts, configure your motion zone(s), set motion sensitivity to max, and disable alerts. This way you capture all events and can review your timeline accordingly. This could also help out your neighbors since smart alerts misses activity due to the detection algorithm. Note: If you are not getting the 4-6 sec. pre-roll then change your router dns to Google's dns.
TITLE: Not all reviews are accurate - do your own research
VARIATION: Color: Black Size: 1 Camera Configuration: Floodlight Cam Wired Pro Only
