BODY: (Updated Feb 18th, 2023) As mentioned in original review (see below), my Explorer 500 died after minimal usage over the past two and a half years. I contacted Jackery support, and at first, it seemed like there was nothing that could be done. But they pointed me to a free extended warranty page (from increased from 2 to 3 years) and they replaced it! And, it seems like they have new guidance on how to maintain your unit when it is in storage. You need to check it every 3 months to see if its charge is below something like 50%. If so, you should charge it to ideally in between something like 60% to 80%. This info is a little inconsistent depending on the source (the manual says something a little different from what the customer service people said) but it basically the same info. Don't let every let your unit completely die when in long term storage! Bumping up review to 5 stars (Original Review) Like a few others who bought the Explorer 500, mine died after two and a half years. I didn't use it much, kept as a backup incase of power outages, using it probably 3 times during power outages, and after charging it, sometimes the unit register no charge, and at others, it says it's fully charged but you actually plug something in, the unit will turn off after a few minutes. Also, the screen flickers like something is really wrong with it. Contact customer service, they gave me some instructions to try to fix it, but since I'm outside my two year warranty (something like 2 years and 5 months), there said I'm not eligible for a new one. Very disappointed. in this product, especially since I didn't use it very much.
TITLE: Died after two and a half years of very light use (but Jackery replaced it!)

BODY: Bought these to replace other extender/surge protectors I bought because they were slimmer and could rotate, making them fit better in tighter spaces. These add a nice bit of protection for my electronics without having to switch out to GFCI outlets everywhere. Very easy to install - just plug in, and if the light in the middle turns on, you're good to go. One improvement I'd like to see is that you can lock the position of the rotating plug. Like others have said, if you laden it with enough weight, it will begin to rotate out of position. It's not too big of an issue other than aesthetics rather than function in my opinion, so I won't deduct a star for it. For the people saying their protectors died after X amount of time. That's honestly probably because it did it's job lol. These don't have reset switches, so once it surges beyond its capacity, it's dead. If you're looking for reusable ones, this probably isn't the one for you, and honestly not something I considered initially, but again, that's not a knock against it because that decision was on me. Overall, it's a cheap bit of protection for your valuable electronics. Ones with reset switches will probably last longer since their breakers will trip before it breaks, but this will do in a pinch.
TITLE: Works great!

BODY: We’ve tried so many solutions for flies and gnats, but Zevo is the only one that’s truly worked for us. Our home has tons of indoor plants, three dogs, a cat, and I bake regularly—basically a recipe for flying pests. Plus, our backyard holds water for weeks after it rains, so we’re constantly battling bugs. Zevo has been a total game-changer. The small plug-in design fits right into any standard outlet without getting in the way. It’s made of lightweight plastic and gives off a soft blue light (which is key—that’s what attracts the bugs). There’s no battery to worry about since it stays plugged in, and it’s totally silent and low-maintenance. The best part? No harsh chemicals. It’s pet-safe, people-safe, and doesn’t add any weird smells to our home. Within a day, we saw a huge reduction in flying pests. Highly recommend for anyone with pets, plants, or a kitchen that sees a lot of action. Zevo is worth every penny!
TITLE: A total game changer for our home

BODY: Recently my Fire HD 10 (9th generation) was acting up to the point I could no longer rely on it. This was odd as the HD 7 I got years ago still works, though its broken screen means I don’t use it. After reading the reviews I decided to go with a new HD 10 - 13th generation rather than the HD 11 which seemed expensive. Note, my Kindle Fire 10 does *not* have ads - I paid to have them deleted. The 13th Generation Fire is night and day when compared to the 9th. It is fast, works seamlessly, and is in almost every way an excellent “Amazon” product. My only caveat is the power button being next to the volume which, as another customer mentioned, is too easy to mix up and annoying. I would also put the 3.5mm stereo jack back where it was on the 9th generation: further up the side and out of the way. Otherwise, this is a fantastic improvement - really fast, no hiccups, and a really nice Kindle Fire. I just hope it lasts longer than the old 9th generation Kindle.
TITLE: Excellent HD Fire iteration!
VARIATION: Digital Storage Capacity: 64 GB Offer Type: With Lockscreen Ads Color: Black Style: Amazon Fire HD 10

BODY: I picked up a Thermos Brand Bluey cup at Target at random. Ok, so it was on an end cap and my son wanted it so I said ok. I liked it so much I bought this one here. First of all, before I was using other brands for his water bottle at school. Sippy cups, NUK brand, that one made from recycled plastic, anything. Usually plastic and typically see through. That stops NOW (for him taking to school, I'm not throwing the others out---yet). First of all, I forgot what made a Thermos great: the insulation. This kid will have his water bottle with ice full at night, and still have ice in the morning. I know when he goes to school his water will stay COLD (it isn't for hot drinks). Second of all, it is durable. Toddler durable. Toddler winging it across a parking lot as they are swinging around telling you a story about Paw-Patrol durable. Sure there may be some surface scrapes, but deep down it's ok. And so are you. This thing is so easy to clean in a dishwasher or by hand. Some sippy cups it feels like you are a EOD expert taking apart and assembling the pieces. Not this guy, pop off the lid, pop off the straw, pop off the mouth piece and clean. Leak proof? Pretty much so, unless you don't screw it on properly. If the lid/mouthpiece is open, yea it leaks, but so do most things when the lids are open. Am I worried about lingering odors? My kid loves water. Like, legit, it's his favorite. We only use this for water so there are no odors. Am I worried about his orthodontic growth due to the impact sippy cups have on his forming teeth? No, is this a new thing? I just want my kid hydrated and not chugging sugar all day. Does it hurt when you drop it on your toe? Oh, you betcha. Will I be buying more? Yes, he has already stated he wanted a space one.
TITLE: Just get it
